_purpose: Orchestrator continuity across Claude Code sessions. The HUMAN talks to ONE AI (the orchestrator); that AI dispatches scope-specific work via the Agent tool. When the human starts a fresh Claude Code session, the new orchestrator MUST read this file early in startup so it knows: what was last being worked on, what's pending, what decisions were just made, what scopes are currently claimed, what to NOT re-do.
_protocol: Update this file at every significant decision/checkpoint. Treat it like a running session journal — newest at the bottom of recent_decisions, oldest pruned. The aggregator script `node scripts/agent-status.js` complements this with per-scope status from data/coordination/sessions/*.json.
_what_vs_why: As of v0.283.x: this file records WHY decisions were made (running journal). `docs/PRIORITIES.md` records WHAT comes next (strict-ranked backlog). Both must be updated when scope changes. Don't put 'next action' here — put it in PRIORITIES.md.

recent_decisions.decision: CONSTRUCTION ARCHITECTURE designed (docs/design/construction-architecture.md). Operator wants the build system to support NON-GRID/organic shapes (alien ships), GENERATE realistic structure (studs/joists/sheathing) from a simple boundary, test REAL physics loads, and be DESTRUCTIBLE (blow a hole + consequences like decompression). Ran a 6-agent research workflow (voxel/grid, SDF/organic, procedural-framing, structural-physics, hybrid/room) -> synthesis. DECISION: a 5-LAYER PIPELINE: (0) Rooms = derived semantic view (today RoomInfo+RoomTypeRegistry+EnclosedSpace, untouched); (1) Boundary = an editable planar WALL-GRAPH (DCEL/half-edge), B-rep-flavored, NOT voxel + NOT polygon-soup - bounding box today, oriented WallSpec next, organic wall-graph + optional SDF outer-hull later; (2) Generator = deterministic boundary->node-beam framing (studs/plates/headers/joists/sheathing via floating-repeat + opening sub-grammar, from materials.ron real strengths); (3) Physics-proxy = node-beam support-propagation solver (event-driven, red/green overlay) + flood-fill island detection for destruction -> rapier debris; (4) Render = instanced members + existing HomesteadMeshes. Destruction = boolean subtract on boundary -> re-solve -> islands fall -> breach flips EnclosedSpace.sealed -> existing AtmosphereSystem decompression (zero new survival code). Grounded in existing stubs (construction/{structural,csg,routing}.rs) + atmosphere.rs + materials.ron (verified present). 6-STEP plan: 1 room placement editor (WallSpec, near-term), 2 framing generator, 3 structural solver+overlay, 4 destruction+debris+decompression, 5 DCEL non-grid, 6 SDF hull. PROTOTYPE-FIRST de-risk: (a) node-beam solver+flood-fill on a hand-built framed wall+floor; (b) framing generator on a 30-deg off-axis wall. Pure geometry/data stays feature-agnostic (relay-buildable, unit-testable).
recent_decisions.why: Operator explicitly asked to figure out wall placement + structural elements, surveying existing options, with hard requirements (organic, realistic structure, physics, destruction). The wall-graph boundary keeps room placement (bounding box) as slice 1 while NOT boxing out organic - the do-it-right-first-time the operator wants. Researched not memory-only (Ultracode).

recent_decisions.decision: CONSTRUCTION MODE v1 (v0.453.0) - data-driven per-wall homestead. CORE: WallKind enum (Auto|Solid|Door|Window|Open|Mirror) + WallSet per room in data/blueprints/homestead_layout.ron. Auto is the DEFAULT (omitted walls) = a STANDARD-size door where a passable neighbour faces it, else solid -> reproduces today's auto-door connectivity with ZERO regression AND fixes the 'doors vary per room' bug (single door_width/height from a new standard-dims block). Door reciprocity (passable = Auto/Door/Open) prevents holes-to-nowhere. New mesh families (each own mesh+material): trim (baseboard/crown/frames wood), windows (tinted glass), mirrors (emissive portal), ceilings (built always, drawn only when roof on). Toggleable roof: gui_state.show_roof default OFF (sky shows), toggle R key OR Settings>Graphics>Show roof checkbox. Files: src/ship/fibonacci.rs (types+helpers+build_meshes rewrite, 3 new unit tests), src/lib.rs (load 4 meshes + render shell + R keybind), src/gui/mod.rs (show_roof field), src/gui/pages/settings.rs (checkbox). recent_decisions.decision: MILKY WAY BAND (v0.452.0). The HYG skybox catalog is only ~120k nearby stars and never showed the galactic band. Added galactic_band_stars(7000): faint points rejection-sampled as uniform sphere dirs weighted by a Gaussian in galactic LATITUDE (cluster near the plane), in the SAME equatorial J2000 frame as the catalog (NGP RA 192.86 Dec 27.13; GC Sgr A* RA 266.4 Dec -28.94) so the band falls where it really is and brightens/warms toward the galactic centre. Deterministic xorshift seed (no rand dep), rides the existing star shader (no new pipeline). Cosmetic; pairs with the celestial pass. Compiles native+relay, theme lint ok.
recent_decisions.why: Operator explicitly asked to recreate the look of the Milky Way in the skybox; chosen as the lowest-regret lead from the 10-item menu (self-contained in the star renderer, no gameplay coupling, no regression surface, high visual payoff).

recent_decisions.decision: CELESTIAL PASS COMPLETED (v0.451.0) - the two follow-ups to v0.450. (1) Real-Sun lighting: render_celestial_onto takes a sun_dir and re-pokes camera_buffer offset 352/368 AFTER the full-uniform write (which stamps the default fake [0.3,1,0.5]); bodies lit by the true Earth-to-Sun dir (state.sun_world_pos.normalize). Found+noted: set_sun_light(2707) is clobbered by the per-pass full-uniform writes, so the SCENE pass also still uses the fake sun - left untouched to avoid an interior-lighting regression; only the celestial pass re-applies the real sun. (2) Un-clipped orbit rings: new Renderer::draw_celestial_lines_onto draws the AU-scale ellipses with the celestial far, BETWEEN bodies+interior (loads celestial depth so rings occlude behind planets; interior clears depth + draws over so walls occlude rings). Orbit-ring BUILD now !showroom-gated (filter |_| !showroom) so un-clipped rings do not reappear in the showroom void. Compiles native+relay, theme lint ok. Blind renderer change - visual needs an in-app look.
recent_decisions.why: Operator: everything looks good, proceed. These were the two follow-ups flagged after v0.450 landed. Completes the real-scale solar system in FPS (bodies + correct lighting + orbit paths).

recent_decisions.decision: CELESTIAL FAR PASS (v0.450.0) - keystone for the real-scale-universe vision. The planet (Earth GEO ~42000km), Sun, and solar bodies were clipped by the ~500m gameplay far plane so never showed in FPS. Added: Camera::celestial_uniforms() (same view+lighting, far=1e13, reverse-Z); Renderer::render_celestial_onto() (sibling of render_scene_onto: color LoadOp::Load to preserve stars, own depth clear so bodies sort, drawn between the star pass + the interior scene which clears depth again + draws over the bodies color where geometry exists -> walls occlude sky, open roof shows it); lib.rs splits planet+solar into a celestial_objects list rendered by that pass. Bodies use the existing floored angular size -> read as discs at true positions. FOLLOW-UPS: orbit rings still use the gameplay far (need a celestial line draw); bodies lit by default sun not real Sun direction; showroom teleport-onto-real-Earth/Mars can build on this. Blind renderer change - compiles + mirrors the proven star pass; visual needs an in-app look.
recent_decisions.why: Operator: proceed with as much as you can + endorsed the celestial pass as the keystone. It is the foundation for the real solar system being visible + the real-scale universe. Verified: native+relay compile, theme_token_lint passes. Visual result the operator verifies (cannot see render).

recent_decisions.decision: DECOUPLE game character from chat profile + custody model (v0.448.0). Operator: decouple game characters from the chat profile; self-custodial characters; offline/local vs cloud saves; self-custodial server accounts (bring your character, server stores minimal); forced cloud saves for MMO anti-cheat. Investigation: appearance+outfit were ALREADY decoupled (local WorldSave, never referenced by relay or profile.rs); only the name was coupled (player ECS Name hardcoded Player; showroom borrowed the chat user_name). Fixed: WorldSave += character_name (serde-default Wanderer) round-tripped to the player ECS Name in save_load (+test); showroom gains a Name field + the left selector shows the CHARACTER name not user_name. So home name / character name / chat profile name are now 3 independent things. Captured the self-custodial save/identity architecture in docs/design/character-and-save-custody.md building on WorldSave.kind (offline|server|real): identity (Dilithium key from seed) ALWAYS self-custodial; character look+name self-custodial data you present; progress custody per-kind (offline=you own; server=relay owns=forced cloud, server-authoritative for anti-cheat; real=deferred); self-custodial server accounts auth by key + store minimal.

recent_decisions.decision: FIXED star skybox clipping (v0.446.0, BUG-040). Operator: showroom black void + no stars/orbits/constellations in the roofless home; guessed showroom overrides play. Real root cause: stars.wgsl places stars+constellations at direction*5000, but StarRenderer::update_camera used the GAMEPLAY projection whose far=render_distance (default 500m) -> the whole skybox was beyond the far plane and clipped. Latent forever (roofed home hid it); the showroom + roof-off merely exposed it. Fix: update_camera builds its OWN projection perspective_rh(fov,aspect,1.0,100000) just for the depthless star pass. Stars+constellations now render in home (roof off) + showroom. STILL OPEN: planet (GEO ~42000km) + solar bodies (millions km) + orbit rings (AU) are also clipped by the 500m gameplay far -> need a dedicated celestial far pass; showroom Earth/Mars backdrop should use a LOCAL placeholder sphere near the avatar, not the real distant body.
recent_decisions.why: Operator-reported visual bug I could not see; traced statically: star distance 5000 > far 500 = clipped. Verified: native compiles, theme_token_lint passes. The celestial-far pass + construction mode are the next builds.

Built a big interconnected chunk. PERSISTENCE (rework-critical, done first+tested): new Outfit component (slots reuse equipment_slots.json); Appearance+Outfit persist in WorldSave (serde-default, old saves load) + round-trip in save_load (extract/apply) with extended test. SHOWROOM: spawn into a character-select showroom - home HIDDEN (render guards on floors/walls/placeholder during showroom_active), avatar on a ground disc, orbit camera + free cursor; right-side egui panel (src/gui/pages/showroom.rs) edits appearance live (skin/hair/eye color pickers + height slider) and the avatar REBUILDS instantly (truncate placeholder_objects to avatar_obj_start + re-place); backdrop selector cycles data-driven scenes (src/showroom.rs + data/showroom/backdrops.ron: space/Earth/Mars/Moon/forest/ocean/mountain/beach/spaceship, tints the ground disc); Enter your home writes appearance->player + saves + emerges to FPS at fps_spawn (cursor re-grabbed). Edit-buffer-then-sync pattern (panel edits gui_state; main loop applies to avatar/camera/backdrop/save).
recent_decisions.why: Operator directive: build the whole interconnected character system so the save format + data model are final and never retweaked. Verified: native+relay compile, showroom+save+room tests pass, 4 gui lints pass, doc links 0. Runtime/visual the operator tests.

recent_decisions.decision: PLAYER AVATAR foundation (v0.440.0). Operator fleshed out the character-select vision: a showroom where walls vanish and the character is previewed against swappable BACKDROPS (spaceship/Earth/Mars/Moon/space/forest/ocean/mountain/beach), then on select the camera goes FPS and the player EMERGES through a 2x2 portal on the respawner side into the wetroom. Said work in whatever order is most efficient. Every piece needs a VISIBLE AVATAR (player was a bodiless FP camera; the ECS Renderable path is not even wired for the home - everything renders via placeholder_objects). So built the avatar foundation: new Appearance ECS component (body_type/height_scale/skin_tone/hair/eye, on the player, designed to persist + later ride signed-profile gossip); place_avatar builds a blockman (leg/torso/arm boxes + head sphere + hair cap) on a reconstruction podium in the respawner via the existing static-mesh path (cleared+re-added each load, no new render path, no rotation hacks), tinted from Appearance, placed facing the spawn point so you WAKE looking at your character on the pod. Showroom/backdrop/portal vision captured in docs/design/room-purpose-architecture.md.
recent_decisions.why: Operators character vision (showroom + backdrops + portal emergence) all depend on a visible body, which did not exist. Avatar is the shared dependency, so it is the efficient first build. A static podium blockman (vs a following/rotating body) is the simplest correct slice AND is literally what the showroom orbits. Verified: native+relay compile.

recent_decisions.decision: ROOM-PURPOSE ARCHITECTURE keystone (v0.439.0). Operator: spawn in the bathroom (char select/customize), bedroom=outfits, extrapolate the whole room-purpose scheme deep + reality-grounded, do it right the first time. Ran a 4-lens design workflow (real-function / connect-not-rebuild / data-architecture / depth-future). Key finding: data/rooms.ron ALREADY had all 12 rooms with purposes but was NEVER loaded by code; it is THE single source of truth. Shipped the keystone (no new gameplay, just the right data model): new src/ship/room_types.rs parses rooms.ron + a new data/rooms/room_actions.ron catalog (serde-default new fields actions/access so 38 entries parse); the live world JOINS room id -> registry at load (no purpose dup onto RoomConfig); gui::RoomBounds carries display_name/purpose/actions/access; HUD shows a bottom-left room card (name+purpose+action labels) when inside a room; spawn moved to respawner (the wake/char-select anchor, one-line). Two honest dependencies documented: outfits need a VISIBLE AVATAR (none today - player is a bodiless FP camera) and appearance must be PERSISTED to WorldSave (RAM-only today) - both BEFORE the bathroom/bedroom UIs. Full architecture + 7-step roadmap in docs/design/room-purpose-architecture.md.
recent_decisions.why: Operator explicitly prioritized correctness over speed here (do it right the first time, less rework). The design pass found the anti-rework move (one source of truth = rooms.ron, join by id) and the real build order (avatar+persistence before the anchor UIs). Verified: native+relay compile, room_types+machines tests pass, 4 gui lints pass, doc links 0.

recent_decisions.decision: GROUNDED MOVEMENT + RANCH DISABLED/CONSOLIDATED (v0.438.0). (1) BUG-039: first-person camera was free-fly noclip (Shift floated down+slowed, Space floated up, gravity commented out, no sprint). Fixed: Shift=sprint(1.9x), Space=jump(impulse), gravity 12 m/s2 + floor clamp at ground_y; main loop feeds CameraController::set_ground_floor(floor_y) from the room AABB the player is in each frame (home floors coplanar y=0). ThirdPerson/Orbit fly untouched. (2) Operator: ranch was a 233x233 open patch with machines at its far centre (long walk). Disabled ranch (homestead_layout.ron commented out) and consolidated: solar array(8)+wind+cistern+new drone_hangar+defense_turret onto the GARAGE hull (55x55, mothership outer hull); field crops+silo into the GARDEN. Connections are by instance id so pipes re-route automatically (mostly same-room now). New drone_hangar + defense_turret machines (turret = live 20W PowerConsumer priority 4). Turret build/maintain/operate defense minigame captured in docs/game/turret-defense-minigame.md for a later arc.
recent_decisions.why: Operator-reported movement bug + spatial-layout fix + idea capture, all in their mothership framing. Verified: native+relay compile, machines RON test passes (relocated instances + new catalog + connections resolve), doc links 0, theme_token_lint passes.

recent_decisions.decision: CONTENT STRATEGY + LIVE HOME SIM arc started (v0.437.0). Ran a 4-lens content-strategy workflow (built-but-shallow / first-playable-gap / mission-payoff / multiplayer-social). Finding: the home is a diorama, not a sim - machines.rs:16 admits stats are placeholder strings, the 4 Home-page loops are a frozen spreadsheet, and ~270 LOC of REAL sim logic (ElectricalSystem load-shedding, PlumbingSystem tank-draw, AtmosphereSystem 470 LOC, VehicleSystem, ConstructionSystem) is written but ticks for nobody (entities never spawned). Ranked roadmap: (1) LIVE HOME SIM [picked], (2) survival stakes/vitals HUD+death, (3) operable machines [E], (4) build mode, (5) enclosed-space climate+nutrient, (6) vehicles/drone fleet. Operator chose #1. Increment 1 shipped: machines.rs gains optional `power` role (Solar/Generator/Consumer), home.ron declares it (8 solar @400W, wind, pump/purifier/irrigation consumers); load_world spawns live ECS entities (PowerGenerator/PowerConsumer/SolarPanel + HomeMachine marker, despawn-old for idempotency); new SolarSystem scales solar by sun_factor(hour); registered ElectricalSystem sums + publishes PowerStatus to DataStore (Mutex pattern); main loop bridges to GuiState; HUD shows live Power gen/use/net line that swings with day/night (~1750W at 8am -> ~3350W noon -> wind baseload at night).
recent_decisions.why: Operator asked for big content chunks to flesh out the game; the survey + operator converged on making the home actually simulate (most on-theme for the to-code/self-sufficiency mission, ~80% already written). This is the seed of the grow-light-vs-power meter. Verified: native+relay compile, machines+solar tests pass, 4 gui lints pass.

recent_decisions.decision: PIPE WALL-PENETRATION SLEEVES (v0.436.0). Operator wants to keep the engineering-accurate to-code theme; asked for a wall-penetration collar (scientifically motivated - pipes never just pass through a bare wall). Added generically: routing.rs::wall_crossings() samples each routed leg against room AABBs and PipePart::Penetration is emitted at every transition between which room (if any) contains the line (leaving a room into open space OR crossing into an adjacent room both = a wall; same-room runs pierce nothing). Rendered as a steel sleeve (fat short tube along pipe axis) + a wider escutcheon ring. plan_pipe now takes room AABBs; lib.rs builds them from room_info. Unit test: 2 rooms with a gap -> 2 penetrations, same-room -> 0. Verified native+relay compile, 5 routing tests pass.
recent_decisions.why: Operator directive: every in-game system maps to a real buildable thing; exposed-services ship aesthetic makes code-correct details (sleeves at penetrations) the authentic look. Also kicked off a 4-lens content-strategy workflow (built-but-shallow / first-playable-gap / mission-payoff / multiplayer-social) to propose the next BIG content chunks per the operators ask.

recent_decisions.decision: FIX mouse sensitivity not applied on boot (v0.435.0, BUG-038). Operator: sensitivity super high on spawn until adjusted; shows 0.0. Diagnosed: config.json DID save the value (0.10948) - not a save bug. Two issues: (1) camera controller booted at CameraController::new(5.0,3.0) hardcoded and only synced from settings in the `if settings_dirty` block (fires only on slider move), so every launch used 3.0 (old default, ~12x operator value) until nudged; FOV+render-distance had the same latent boot bug. (2) shows-0.0 was a DISPLAY artifact: slider max 10.0 selects labeled_slider 1-decimal format so 0.109 -> 0.1 with no low-end precision. Fix: set settings_dirty=true right after config load so the apply block pushes loaded fov+sensitivity+fullscreen+render-distance on frame 1; retune default 3.0->0.25 (3 spots); slider range 0.01..=10 -> 0.02..=1.0 (2-decimal display + usable band); guard apply against <=0 (would freeze the look).
recent_decisions.why: Operator-reported camera bug. Lesson: a settings_dirty-gated apply block silently never runs on boot, so loaded settings dont reach the engine until the user touches each control. Verified: native+relay compile, all 4 gui lints pass.

recent_decisions.decision: REALISTIC PIPE ROUTING (v0.434.0). Operator (home is on a rotating spaceship ring): pipes were straight diagonal tubes spearing through walls/machines; wants proper plumber/electrician routing with inner/outer diameter, brackets, valves. Grounded the params in a real-world spec via a research agent (ASME A13.1 pipe color, IPC 308.5/NEC 358.30 support spacing, NEC 300.4 power-above-water). Implemented: orthogonal up-over-down routing (riser up, right-angle run along an overhead service band one axis per leg = single 90deg elbows, riser down; no diagonals; degenerate legs collapse; run height clears machines + clamps under the lower of two ceilings for cross-room). Real fittings: new Mesh::tube ROUND primitive (vs old square segment), sphere elbows at corners, fat short COLLARS at machine ports (the diameter-step that reads as hollow pipe), grey BRACKETS at 1.8m code spacing on horizontal runs, red-lever shutoff VALVE on every fluid line. Services stack in vertical lanes (power above water). Data-driven: ALL geometry in data/routing_rules.ron (run height, per-kind lane+radius, bracket spacing, fitting mults, fluid_kinds) so the operator retunes without recompiling. Fleshed out the previously-empty AutoRouter stub in src/systems/construction/routing.rs with the router + PipePart plan + 4 unit tests. Added air/waste colors.
recent_decisions.why: Operator wants the home to look like real-life plumbing/electrical (north star: in-game maps to a real buildable thing). The exposed-services ship aesthetic makes code-correct orthogonal overhead runs the AUTHENTIC look, not a compromise. Verified: native+relay compile, 4 routing tests pass, theme_token_lint clean (touched renderer/mesh.rs).

recent_decisions.decision: INDOOR GARDEN honest all-food design + dense fill (v0.433.0). Operator asked to fill the 34x34x6m garden with aeroponic towers to provide ALL food. Ran a 4-lens design workflow (density/nutrition/staple-calories/grow-light-energy). Verdict (now in docs/design/self-sufficiency.md): LIGHT not floor area is the cap. Free sunlight = ONE canopy, so 1156 m2 ~= 1000 m2 real canopy ~= a ONE-person complete diet, not three; stacking towers under sun just self-shades. Grow-lights to feed 3 indoors = 30-150+ kWh/day (2.5-12x the 12 kWh budget, EROI ~0.005) = breaks the energy loop. So built SUN-LIT: delivers ALL veg+herbs, ~100% protein, all plant vitamins, and via a small aquaponic fish loop CLOSES B12+omega-3 (the gaps no plant fills); beds+mushrooms+fish add ~half calories + ~a fifth fat; outdoor fields carry the rest. New `arrays` data feature (machines.rs all_instances() + lib.rs): a grid of identical machines is ONE RON line, expanded at load (infinite-of-X). Garden now holds 24 variety towers + 2 apothecary + 18 potato beds + 8 grain trays + 10 oilseed beds + 6 mushroom racks + 2 aquaponic tanks (N=staples, S=towers, E=dark mushrooms, support machines on west wall = short pipes). Greenhouse placeholder retired. Food+Energy loop notes rewritten with real numbers.
recent_decisions.why: Operators all-food-from-towers instinct collides with physics, and the mission demands the honest answer not a faked 100%. The tradeoff (sun = area-limited single canopy; grow-lights = energetically negative) IS the educational payoff. Verified: native+relay check clean, machines lib test passes (79 instances expand, all connections resolve), ASCII-clean RON.

recent_decisions.decision: CLOSED-LOOP HOMESTEAD wired (v0.432.0). A 4-agent design workflow computed credible balanced numbers (grounded in docs/design/self-sufficiency.md) for the energy/water/food/nutrient loops. Wired into data/machines/home.ron: machine cards show REAL per-loop flows; counts sized to CLOSE (8 solar + 8 battery for 12.2 kWh/d + 2.8d autonomy, 8000 L cistern for 33d water autonomy, 2 composters for the maturation lag, 4 field crops [grain/tuber/legume/oilseed] which are what actually close the calorie loop); recycle connections drawn (greywater->irrigation, compost->growing, fields->silo). New Home-page closure summary (machines::HomeLoop + GuiState.homestead_loops, loaded at startup): the 4 loops with closed/short + demand vs supply + the HONEST weakest-loop callout (Food: towers give variety+vitamins but only ~6% of calories, field crops carry the bulk). Also fixed the pinned-card [E] close to work from anywhere.
recent_decisions.why: Operator goal: a theoretical 100% closed-loop self-sustaining homestead anyone can load and SEE how a real self-sufficient home is designed. The honest framing (food is the weakest loop, energy needs a real 8-panel array + 8-battery bank, water needs storage not just rainfall) is the educational payoff, not a fake 100%. Verified: native+relay check clean, machines RON test passes, 4 gui lints pass.

recent_decisions.decision: WALK-UP INTERACTION v1 (v0.431.0). The machine you look at within ~5m (camera.forward() look-cone) becomes GuiState.targeted_machine; the HUD gives it an accent ring + a [E] open <name> crosshair prompt; pressing E pins its info card in a fixed top-left panel (GuiState.selected_machine) that persists until E again. Reuses the machine_labels + card (refactored into machine_card_size + draw_machine_card_body for floating + pinned). No ECS conversion, machines stay GuiState data; E still feeds input.interact so the ECS interaction system is unaffected.
recent_decisions.why: First-Playable milestone: machines become operable stations, not just readable scenery. Verified native+relay check clean, 4 gui lints pass.

recent_decisions.decision: MACHINE-LABEL FEATURE COMPLETE (v0.429.1 + v0.430.0). v0.429.1 fixed the Tab-freezes-movement bug (Tab was reaching egui in FPS mode -> egui grabbed keyboard focus via focus-traversal and swallowed WASD; fix: do not feed Tab to egui while in GuiPage::None). v0.430.0: distances retuned to Fibonacci 21/13/8; the info card now shows painted STATUS ICONS instead of kind text (new widgets/icons.rs: paint_bolt/droplet/flame/leaf + reused box/cog), colored by status (ok green / warn-low amber / off red), two-column row icon+value; Settings>Graphics gained live sliders for the 3 distances; keybinds list shows Inventory=I, Reveal(hold)=Tab. Occlusion is room-based (operator confirmed fine, no raycast wanted).
recent_decisions.why: Operator iterated the labels to completion. Each increment built + pushed origin+forge + local exe (SOP). The machine-label/LOD feature is now done: dot->name->card LOD, room occlusion, Tab x-ray peek, status icons, configurable distances.

recent_decisions.decision: WORLD-SPACE MACHINE LABELS arc (v0.428 -> v0.429). v0.428: floating labels over each machine with distance level-of-detail, dot (far) -> +name -> +info card (clean two-column stat rows colored by status). world_to_screen projection in hud.rs using the camera view_projection_matrix (verified against the reverse-Z perspective_rh: NDC stays [0,1], x/y standard). Each machine type got a display label + demo stats (kind/value/status) in data/machines/home.ron; GuiState.machine_labels populated by load_world. v0.429 (operator feedback): smaller dots (1.7px), distances retuned to dot 8 / name 5 / card 3 (GuiState fields), ROOM-BASED OCCLUSION (default shows only machines in the camera-current room; GuiState.room_bounds from the homestead rooms), and HOLD-TAB reveal peek (markers through walls across all rooms + x3 distance). Tab was the inventory key -> moved inventory to the I key. SOP followed correctly this time: pushed origin + forge + built the local exe each release.
recent_decisions.why: Operator first-playable interactivity: walk up to a machine and read its state. The operator brain-dumped a larger stealth/expedition gameplay vision (awareness as a contested resource: in-sight / owned-explored Tab-peek / expedition explore-to-reveal / last-known-only; AI symbionts sharing sight; speech + equipment noise; EMF-hunting aliens; remote cameras), captured in docs/game/expedition-stealth-and-awareness.md as FUTURE design. Verified each increment: native + relay check clean, all 4 gui lints pass.

recent_decisions.decision: HEADLINE ARC started, 3D home machines + connections (v0.427.0). Operator: populate the home with rudimentary cubes/spheres/pyramids/pipes for all machines + the connections between them. Added 3 renderer primitives to mesh.rs: box_xyz(w,h,d) (floor-sitting cuboid), pyramid(base,height) (outward-normal-flipped so culling is right without a visual check), segment(a,b,radius) (an oriented square tube built in world space, since the placeholder render path is translation-only, for pipes/connections). Data-driven layout in data/machines/home.ron + src/machines.rs (pure data, relay-safe, ungated): a catalog (machine type -> shape/size/color), instances (machine + room + offset), and connections (power/water/nutrient/fuel, each a colored tube). load_world loads it, maps each instance to its room floor via room_info, draws the primitive, then draws a colored pipe per connection between anchors. Shipped 15 key machines (garage power+water cluster, garden food cluster, study fabrication, ranch storage) + 13 connections. Falls back silently if the RON is absent.
recent_decisions.why: First-Playable groundwork, makes the homestead VISIBLE + shows how systems connect. Verified: cargo check native + relay clean, theme_token_lint passes, test parses_the_shipped_home_layout validates catalog/instance/connection cross-refs. Could not visually verify (no preview for the native 3D app); the operator builds via just play / just build-game to view, then directs the next increment (more machines to cover the rest of the blueprint, position/size tuning, or walk-up interactivity).

recent_decisions.decision: SECURITY SPRINT TAIL closed (v0.423.0->0.426.0). A 9-agent scoping workflow re-verified every prior shipped fix SOLID (SVG-XSS, vault-replay, object/announce quotas, security headers, twemoji-SRI, gossip-amplification, future-timestamp) and surfaced 3 new low findings + planned the open items. SHIPPED: (1) v0.423.0 object storage hardening: the per-author object quota capped COUNT not BYTES, so added MAX_SIGNED_OBJECT_PAYLOAD=256KiB at the put_signed_object chokepoint (covers REST submit + inbound gossip; 413 early on REST) + a 10k size cap on the previously-uncapped federation_rate map; left the already-50k-bounded object_submit_rate map as-is. (2) v0.424.0 hard-delete remnants: PRAGMA secure_delete=ON on the writer + wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) after the bulk-wipe paths; byte-scan test proves the marker is gone from relay.db + WAL; honest residual (rotating backups hold deleted PUBLIC content until they age out; DMs E2EE so never exposed; operator/root-readable only) documented in docs/reference/retention_and_deletion_semantics.md + a CLAUDE.md audit note. (3) member-directory opt-out FULLY done: v0.425.0 backend (profiles.privacy directory=unlisted honored in get_members/count/get_member via ONE shared LEFT JOIN server_members->profiles + json_extract; recent_joins left unfiltered for admins) + web checkbox; v0.426.0 native (built the first-ever native profile_update WS send + a directory checkbox + Save button in the Network Profile editor). REMAINING = operator-only, no code: /api/send nginx edge rate-limit (paired VPS zone-def + humanity.conf location block; NOT done in-repo because the zone lives off-repo and a dangling reference breaks nginx -t; LOW value since /api/send is API_SECRET-gated) and GitHub branch/tag protection + deploy approval gate. Both with exact steps in the new docs/admin/security-hardening-tasks.md.
recent_decisions.why: Operator asked to continue the security sprint and get it out of the way long-term. Verified per increment: cargo check --features relay (+ native where touched) clean; new tests pass (put_rejects_oversized_payload, secure_delete_scrubs_deleted_message_bytes, unlisted_member_hidden_everywhere_else_listed); all 4 gui lints pass for the native profile.rs change. The audit code surface is now closed; the residue is GitHub-settings + VPS-nginx operator tasks.

recent_decisions.decision: DOCS REFACTOR shipped (v0.422.1->v0.422.3, docs-only patches on main, not separately tagged). (1) v0.422.1 em-dash sweep: 2178 prose em dashes -> commas/colons across 131 files + 79 code-block ones -> hyphens (scripts/strip-em-dashes.js); stale crypto fixed in 9 live docs (Ed25519->Dilithium3 identity, ECDH/X25519->Kyber768 DM) per the CLAUDE.md table. (2) v0.422.2 ROADMAP-AS-TODO: docs/ROADMAP.md is now the single canonical roadmap (themed: survival/identity/communication/governance/simulation/accessibility/infrastructure; status tags done/building/next/planned/future + a Right-now queue), the public roadmap AND the build to-do; scripts/roadmap-to-json.js -> data/roadmap.json; web/pages/roadmap-app.js rewritten to render it (was reading /api/tasks). (3) v0.422.3 AUDIENCE-FIRST STRUCTURE: docs root 50->17; new docs/README.md router + user/admin/ai/contributor folders each with a who-this-is-for README; user/getting-started.md (who/what/when/where/why/how for non-tech readers); 36 files moved (admin runbooks, design specs, AI cluster, numbered 00-09, archives->history/); stale indexes fixed (docs/index.md, 02-ARCHITECTURE, 06-SOURCE-OF-TRUTH-MAP, knowledge_manifest 34/42-phantom->19 real); ai-onboarding.md -> ai/onboarding.md.
recent_decisions.why: Operator: docs were scattered, had deprecated info (people kept missing the Ed25519->Dilithium upgrade), em dashes read as AI-written and drive readers away, onboarding was not divided by audience, and the roadmap should BE the to-do list so updating it updates the public roadmap. Verified: scripts/check-doc-links.js took the tree from 186 pre-existing broken links to 0; 0 em dashes in active docs; roadmap JSON + knowledge_manifest regenerate and resolve. New tooling kept: check-doc-links.js, reorg-docs.js, fix-doc-links.js, roadmap-to-json.js, preview-server.js.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.413.0 — INLINE ITEM-DETAIL EXPANSION ('the big one' from the operator's inventory batch: 'click an item row to expand in place — picture/3d + full details over rows — instead of a popup/top detail'). Threaded an inline-body renderer through the tree widget: widgets::container_node + tree_list_ex now take `inline: &mut dyn FnMut(&mut Ui, &str)`, and when a selectable LEAF is the selected one they render inline(ui,id) indented directly UNDER that row (the plain tree_list passes a no-op). In src/gui/pages/inventory.rs: (a) new ItemCardActions struct + draw_item_card() helper that renders the expand-in-place item card = colored placeholder image tile (widgets::placeholder_tile + swatch_color, the universal widgets from v0.411) + category badge + details grid (ID/Qty/Category/Subcategory/Weight/Stack/Material/Durability) + description + Eat/Drink/Plant/Use + Equip + Drop compact buttons, recording the chosen action into ItemCardActions; (b) the You&places backpack tree passes an inline closure that looks the item up from an inventory SNAPSHOT (state.inventory_items.clone()) and calls draw_item_card — snapshot so the card never borrows GuiState mid-render; (c) DELETED the old top-of-panel detail block (~100 lines) + its 5 mid-panel action handlers; the item action now applies AFTER the panel closes (where item_acts is readable and the snapshot-borrowing closure is dropped), bridging into the same pending_consume_item/pending_drink_item/pending_plant_seed/equip/drop paths as before. Net: click an item -> its row expands into the card in place; click again -> collapses (selected_slot toggles). VERIFIED: cargo check --features native clean (borrow design held first try); cargo test --lib 398/0; all FOUR gui lints PASS. NEXT v0.414: adopt expandable_row/row_cell in the Mining section (asteroids/manifest/drones grids -> aligned expandable rows) + the You&places place nodes; consider the long-deferred garden PLOT-TYPES arc (soil/sand/pots/trays, infinite-of-X); web Mission-Dashboard mirror.
recent_decisions.why: This was the headline UX item the operator flagged as 'the big one' and the natural completion of the inline-expansion theme (garden slots v0.411, then sections v0.412, now items). Implemented by enhancing the EXISTING tree widget with an optional inline-body callback rather than rebuilding the backpack as a flat expandable_row list — that keeps the place hierarchy (You > home > backpack > items) intact while still expanding the right leaf in place. The snapshot-clone of inventory_items is the key borrow trick: the card sets actions into a plain struct (ItemCardActions) that lives OUTSIDE the panel, so the closure only needs the owned snapshot + &mut struct + theme and never touches GuiState during render — which is why it compiled clean on the first try despite the deeply nested closures. Relocating the 5 action handlers to after the panel (vs their old mid-panel spot) was required because the card now sets them LATER in the frame (inside the tree, not at the top); applying post-panel keeps it single-frame, no lag. Reused placeholder_tile + swatch_color so items get the same picture-placeholder treatment as garden slots — one consistent card language across the app.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.412.0 — COLLAPSIBLE INVENTORY SECTIONS (operator batch item: 'every section collapsible + more defined, with the standard Collapse/Expand/Start-collapsed on ALL nested lists', and the latest-message ask 'add the collapse/expand to the garden section as well'). New widget src/gui/widgets/mod.rs::section_disclosure(ui,theme,id,title,force)->bool: a closure-FREE collapsible section header — paints a triangle (▶ collapsed / ▼ open) as a SHAPE (no font glyph, never tofu), persists open-state per id in egui memory, and honors a global `force` (Some(false)=collapse, Some(true)=expand). Returns the open bool so the caller guards its body with `if section_disclosure(..) { ...body... }` — NO body closure, so the inventory panel's many `action_*`/with_state borrows stay exactly as-is (this is why I didn't reuse the existing closure-form `collapsible_section`). In src/gui/pages/inventory.rs: HOISTED the Collapse-all/Expand-all/Start-collapsed control bar from inside the You&places section to a GLOBAL bar at the top (so its `tree_force` is computed before any section reads it), and wrapped all five sections — Status / Equipment / You&places / Garden / Mining — each in `if widgets::section_disclosure(... tree_force) {`. The same control set now drives the sections AND the nested places-tree + garden tower-groups in one click. Refreshed the stale 'Garden = aligned TABLE' comment to the slot-row reality. VERIFIED: cargo check --features native clean; cargo test --lib 398/0 (no regression); all FOUR gui lints PASS (note: section_disclosure uses theme.text_secondary()/text_primary() — no color literals; the triangle is painted so no glyph lint; my `// ── ──` box-drawing markers are in COMMENTS which emdash_lint doesn't scan). NEXT v0.413: relocate the selected inventory-ITEM detail from the panel TOP to an inline expandable_row (click an item row -> expand in place into a card, same universal widget as the garden slots — operator's flagged 'the big one'); this needs tree_list_ex (the places/backpack tree) to support per-node inline expansion. Then adopt expandable_row/row_cell in Mining (asteroids/manifest/drones grids) + You&places.
recent_decisions.why: Closes the remaining items from the operator's inventory-styling batch in one coherent increment. Chose a CLOSURE-FREE disclosure widget (returns bool, caller guards with `if`) specifically to avoid moving ~340 lines of section body (Garden) into a body closure, which would have forced a massive re-indent AND risked the action_*/with_state mutable-borrow plumbing — the closure-free guard keeps every borrow identical and the diff to ~12 lines (the guards + closes). Painted the triangle as geometry rather than a glyph because the egui font's Geometric-Shapes block (▶▼) renders as tofu (icon_glyph_lint would reject it). Hoisting the control bar to the top fixes a latent ordering issue (it previously lived in the You&places section, AFTER Equipment, so a future Equipment use of tree_force wouldn't see it) and gives the operator one global control set, which is what 'on ALL nested lists' asked for. Left the selected-item-detail relocation for v0.413 because it's a deeper tree-widget change (inline node expansion), not a quick guard.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.411.0 — GARDEN SLOT ROWS + click-to-expand plant CARD (the visible payoff of the v0.410 slot model; operator's explicit ask: 'expanded it's rows of like slot 1,2,3 ... click on the specific plant can expand that single row to a multirow card with image or 3D model representation. This nesting style ... should be a universal widget with configurable columns'). Built two NEW reusable widgets in src/gui/widgets/mod.rs: (1) swatch_color(seed) — deterministic FNV-1a-hash -> HSV->RGB colour per arbitrary id (infinite data-seeded palette, computed args so the theme-token lint doesn't flag it, no token applies); (2) placeholder_tile(ui,theme,color,size,glyph) — the 'colored placeholder tile' the operator approved as the image/3D stand-in (rounded swatch + centered white initial). Rewrote the garden body in src/gui/pages/inventory.rs: each tower now renders SLOT ROWS (slot 1..N, flattened from the tower's plantings EXACTLY as the farming handler assigns tower_slot, so slot index == crop.tower_slot). Each slot is a nested widgets::expandable_row using widgets::row_cell for aligned columns (Slot# | plant name | status | growth bar); clicking a slot expands its BODY into the multi-row CARD = placeholder_tile (swatch of the plant id) + name heading + role (italic) + DESCRIPTION (the planting note) + a details grid (Stage/Growth/N·P·K/Water·day/Temp window/Reservoir/Health) + Harvest/Water/Fertilize compact_buttons — or, for a planned-but-empty slot, the tile+name+description + 'Not yet planted'. Live crops map to slots by tower_slot; the 'Other crops' (loose seed-planted, no tower config) group falls back to one row per crop positionally. Also: added `test = false` to the [[bin]] in Cargo.toml so `cargo test --lib` no longer links the full native bin. VERIFIED: cargo check --features native clean; cargo test --features native --lib = 398 passed / 0 failed (farming slot model + gui tower_compat + all crypto/etc green); all FOUR src/gui lints PASS (emdash/theme_token/theme_editor_coverage/icon_glyph). NEXT v0.412: make the inventory MAIN sections (Status/Equipment/You&places/Garden/Mining) collapsible CollapsingHeaders; relocate the selected-inventory-ITEM detail from the top of the panel to an inline expandable_row (same universal widget); adopt expandable_row/row_cell in Mining + You&places.
recent_decisions.why: This is the operator's headline request from the v0.407 screenshots, and the data layer (tower_slot, shipped v0.410) was built specifically to feed it. Doing it as expandable_row + row_cell + swatch_color + placeholder_tile makes the nesting a GENUINELY universal widget set (the operator explicitly wants this pattern reused across HumanityOS — market listings, the inventory tree, future sections — so the colour/tile helpers live in widgets/, not inventory.rs). The slot view (vs the old flat crop table) is what makes 'Plant this tower' legible: you see the fixed slots, which are filled, which are empty/planned, and click any one for the full plant context + actions. Hit a Windows LNK1318 PDB type-server limit linking the native test bin — diagnosed as an environment limit (release + Linux CI unaffected), fixed `cargo test --lib` via bin `test = false`, and verified the four file-scanner gui lints by compiling them standalone with `rustc --test` (they import nothing from the crate). Documented both in CLAUDE.md gotchas so the next session doesn't rediscover it.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.410.0 — TOWER SLOT MODEL + STACKING-BUG FIX (the data layer for the garden slot redesign; operator hit the bug: 'plant this tower' kept stacking, count went 33->66->99). CropInstance gains tower_slot: Option<u32> (serde default); the FarmingSystem tower handler now FILLS FIXED SLOTS instead of appending: for each slot index it (a) skips if a LIVE crop already occupies that (tower_id, slot) — making replant IDEMPOTENT — and (b) despawns any DEAD crop in the slot first so it refills. Threaded tower_slot through the seed-plant + test CropInstance constructions, GuiCrop, and the lib.rs crop sync. VERIFIED: cargo check clean; farming 9/9 incl. NEW tower_replant_fills_slots_idempotently (plant the same tower twice -> still 2 crops not 4; slots 0 and 1 recorded) + the existing creative/survival tower tests still green (slots empty on first plant). This kills the operator's stacking bug and records which slot each crop is in. NEXT v0.411 (the visible payoff): SLOT ROWS — render the tower body as slot 1..N (from the tower's plantings expanded; map each live crop to its tower_slot) using widgets::row_cell for aligned columns, each slot an expandable_row; click a slot -> body = a multi-row CARD (colored-placeholder image tile in the plant's category colour + plant DESCRIPTION [reuse the planting note] + full details [NPK/water/temp/growth_days] + Harvest/Water/Fertilize). Then other sections adopt expandable_row + row_cell.
recent_decisions.why: Split the garden redesign so the DATA + the real bug fix land first, verified, before the bigger slot/card UI. The stacking bug was a genuine defect the operator reported, and the correct fix IS the slot model (fixed N slots per tower, planting fills empties) rather than a band-aid — so building tower_slot now both fixes the bug and is the foundation the slot rows + card render from. Despawn-dead-then-refill makes 'Plant again' clear a wiped tower without the player manually removing dead crops. Kept the crop-table UI for this increment (it still works) so the schema + handler change is provable on its own; the slot-row + card view is its own focused increment.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.409.0 — UNIVERSAL expandable_row WIDGET + tower MAKE/MODEL/VERSION title (operator-confirmed via AskUserQuestion: add make/model/version now; colored-placeholder card image). Step 1 of the garden redesign. (1) NEW widgets::expandable_row(ui, id_salt, default_open, force, header_fn, body_fn) — the universal nesting primitive he asked for ('this nesting style ... universal widget with configurable columns per implementation'): any row whose body can be nested rows OR a detail card, wired to the standard Collapse/Expand/Start-collapsed (default_open + force), composable to any depth. Plus widgets::row_cell(ui, width, content) — a fixed-width clipped cell so rows align into columns WITHOUT egui::Grid (which can't host an inline expanding body). (2) TowerConfig gains make/model/version (serde default), set in the RON (nutrition = HumanityOS Helix Wide 60 v1.0; apothecary = HumanityOS Helix Slim 32 v1.0). (3) Refactored the garden tower groups from egui::CollapsingHeader to expandable_row: the title row now shows name + make/model/version + N/M-ready + the Plant button (widgets in the title row, his ask); the body is the existing crop table (slots + card come next). VERIFIED: cargo check clean; homes RON-parse test + tower_compat 2/2 pass (fixed the tower_of test literal for the 3 new fields); all FOUR gui lints green. NEXT v0.410 (the meaty redesign on this widget): SLOT model — give CropInstance a tower_slot so 'Plant this tower' FILLS fixed slots (idempotent — kills the 33->66->99 stacking bug the operator hit) -> render SLOT rows (slot 1..N: plant + simple stats via row_cell) -> click a slot -> expandable_row body = a multi-row CARD (colored-placeholder image tile + plant DESCRIPTION [reuse the planting note] + full details + actions). Then other sections (Mining, You&places) can adopt expandable_row + row_cell.
recent_decisions.why: Built the universal primitive FIRST (his explicit 'should be a universal widget') so the garden — and later Mining / the inventory tree — all compose the same expand-a-row building block instead of bespoke CollapsingHeaders. Chose a header_fn/body_fn closure API (not a data struct) because egui is immediate-mode and the cells/cards need arbitrary rich rendering (colors, bars, buttons, images); row_cell gives the configurable-column alignment without a Grid (Grids can't host an inline expanding card). Added make/model/version as DATA so the community can brand designs. Kept the crop table in the body this increment so the widget + the title row land verified before the bigger slot-model + card work (which needs a CropInstance schema change, so it is its own increment).

recent_decisions.decision: v0.408.0 — ROW STRIPE -> #040404 + ANIMATED RGB SECTION DIVIDERS (operator on v0.407 screenshot: happy, 'make the stripe #040404 instead of #020202; add a 1px slow rgb divider between each main section'). (1) row_stripe token #020202 -> #040404 (0.0157) — theme.rs default fn/reset/Default + theme.ron + theme.css; slightly more visible stripe. (2) new widgets::rgb_section_divider(ui, theme) — a 1px full-width line coloured by row::rgb_from_time(time*0.3) (the same hue helper the nav separators use; *0.3 -> ~40s 'slow' cycle), animated via ctx.request_repaint(). Placed between the inventory's main sections: Status|Equipment, Equipment|You&places, You&places|Garden, Garden|Mining (replaced the plain ui.separator()s). VERIFIED: cargo check clean; all FOUR gui lints green. NOTE: the page now continuously repaints for the divider animation (same accepted pattern as the nav RGB separators). NEXT — the BIG one the operator detailed: a UNIVERSAL NESTED-EXPANDABLE WIDGET (he wants this 'nesting style ... in many sections throughout HumanityOS ... universal widget with configurable columns per implementation'). GARDEN redesign on top of it: tower title row = 'Aeroponic Tower make/model/version' (+ widgets in the row) -> expand to SLOT rows (slot 1..N, the plant + simple stats) -> click a slot -> expand THAT row to a multi-row CARD with an image/3D-model placeholder + a DESCRIPTION + details. Also FIX the 'Plant this tower' STACKING bug (replant kept adding 33 more crops -> 66 -> 99; the slot model makes planting fill fixed slots, idempotent). And the GARDEN section gets collapse/expand (he asked again) — falls out of the universal widget. This is a multi-increment build: v0.409 = the universal widget; v0.410 = garden-on-the-widget incl. slots + the stacking fix + the card expansion.
recent_decisions.why: Shipped the two quick, low-risk styling items from his latest message first (a token value + a small reusable divider widget) for an immediate visible win, while the big garden/universal-nested-widget redesign — which he wants to be a reusable cross-app widget, so it deserves careful design — is staged as its own increments. Reused row::rgb_from_time (the existing nav-separator hue source) for the divider so the RGB look is consistent app-wide and there's one source of truth for the animation. Deferred the 'Plant stacks crops' fix into the slot redesign because the slot model (fixed N slots per tower) is the correct fix (idempotent fill) rather than a band-aid on the current append-crops logic.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.407.0 — 'START COLLAPSED' IS NOW A BORDERED BUTTON (operator batch item: 'the start collapse should be given a proper border like the other buttons with the full bordered button area clickable, not just the tiny check box'). Replaced the widgets::toggle checkbox with widgets::Button::new('Start collapsed').active(state.trees_start_collapsed).show() — a full bordered button whose entire area is clickable; .active() renders the ON state like a pressed/primary button (so the on/off state is still obvious). Click toggles trees_start_collapsed + force-applies to the rendered trees that frame (unchanged behavior). VERIFIED: cargo check clean; all FOUR gui lints green. NEXT (the two BIG remaining batch items, each its own careful increment): v0.408 — make the inventory SECTIONS (Status/Equipment/You&places/Garden/Mining) collapsible CollapsingHeaders honoring the standard Collapse/Expand/Start-collapsed controls; this needs the controls block MOVED UP (currently declared after Equipment, so tree_default_open/tree_force aren't in scope for the earlier sections) + wrapping each section's content in a CollapsingHeader closure (re-parenting = careful brace work, hence its own increment). v0.409 — INLINE ROW EXPANSION: click an item row to expand it IN PLACE to a picture/3d-model + multi-row detail, click again to collapse (replaces the current detail-at-top); the operator's lead ask in this batch.
recent_decisions.why: A clean, contained fix for the specific 'make Start-collapsed a real button' ask, using the existing Button.active() (which is exactly the 'bordered, full-area-clickable, shows-its-state' control he described) rather than inventing a new toggle-button widget. Kept it its own increment because the next two items (collapsible sections, inline row expansion) are substantial restructures — the section work needs the controls relocated + 5 content re-parentings, and the row expansion is a new tree interaction — both warrant focused, individually-verified increments rather than being rushed in alongside a one-line button swap.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.406.0 — ROW STRIPING (#020202) + LOCATION INLINE (two quick items from the operator styling batch). (1) STRIPING: new row_stripe COLOR token (#020202, a subtle stripe against the #000000 panel — operator's exact ask); egui's visuals.faint_bg_color now points at it (was bg_card #1a1a22, too visible), so every striped Grid app-wide gets the subtle odd-row tint. Wired the full COLOR path: theme.rs field+accessor+default fn+reset+Default, theme.ron, the Settings APPEARANCE color editor ('Row stripe (odd rows)'), theme.css regen. theme_editor_coverage PASS (the color is an editable swatch). (2) LOCATION INLINE: place_to_tree now puts a place's location in the LABEL in parentheses — 'Home (Silverdale, WA · 47.6448°, -122.6952°)' — instead of right-aligned to the page edge (operator: move it next to the title in parens). VERIFIED: cargo check clean; all FOUR gui lints green. NEXT (remaining operator batch): v0.407 — 'Start collapsed' becomes a bordered Button (full-area clickable, via Button.active() toggle) + make the inventory SECTIONS (Status/Equipment/You&places/Garden/Mining) collapsible CollapsingHeaders that respect the standard Collapse/Expand/Start-collapsed controls (moved up to govern all); v0.408 — INLINE ROW EXPANSION (click an item row to expand in place to a picture/3d + multi-row detail, click to collapse).
recent_decisions.why: Picked the two lowest-risk, independent, immediately-visible items from the batch to ship quickly while the bigger section/expansion work is staged. Tokenized the stripe as a real COLOR (not a literal) per the operator's standing 'nothing hardcoded' + his exact #020202, and routed it through egui's faint_bg_color so all striped tables share it. Moved the location into the label because right-aligning it to the page edge (in a now-full-width single panel) pushed it far from the row it describes; parentheses keep it readable next to the name.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.405.0 — SLIM BUTTON SYSTEM (operator batch, item: 'update all buttons to be slim, even padding, the font sets the height; slim the mining +/- to match the garden'). The universal Button (button.rs) is now FONT-DRIVEN: removed the forced fixed-height min_size (was button_height token), so the egui button_padding wraps the content and the font sets the height. Padding is now even + tokenized: new theme.button_pad_y (vertical, default 3) pairs with button_padding_h (set to 8 in theme.ron, was a leftover 1.65; Default 16->8); apply_to_egui's button_padding was hardcoded (h, 6.0) -> now (button_padding_h, button_pad_y). .full_width / .min_height still override. STEPPERS: widgets::stepper_button square is now sized to theme.compact_button_height (18) instead of font+8, so the mining +/- match the garden's compact buttons. This makes EVERY standard button across the app slim + consistent (nav tabs, settings, dev buttons, Launch drone, …). button_pad_y wired the full path (theme.rs field+default+reset+Default, theme.ron, Settings 'Button Padding V' slider, theme.css regen). VERIFIED: cargo check clean; theme_editor_coverage PASS (button_pad_y is an editable row); theme_token + emdash + icon lints green. NEXT (same big operator batch, sequenced): v0.406 — 'Start collapsed' becomes a proper bordered button (full area clickable, not a tiny checkbox) + make every section (Equipment/You&places/Garden/Mining) a COLLAPSIBLE, more-defined section with the standard Collapse/Expand/Start-collapsed controls on ALL nested lists; v0.407 — row striping #000000/#020202 token + move the location text inline after the title in parentheses (was right-aligned to the page edge); v0.408 — INLINE ROW EXPANSION: click an item row to expand it in place (picture/3d model + full details over multiple rows), click again to collapse (instead of a popup/top detail).
recent_decisions.why: First + most foundational item of the operator's styling batch — the button look underpins everything else he asked for (the Start-collapsed button, the section headers, etc.), so doing the slim universal button first means the rest inherits it. Made height font-driven (his exact ask: 'the font sets the button's height') by dropping the fixed min_size, and made padding even + tokenized so he can fine-tune in Settings. Slid the steppers to the compact-button height so the whole inventory's buttons match. Shipped as its own increment because it is an app-wide visual change worth isolating + screenshotting before layering the section/striping/expansion work on top.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.404.0 — MINING SECTION ALIGNED INTO TABLES (operator, same message: 'fix up the mining section so the widgets are organized into aligned columns and rows'). The mining sub-sections were a loose list; converted all three to egui::Grid tables like the garden: (1) ASTEROIDS -> Grid [Asteroid | Type | Ores] (was a single concatenated label per asteroid); (2) DRONE MANIFEST builder -> Grid [Ore | Available | In-manifest] where the In-manifest cell holds the [-] qty [+] steppers with item_spacing.x=4 (was hand-aligned via allocate_ui_with_layout with magic widths 90/74/28); (3) ACTIVE DRONES -> Grid [Drone | Status | Fetching | Cargo] + a thin progress bar per drone capped at status_bar_width/height (was a single '·'-joined label + a full progress_bar). All striped, spacing [12,3]/[10,3] matching the garden table. VERIFIED: cargo check clean (no warnings); all FOUR gui lints green (no em-dash; headers are plain words). ** The operator's full v0.402-screenshot polish message is now done: garden rows tightened + compact buttons [v0.403], thin 5px bars [v0.403], mining aligned [v0.404]. ** NEXT (queued, his pick): the minor selected-item-detail-at-top relocation; then the garden PLOT-TYPES arc (infinite-of-X gardens). Let the operator screenshot + react.
recent_decisions.why: Completes the mining half of the operator's polish message, matching the garden table's aligned-grid style so the whole inventory page reads as one consistent spreadsheet. Replaced the ad-hoc allocate_ui_with_layout magic-width alignment in the manifest builder with a real Grid (self-aligning columns, no magic numbers), and gave the asteroids + drones proper column headers instead of concatenated label strings. Reused the thin capped bar (status_bar_height/width tokens from v0.403) for the drone progress so every bar in the page is consistent.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.403.0 — GARDEN ROW POLISH: compact action buttons + thin bars + tighter rows (operator on v0.402 screenshot: 'tighten the plant rows; the Harvest/Water/Fertilize buttons overlap + clip; make the thick orange bars thinner ~5px; not sure how to make nice short buttons right next to each other'). ROOT CAUSE of the overlap: the default-height buttons were TALLER than the tight grid rows, so adjacent rows' buttons overlapped. FIX: (1) new widgets::compact_button(ui, theme, label, ButtonVariant) — a SHORT, tight inline button: height = new theme.compact_button_height (18px), button_padding tightened to (6,1) for just that widget then restored, small font; the crop row's Harvest(Primary)/Water/Fertilize(Secondary) now use it with item_spacing.x=4 so they sit cleanly side by side. (2) new theme.status_bar_height token (5px) — the vitals (stat_row) + the garden growth bars are now 5px thin (stat_row was hardcoded 6.0). (3) Grid spacing tightened [12,4]->[10,3]. Both new tokens wired the full multi-site path (theme.rs field+default+reset+Default, theme.ron, Settings sliders, theme.css regen) so they are editable in-app — operator's 'nothing hardcoded'. theme_editor_coverage PASS (the 2 tokens are editor rows). VERIFIED: cargo check clean (no warnings); all FOUR gui lints green. NEXT (same operator message): v0.404 — align the MINING section into clean columns/rows like the garden table (asteroids list + the drone-manifest +/- stepper builder + active drones are currently a loose list).
recent_decisions.why: Directed polish on the spreadsheet inventory the operator is happy with. Diagnosed the button overlap as a height mismatch (buttons taller than the tightened rows) and solved it the way he asked — a reusable compact_button so several fit on one row, which is the 'nice short buttons right next to each other' he wasn't sure how to build. Made it a universal widget + theme token (not a one-off) so the whole app can use short buttons and the operator can tune the height/bar-thickness in Settings. Tokenized the 5px bar height too (he named 5px) so vitals + garden bars share one thin, editable height. Split the Mining alignment into its own increment so this one stays a clean, verifiable row-polish fix.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.402.0 — INVENTORY REDESIGN step 3: GARDEN = ALIGNED SPREADSHEET TABLE (operator: 'rows/columns/excel spreadsheet like design'). Reverted the v0.396 garden TREE+detail to a grouped TABLE: one collapsible group per tower (header = name + N/M ready or 'not planted'; respects the Collapse/Expand-all via .open(tree_force) + .default_open(tree_default_open)), each containing a Plant button + a compact egui::Grid with fixed columns [Plant | Stage | Growth | N·P·K | Water/day | Temp | State | Actions], one row per crop, the growth bar capped at theme.status_bar_width, and Harvest/Water/Fertilize inline. Seed-planted crops fall under 'Other crops'. SIMPLIFIED the detail block to ITEM-ONLY (dropped the crop/tower detail branches — the table shows that data inline; -113 lines via a boundary-checked splice) and removed the now-dead garden_tree_nodes + crop_leaf helpers (-70 lines). The Plant button per group restores tower-planting (was the dropped tower-detail's job). VERIFIED: cargo check clean (no warnings — dead helpers removed); all FOUR gui lints green (· U+00B7 + ° reliable; no em-dash). ** The operator's inventory feedback is now FULLY addressed: (1) ONE panel [v0.401]; (2) rows/columns spreadsheet [v0.402 garden table + v0.400 capped stat rows]; (3) stair-stepping fixed [capped bars + one panel + aligned Grid]; (4) status bars capped ~200px [v0.400 status_bar_width token]; (5) widths tokenized + editable in Settings, nothing hardcoded [v0.400]. ** KNOWN minor rough edge (noted, easy follow-up if he wants): the SELECTED-ITEM detail card still renders at the TOP of the panel (above the title) when an inventory item is clicked — could relocate inline after the places tree. The default (nothing-selected) view is clean. NEXT: let the operator test the redesign (v0.400-402) and react; otherwise the deferred garden PLOT-TYPES arc (infinite-of-X gardens) + the item-detail relocation.
recent_decisions.why: Completes the operator's 'then spreadsheet' step after the v0.401 one-panel merge. The garden table IS the rows/columns design he asked for, and moving the crop data into table columns dissolves the need for a separate detail panel (which was the multi-panel thinking he is moving away from). Capped the table's growth bar with the same theme token as the vitals so every bar is one consistent width. Kept the item detail (still useful for weight/category/Eat/Drop) but simplified the detail block to item-only and removed the dead tree helpers to keep the file clean. Left the item-detail-at-top as a flagged minor follow-up rather than risk another content relocation in the same increment — the default view is already clean and it only shows on item-click.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.401.0 — INVENTORY REDESIGN step 2: BACK TO ONE PANEL (operator: 'go back to one panel; the 3-panel split is stair-stepped'). Reverted the v0.390 left-nav + v0.396 right-detail SidePanels + the central Mining panel into a SINGLE egui::CentralPanel with one vertical ScrollArea. Done as pure WRAPPER SURGERY (a boundary-checked node splice, 1166->1136 lines): the detail block, the left content (creative toggle, vitals, equipment, tree controls, places tree, garden tree), and the Mining section were already in render order, so I removed the 3 panel wrappers and wrapped everything in one panel; the item-applies stayed inline (they only read detail-set actions). No content relocation. The DEFAULT view (nothing selected) is now a clean single column: Inventory title -> creative -> vitals (bars capped at theme.status_bar_width from v0.400) -> equipment -> Collapse/Expand/Start-collapsed -> 'You & your places' tree -> Garden tree -> Mining. The crop/tower/item detail renders inline only when something is selected (currently at the top of the panel). VERIFIED: cargo check clean (no warnings), all FOUR gui lints green. KNOWN ROUGH EDGES (next step): (1) the garden is still a TREE+detail, not the 'rows/columns Excel spreadsheet' the operator wants -> v0.402 = revert the garden to the aligned grouped TABLE (v0.389 style: Plant|Stage|Growth|N·P·K|Water/day|Temp|State|Actions, growth bar capped at status_bar_width, a Plant button per tower group), and drop the crop/tower detail (data lives in the table); (2) the selected-item detail currently appears at the TOP of the panel (above the title) -> relocate it inline after the places tree, and cap its growth bar. NEXT: v0.402 spreadsheet garden table + detail placement.
recent_decisions.why: The operator's feedback was sequential ('go back to one panel; THEN spreadsheet design'). This increment does the panel merge (step 1 of his 'then'), delivering his explicit lead ask cleanly + verified, while the v0.400 capped bars make the single column tidy. Did it as wrapper-only surgery (no content moved) so the diff is minimal and the risk low — the 600-line content stayed byte-identical, only the 3 panel wrappers became 1. Held the garden-table + detail-relocation for v0.402 so each step is verifiable and the operator can confirm 'one panel' before I rework the garden into the spreadsheet; shipping the merge now avoids another awkward-3-panel round trip and the default (nothing-selected) view is already clean.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.400.0 — INVENTORY REDESIGN step 1/2: STATUS BARS CAPPED + WIDTHS TOKENIZED (operator on v0.397 screenshot: 'limit those status bars like saturation/hydration/energy to 200px; make sure the variables are editable in settings/theme; universal so nothing is hardcoded'). Added THREE theme tokens — status_bar_width (200), stat_name_width (86), stat_value_width (82) — fully wired through the established multi-site path: theme.rs (field + serde default fn + reset() + Default), data/gui/theme.ron, a styled_slider for each in settings.rs draw_widgets_content (so they are editable in-app), and theme.css regenerated via gen-theme-css.js. widgets::stat_row now reads theme.stat_name_width/stat_value_width and CAPS the bar at theme.status_bar_width (was uncapped -> spanned the whole panel, the main 'stair stepping' the operator saw on the vitals). MIGRATED the old hardcoded STAT_NAME_W/STAT_VALUE_W consts to the tokens (removed the consts; the inventory body-temp row reads the tokens too) so nothing is hardcoded. VERIFIED: cargo check clean; theme_editor_coverage PASS (the 3 new tokens are editable rows in Settings — the test enforces 100% token coverage); theme_token_lint + emdash + icon_glyph green. NEXT (step 2/2, IMMEDIATELY): v0.401 — GO BACK TO ONE PANEL. The operator wants the 3-panel split (left nav / center Mining / right detail, from v0.390 + v0.396) reverted to a SINGLE panel with a clean rows/columns 'Excel spreadsheet' layout (aligned widgets, no stair-stepping), the garden back to an aligned TABLE. This step (theming) is the foundation that makes the single panel look clean; the merge is the structural revert.
recent_decisions.why: The operator's feedback was multi-part (one panel + spreadsheet alignment + cap bars + tokenize widths). Split into 2 increments: this one locks the THEMING (the explicit cap-bars + nothing-hardcoded asks) as a verifiable foundation, because the uncapped stat_row bar was the biggest single cause of the jagged look and capping it is independently correct regardless of panel count. Tokenized the stat column widths too (not just the bar) to honor 'universal, nothing hardcoded', and migrated the old consts so there is one source. Doing the big panel-merge as its own focused step (v0.401) de-risks it — the theming is proven before the structural rewrite.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.399.0 — HARVEST YIELDS SEEDS: the saved-seed loop is closed (operator chose 'all three' incl. harvest-yields-seeds). A SURVIVAL harvest now returns produce AND 2 seeds of that plant (seed_<plant>_0) into the player, so the garden is self-sustaining: plant 1 seed -> grow -> harvest -> get 2 back -> replant + a surplus to expand. Gated on !creative so CREATIVE inventories stay clean (no unneeded seed accumulation). Plot-agnostic (works for any plot type, not just aeroponics). One edit in the FarmingSystem harvest handler (right after the produce add, inside the player-inventory loop; uses item_registry.max_stack_for for the seed stack). VERIFIED: cargo check clean; farming 8/8 — extended plant_grow_harvest_full_loop to assert the survival harvest yields exactly 2 seed_tomato_0 (planted 1 -> 0, harvest granted 2); emdash + icon_glyph lints green (only farming/mod.rs changed, no gui strings). The basic seed ECONOMY LOOP is now complete end to end: seed items exist (v0.398), survival planting consumes them (v0.398), the starter set is grantable (v0.398 Dev: stock seeds), and harvest regenerates them (v0.399). NEXT — the BIG arc the operator opened, which needs his design input: DATA-DRIVEN GARDEN PLOT TYPES (gardens are infinite-of-X — aeroponic / soil / sand / pot / raised-bed / direct-sow / trays / not-yet-imagined, each data-defined + moddable). Also queued: optional seedling-tray sub-system; buy/trade seeds; on-new-game starter grant. This is a good point to let the operator test the accumulated work (the v0.396 left-tree layout, the seeds) before the plot-types architecture, which is a design collaboration.
recent_decisions.why: The next small, directed, non-forky step after v0.398 — it completes the satisfying core loop (plant -> grow -> harvest -> seeds -> replant) so survival is genuinely self-sustaining, not a dead end. Yielded 2 seeds (net +1 per cycle) so the loop sustains and slowly grows rather than just breaking even. Survival-only so creative stays tidy. Kept it plot-agnostic for the coming plot-type generalization. Stopping the autonomous run here after closing the loop: the next arc (data-driven plot types) is large and genuinely his to shape (which plot types, the schema), and 12 increments have landed since his last hands-on look — surfacing now respects the collaborative loop.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.398.0 — SEED ECONOMY step 1: seed items + survival-mode planting (operator's chosen first step, via AskUserQuestion). Added 47 new seed items to data/items.csv (seed_<plant>_0, generated from plants.csv display names) so all 51 distinct tower varieties have a seed (9 existed). SURVIVAL mode now consumes seeds: the FarmingSystem tower handler consumes one seed_<plant>_0 per variety and SKIPS varieties with no seed (logs planted/skipped); the individual seed-plant already consumed in survival (v0.395). CREATIVE (default) plants free. Seeds are PLOT-AGNOSTIC (a seed plants a crop in any plot, not just aeroponics) so this generalizes to the future non-aeroponic gardens. Added a 'Dev: stock seeds' button (inventory Garden header) that grants the STARTER SET = one seed of each distinct tower variety (operator chose 'one seed of each'), via a new pending_stock_seeds -> stock_seeds_request channel (registered in lib.rs runtime + farming make_store, bridged each frame) -> a FarmingSystem handler that ensure_slots + add_item; makes survival testable now (the on-new-game starter grant comes when the game is closer to ready). VERIFIED: cargo check clean; farming 8/8 incl. new survival_tower_planting_consumes_seeds (consumes the seeded variety, skips the unseeded) + creative_tower_planting_is_free; all FOUR gui lints + engine_wiring_lint green; items.csv validated (the tests load it). ** OPERATOR VISION captured (big, durable): GARDENS ARE INFINITE-OF-X. Aeroponic towers are just ONE plot type. He wants soil beds, SAND (he has sand at home, wants to recreate it), pots 'made out of anything', direct-sow (no tray), optional seedling/sprout trays, and 'all possible options, including those we haven't thought of yet' = a DATA-DRIVEN plot/growing-method system. Seed acquisition = ALL of: harvest-yields-seeds + optional tray step + buy/trade. The seed work here is deliberately plot-agnostic so it survives that generalization. ** NEXT (seed economy, his pick): harvest-yields-seeds (close the saved-seed loop); then the DATA-DRIVEN GARDEN PLOT TYPES (generalize 'tower' -> a plot/method registry: aeroponic/soil/sand/pot/raised-bed/..., each data-defined) which is the larger architectural arc he just opened; optional seedling-tray sub-system; on-new-game starter grant.
recent_decisions.why: Built exactly the first step the operator picked (seed items + survival planting), keeping creative the free default. Generated the 47 seed items from data rather than hand-typing, and named them from plants.csv so labels match. Made the survival path skip-missing-seed (not all-or-nothing) so a partial seed stock still plants what it can. Added the dev seed-grant because survival is otherwise untestable with no seed source, and it doubles as the 'one seed of each' starter set he chose. Kept everything PLOT-AGNOSTIC because his answer revealed a much bigger vision (gardens as infinite-of-X: soil/sand/pots/direct-sow/trays/unknown), and I did not want this increment to bake in aeroponic-only assumptions that the plot-type generalization would have to unwind.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.397.0 — TREE COLLAPSE CONTROLS: Collapse-all / Expand-all buttons + a 'Start collapsed' toggle for the inventory + garden nested lists (operator's third ask: 'add collapse/expand all buttons to the nested lists ... and a checkbox for default collapsed so when I first load the lists start collapsed instead of expanded'). WIDGET: new widgets::tree_list_ex(default_open, force) threads two controls through container_node -> the CollapsingState: default_open sets the initial state of branches with no stored state (Start-collapsed passes false); force = Some(bool) overrides EVERY branch open/closed for one frame (set_open), then persists until the next manual toggle. tree_list stays as the (true, None) wrapper, so no other caller changes. INVENTORY: a one-row control bar above the trees (Collapse all | Expand all | Start collapsed) drives BOTH the places tree and the Garden tree; the three tree_list calls became tree_list_ex(tree_default_open, tree_force). PERSISTENCE FINDING: neither GuiState nor SettingsState is actually saved to disk (SettingsState has a manual Default, is runtime-only), and egui's collapse state is also in-memory only -> so to honor 'when I FIRST LOAD start collapsed' across launches without building a settings-persistence layer, I put trees_start_collapsed in GuiState DEFAULTED TRUE: every launch starts collapsed (his wish) for free; he can Expand-all or untoggle for a session. Toggling 'Start collapsed' also force-applies to the already-rendered trees that frame so it feels immediate. VERIFIED: cargo check clean, all FOUR gui lints green. This completes the operator's three-ask batch (v0.395 creative mode, v0.396 garden->left tree + right detail, v0.397 collapse controls). NEXT (unblocked / his pick): the SEED ECONOMY (build it gated behind survival mode now that creative is the default), seedling/sprout harvesting trays, the base starting plant set; plus the still-standing backlog.
recent_decisions.why: Third of the operator's directed asks, completing the batch. Generalized the existing tree widget (tree_list_ex) rather than special-casing the inventory, so collapse control is reusable and other callers are untouched. Defaulted trees_start_collapsed=true because the deeper finding is that NO GUI pref persists to disk today and egui's own collapse state resets on restart, so the only way to satisfy 'start collapsed when I first load' without a new persistence layer is to make collapsed the launch default; the toggle still lets him flip it per session, and force-applying on toggle makes it immediate. A real persisted-settings layer is the proper future home for this + creative_mode.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.396.0 — INVENTORY LAYOUT CORRECTED: the GARDEN now lives in the LEFT nav tree (not the central panel), and the RIGHT panel shows details/actions for whatever is selected (operator: 'I explained the inventory/garden wrong; the garden tree stuff should be on the left with the rest of the inventory; when someone clicks an entry, either the player inventory or one of the garden plots, the right panel displays details about that selected object'). I had wrongly put the garden as a central workspace table; this is the file-manager model he actually wanted. CHANGES: (1) new GuiState.garden_selection: Option<String> ('crop:<entity_bits>' or 'tower:<id>'), mutually exclusive with selected_slot (selecting one clears the other). (2) LEFT panel gains a 'Garden' tree section under the 'You & your places' tree: garden_tree_nodes() builds one node per tower (a garden PLOT, id tower:<id>, detail 'N/M ready' or 'not planted') whose children are its growing crops (crop_leaf, id crop:<bits>, coloured/labelled by state); seed-planted crops fall under 'Other crops'. A 'Dev: grow all' sits in the Garden header. (3) RIGHT detail panel now renders by selection: a CROP (name, stage, growth bar, a card of Growth/Water/Health/N-P-K/Water-need/Temp, + Water/Fertilize/Harvest) OR a TOWER (name, purpose, Slots/Varieties/Planted/Water-draw/Harvest card, compat conflict warnings, + Plant this tower / Plant again + Dev grow) OR the existing ITEM detail. (4) CENTRAL panel is now Mining-only (the 8-column garden TABLE is gone — this also retires the table-width concern, since per-crop data now lives in the right detail).
