(5) WIDGET CHANGE: widgets::container_node now makes a branch HEADER selectable when it carries an id (was leaf-only), so a planted tower [a branch with crop children] can be PICKED, not only expanded; the collapse triangle stays independent; empty-id branches are unchanged so other tree_list callers are unaffected. Removed the old towers_tree_node + tower_humanize (static config display, superseded). VERIFIED: cargo check clean (no warnings), all FOUR gui lints green (⚠ · ° reliable, no em-dash). The central-garden splice was done with a node script (160-line block) after asserting the boundary lines, not a fragile Edit. NEXT: v0.397 = collapse/expand-all buttons + a default-collapsed checkbox for the trees (operator's third ask).
recent_decisions.why: Direct correction of my layout mistake, and the operator's described model IS the file-manager pattern: left tree = everything selectable (inventory + garden plots + crops), right panel = detail of the selection. Used a separate garden_selection so the existing item-detail flow stays intact (additive, lower risk) while the two selections stay mutually exclusive. Made tower branches selectable at the widget level (a clean, general improvement: a container you can select OR open) because the operator must be able to click a garden plot that already has crops. Moving the per-crop data into the right detail also dissolves the 8-column-width worry I had flagged. Spliced the big central block with a boundary-checked node script for reliability over a 160-line literal match.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.395.0 — CREATIVE / SURVIVAL MODE (operator: introduce creative+survival, default Creative during early dev, so we can build out the seed economy without needing seeds yet, and get seeds RIGHT before they bite). GuiState.creative_mode bool, default TRUE (Creative), NOT persisted, so it defaults Creative on every launch, exactly the early-dev default asked for. Bridged to a DataStore Mutex<bool> creative_mode EVERY frame (not one-shot). The resource-consuming systems read it: FARMING (plant seed + fertilize) and CRAFTING (materials) now skip BOTH the inventory requirement AND the consumption when creative. Absent flag = survival = consume, so every existing farming/crafting test still asserts consumption and passes. Toggle lives at the top of the inventory left panel (a widgets::toggle Creative mode + a one-line hint that flips Creative/Survival), discoverable where the operator is building the garden. VERIFIED: cargo check clean; farming 6/6 incl. new creative_mode_plants_without_consuming_seed (plants with no seed in inventory + does NOT consume a held seed); crafting 7/7 (consumption still works in survival). RESOLVES the seed-economy fork: build the seed system gated behind survival; creative (default) ignores it. FUTURE (operator): seedling/sprout harvesting trays + the player starting with a base plant set when the game is ready. DEFERRED here: mode persistence (default-Creative is correct for now); a proper Gameplay settings section (toggle is in the inventory for now); vitals/other consumers respecting creative (only seeds/fertilizer/crafting wired, his stated focus). NEXT (same operator batch): v0.396 move the GARDEN into the LEFT nav tree (I wrongly put it in the central panel) with the right panel showing the selected object's details; v0.397 collapse/expand-all + a default-collapsed checkbox for the trees.
recent_decisions.why: First of the operator's three directed asks and the foundation for the seed economy he wants to get right. Defaulted Creative + non-persisted because he explicitly said default Creative during early dev, so every launch starting Creative IS the spec and skipping persistence keeps the increment tight. Wired creative as a skip-requirement-AND-consumption flag on the two real resource consumers (farming seeds/fertilizer = his focus, crafting materials = coherence), reading absent-as-survival so no existing test breaks. Put the toggle in the inventory left panel (not buried in Settings) so it is discoverable right where he is iterating on the garden, honoring GUI-first.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.394.0 — TOWER SELF-SUFFICIENCY NUMBERS: each tower now shows its total daily WATER DRAW + HARVEST WINDOW on the Home page (the v0.383-proposed tie-in to the self-sufficiency loops). Folded into the existing compute_tower_compat registry pass (no new data flow): TowerCompat gains water_per_day_total (sum of plant water_per_day x slots across EVERY slot), first_harvest_days + full_harvest_days (min/max growth_days across the distinct species). Rendered as a ‘Water draw ~X L/day · Harvest ~Y-Z days’ line under each tower’s compatibility block. Completes the tower dashboard: plantings (what grows) + compatibility (grow together) + water/harvest (the loop numbers) + parts (build it). VERIFIED: cargo check clean; tower_compat tests 2/2 (extended the compatible-plants test CSV with growth_days+water columns, asserts water_total=1.1, harvest 40..45); homes test passes; all FOUR gui lints green (· separator safe, no em-dash). Uses data already in PlantDef (water_per_day, growth_days) — zero new parsing. DEFERRED — NEXT (operator’s pick / awaiting his feedback on the v0.389-394 visual changes he has not seen yet): seed economy (tower planting still free/dev; genuinely forky — requiring 33 specific seeds conflicts with towers-as-variety-demonstration, his call), deepen compat (largest compatible subset), or other direction. The aeroponic-tower feature is comprehensively end-to-end: curate -> Home (browse + compatibility + water/harvest + parts) -> 3D placeholder -> inventory tree -> plant/grow/harvest -> grouped crop table with NPK/water/temp.
recent_decisions.why: Continuing to ship clean, non-forky, directed-vision work per the standing directive (keep shipping; do not present option-menus or pause). The water/harvest tie-in was an explicit v0.383 proposed improvement, advances the documented self-sufficiency loop model, uses data already in PlantDef, and folds into the existing per-tower compute — so it is low-risk, independent of any likely inventory-panel feedback, and completes the tower’s needs/produces/grow-together/build picture. The genuinely forky next step (seed-economy mechanics) is held for the operator because it conflicts with the towers-as-demonstration purpose in his vision domain — an irreversible-ish product decision, the one kind the directive reserves for him.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.393.0 — TOWER REAL-WORLD PARTS LIST (the game->real bridge / NORTH STAR: every in-game system maps to a real buildable thing -> a parts list to 3D-print/buy/trade). Each aeroponic tower now carries a `parts` bill of materials, shown on the Home page under its plantings. Data-driven (infinite-of-X): new TowerPart {name, qty, source, note} + `parts: Vec<TowerPart>` on TowerConfig (serde default), populated in data/towers/aeroponic_configs.ron — ~9 parts per tower for the low-pressure tower-garden design (column body + 50 net cups + food-grade reservoir + submersible pump sized to the tower height + riser/cap + cycle timer + clay-pebble medium + A/B nutrients + pH kit), scaled by each tower’s geometry (nutrition 0.6m/2.4m gets a bigger reservoir/pump than apothecary 0.32m/1.9m). `source` tags (3d_print/buy/diy/trade/scavenge) render via a humanizer. Framed in the UI + the RON header as a STARTING list to refine, NOT a fixed spec — the structure is the durable part; the operator (who builds these for real) + community tune the specific parts via the data file (GUI-first/infinite-of-X intent: I built the scaffold, the values are his to edit). VERIFIED: cargo check clean; homes tower_configs_parse_max_variety extended (asserts >=5 parts each, every part has a name + source) PASS; tower_compat 2/2; all FOUR gui lints green (no em-dash; the `·` source separator + `3D-print` humanizer are safe). NOTE: the parts CONTENT is standard tower-garden components (uncontroversial general knowledge) but the operator may swap in his actual parts/sources — that is the intended division of labor, and only the RON changes. DEFERRED — NEXT tower features (operator’s pick): seed economy (tower planting is still free/dev), deepen the compat readout (per-plant axis breakdown / largest compatible subset), or wire the parts list into the homestead BOM aggregation. The aeroponic-tower feature is now FULLY end-to-end incl. the real-build bridge.
recent_decisions.why: After completing the operator’s entire redesign message (v0.388-391) + the grow-together compat check (v0.392), the standing NORTH STAR (CLAUDE.md/PRIORITIES: design game systems NOW as real buildable things, each with a parts list) was the clear, mission-central next step — the bridge from the game tower to a real homestead someone can actually build, which is the platform’s actual purpose. Built it data-driven so the durable engineering (the parts field + render + the game->real framing) is mine and the domain-specific content is a refinable data file — the right split given the operator is the homestead expert. Kept the starter BOM to standard, uncontroversial tower-garden components and labeled it refinable, so even if he swaps parts the scaffold + most of the list stands. Reversible placement (Home page, the design-reference view), so no pause per the directive.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.392.0 — TOWER COMPATIBILITY CHECK: "can these plants share one reservoir + air?" (operator’s standing ask from the max-variety reframe: "make sure they’d all grow together too"). Aeroponics shares ONE nutrient reservoir + ONE air volume (not soil), so soil companion/adverse rules relax and the real constraint is a COMMON pH / temperature / humidity window every plant in a tower tolerates. Built it on the ph/temp/humidity data v0.391 just made PlantDef parse: new TowerCompat struct + compute_tower_compat(tower, registry) + an intersect_axis helper (gui/mod.rs) that INTERSECTS each axis across the tower’s distinct species — Some((lo,hi)) if all windows overlap (they can share it), None if empty (a conflict), naming the two binding extremes (highest floor vs lowest ceiling) per conflicting axis. Degenerate 0..0 windows (a missing CSV column) are skipped so absent data can’t fake a conflict; species missing from the registry are skipped. Cached in GuiState.tower_compat (parallel to tower_configs), computed ONCE in the lib.rs crop sync (where plant_reg is already fetched; same disjoint-field borrow as the crop loop; guarded by is_empty so it runs a single time, in the menu loop too). Rendered on the Home page per tower (homes.rs draw_tower, threaded compat through draw_design): green ‘✓ These N plants share one reservoir: pH x-y, a-b°C, humidity p-q%’ when clean, else ‘Shared where they can: …’ + a ⚠ line per conflict (‘Temp: Rosemary vs Lettuce have no overlap’) + a ‘split into a separate tower/zone’ hint. VERIFIED: cargo check clean; new gui::tower_compat_tests 2/2 (compatible plants → one shared window + no conflict; non-overlapping temp → flagged, pH still reported); farming 5/5; all FOUR gui lints green (✓ U+2713 + ⚠ U+26A0 are reliable per CLAUDE.md; no em-dash, hyphen ranges). The real max-variety towers (33 distinct food / 24 herbs) will likely surface genuine temp conflicts — that IS the feature working (the data is real: tomato ph 6.0-6.8, 18-30°C, humidity 0.5-0.8). DEFERRED — NEXT tower features: seed economy (planting a tower should cost seeds, not be free/dev), and the real-world parts list (the north-star real-build bridge). Could also DEEPEN the check: show per-plant which axis pushed it out, or compute the largest compatible SUBSET. NEXT: seed economy or parts list (operator’s pick); else deepen the compat readout.
recent_decisions.why: This is the operator’s explicit "grow together" requirement, and v0.391 had just made the needed pH/temp/humidity data available in PlantDef — so it was the natural, ready next feature (the directive is to keep shipping directed work, resolving reversible forks by making the call). Chose the reservoir-intersection model because it is the physically correct constraint for aeroponics (shared water+air, not soil) — the insight the operator himself flagged when reframing to max-variety. Put the compute where the registry already lives (the crop sync) and cached it in GuiState so the Home page (which only gets GuiState) can render it without plumbing the DataStore into the page. Surfaced it on the Home page tower browser because that is the design-reference view; reversible placement, so no pause. Named the binding plants per conflict so the readout is ACTIONABLE (which plants to move), not just a red/green verdict.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.391.0 — GARDEN crop table gains NPK + Water/day + Temp columns (operator’s last redesign “maybe”: “add columns for npk and other nutrients, water, temp, etc.”). FINDING: the data existed in plants.csv (nutrient_n/p/k, ph_min/max, temp_min_c/max_c, humidity_min/max) but was DROPPED at parse — the in-memory PlantDef/PlantRow only kept id/name/growth_days/water_per_day/stages/seasons/yield. So extended the farming data model to actually parse the full row: added nutrient_n/p/k, ph_min/max, temp_min_c/max_c, humidity_min/max to PlantRow (all #[serde(default)], so the lean test fixture + any leaner CSV still parse) + PlantDef + the from_csv construction. Surfaced N/P/K, water_per_day, temp_min/max into GuiCrop (populated in the lib.rs crop sync, which already had the PlantDef `def` in hand). Rendered three new compact columns in the grouped Garden Grid — “N·P·K” (0.15·0.05·0.20 demand fractions), “Water/day” (L), “Temp” (18-30°C) — between Growth and the renamed “State” (current water/health) column; 8 columns total. Parsed ph/humidity too (not shown yet) because the future tower compatibility check (shared-reservoir pH/temp/humidity window) needs them — parse the full row ONCE. VERIFIED: cargo check clean; farming lib tests 5/5 (added from_csv_parses_nutrient_temp_and_humidity_columns, a full-width-row parse lock that also asserts yield still parses PAST the new columns); all FOUR gui lints green (· U+00B7 and ° U+00B0 are reliable Latin-1 glyphs, already used elsewhere; no em-dashes — used a plain hyphen in the temp range). NOTE/WATCH: 8 columns + the 200px bar is wide; on a narrow window the rightmost Actions could clip (central workspace uses vertical-only scroll). Operator runs a large monitor + the left panel is resizable, so it should fit; if it clips, move the needs-columns into a tooltip or the detail panel (did NOT switch to ScrollArea::both because the Mining manifest_bar relies on ui.available_width()). DEFERRED (sequenced): the tower COMPATIBILITY CHECK (compute the shared-reservoir pH/temp/humidity window across a tower’s plants + flag outliers = the operator’s “make sure they grow together” feature) — the ph/humidity/temp data is now parsed and ready. NEXT: that compatibility check.
recent_decisions.why: This is the final item from the operator’s inventory-redesign message (v0.388 table, v0.389 group-by-tower, v0.390 left+right panels, this = the needs columns). The honest fix was bigger than a render tweak: the CSV had the columns but the parser silently discarded them, so per-CObY the do-it-right principle I extended the data model to parse the WHOLE row once (including ph/humidity the next feature needs) rather than bolt on just the three asked-for fields and re-touch PlantDef again next increment. Kept every new field serde-default so nothing downstream breaks on a short CSV, and locked it with a full-row parse test. Rendered them compactly; flagged the width risk honestly instead of pre-optimizing a scroll change that would have destabilized the Mining bar.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.390.0 — INVENTORY page split into LEFT nav + RIGHT detail + CENTRAL workspace (operator: “upgrade the inventory page to be at least left panel and right panel”; the page was one cramped vertical column). The vitals/equipment/tree/garden/mining were all nested inside ONE shared CentralPanel+ScrollArea, so I rewrote the panel boundaries (4 hand edits, brace-balanced — verified by re-deriving the open/close depth): NEW egui::SidePanel::left("inv_nav") (resizable, default 330 / min 250, bg_sidebar) holding a single ScrollArea over [title, weight, the 6 survival vitals + Rest/Compost, Equipment, the “You & your places” container tree]; the existing RIGHT SidePanel item-detail is unchanged (now reads a selection the left tree sets — a 1-frame lag, invisible since a click forces a repaint); the CentralPanel keeps Garden + Mining in their own scroll. So the horizontal space is used: structure left, act center, selected-item detail right (file-manager shape). VERIFIED: cargo check clean, all FOUR gui lints green (emdash 2/2, theme_token 1/1, theme_editor_coverage 2/2, icon_glyph 1/1), farming 4/4, release build exit 0; inventory.rs diff is a tight 30 lines. DEFERRED (sequenced, unchanged): (c) NPK/water/temp columns on the crop table (surface PlantDef data — plants.csv -> runtime PlantRegistry — into GuiState/GuiCrop); then the tower compatibility check (shared-reservoir pH/temp/humidity window + outlier flags = the “grow together” feature). NEXT: the NPK/nutrient columns.
recent_decisions.why: This is the last unaddressed item in the operator’s redesign message (nested-by-tower DONE v0.389; single-row/fixed-cols/200px-bar DONE v0.388; left+right panels = this). He set the minimum bar (“at least left + right”), resolving the 2-vs-3-panel fork I had held — landed on 3 columns (left nav / center workspace / right detail) because the right detail panel already existed and worked, making this a clean MOVE of the status+tree block into a new left panel, not a rewrite of the detail flow. Rewrote the panel boundaries deliberately (the blocks shared one closure) and verified depth balance before compiling.
recent_decisions.lesson: DO NOT run cargo fmt (or `cargo fmt -p humanity-engine`) in this repo. The codebase is NOT maintained rustfmt-clean, so a whole-crate fmt reformatted 242 files (24k+/12k- lines) of pure whitespace churn — and worse, it MOVED a trailing `// theme-exempt:` comment onto its own line (the source line exceeded rustfmt’s 100-col max_width), which silently BROKE theme_token_lint (the marker must be INLINE on the same line as the Color32 literal). I nearly shipped both the churn and a self-inflicted lint failure mis-diagnosed as a pre-existing main bug. Caught it with `git diff --stat` showing 242 files before pushing, reverted every fmt-only file to HEAD, and re-applied my real edits by hand. RULE: match surrounding style manually; never run cargo fmt here. And the EARLIER lesson still holds: when touching src/gui/ or src/renderer/, run all four style lints before shipping (emdash, theme_token, theme_editor_coverage, icon_glyph), not just emdash.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.389.0 — GARDEN crop list now NESTED + COLLAPSIBLE BY TOWER (the operator’s lead redesign request: “make the list more like a nested list similar to the inventory; each tower can be collapsible into their individual selves”). DID: added a crop->tower LINKAGE (tower_id: Option<String>) to CropInstance (ecs/components.rs) + GuiCrop (gui/mod.rs), threaded end-to-end: pending_plant_tower is now a (tower_id, plant_ids) TUPLE; the plant_tower_request DataStore channel carries the tuple (registered in BOTH lib.rs runtime ~984 AND farming make_store ~567 — the v0.387 lesson); the FarmingSystem tower handler tags each spawned crop with tower_id: Some(id); lib.rs crop sync mirrors tower_id into GuiCrop. The inventory Garden render groups state.crops by tower_id into an egui::CollapsingHeader per tower (title = tower config name, “N/M ready”, default_open, id_salt) each containing the v0.388 compact aligned Grid (Plant | Stage | Growth 200px | Water/Health | Actions); seed-planted crops fall under “Other crops”. The “Plant a tower” button now sends (tower.id, ids). VERIFIED: cargo check clean; release build exit 0; emdash_lint 2/2; farming lib tests 4/4 (seed+harvest mapping, full plant/grow/harvest loop, fertilize, csv parse). DEFERRED (sequenced, unchanged): (5) the LEFT+RIGHT panel inventory page restructure; (6) NPK/water/temp columns — needs PlantDef data (plants.csv -> runtime PlantRegistry) surfaced into GuiState/GuiCrop. NEXT: left/right inventory panels, then plant-data columns; then tower compatibility check (shared-reservoir pH/temp/humidity window + outlier flags = the “grow together” feature).
recent_decisions.why: This is the exact next slice the operator led with after confirming the farming loop works. It required the data-model change I sequenced in v0.388 (a crop->tower linkage) so the table could group by tower — done as its own verifiable increment per the no-big-risky-multi-file-change-without-isolation norm. Reused the v0.388 compact Grid verbatim inside each collapsible group, so the only new surface is the grouping + the tower_id plumbing, all type-checked and test-covered. Registered the tuple channel in BOTH runtime and test stores up front to avoid repeating the v0.387 channel-only-in-test bug.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.388.0 — GARDEN crop list = compact aligned TABLE (first step of the operator's inventory redesign). Operator confirmed the farming loop works (screenshots: crops growing -> ready-to-harvest) and asked for a UX redesign of the crop list: (1) make it a NESTED list like the inventory, each TOWER collapsible; (2) each crop a SINGLE row not three; (3) FIXED-size columns per variable; (4) the progress bar ~200px not the full page width ('rough to view'); (5) upgrade the whole inventory page to LEFT + RIGHT panel; (6) maybe add columns for NPK / nutrients / water / temp. DID (the highest-value, lowest-risk first slice): replaced the 3-row-per-crop cards in the inventory Garden section with a compact egui::Grid TABLE -- one row per crop, fixed auto-aligned columns (Plant | Stage | Growth | Water/Health | Actions), striped rows, and the growth bar fixed at desired_width(200) instead of full width. Addresses points 2/3/4 (single row, fixed columns, 200px bar) immediately + low-risk (one render edit, no data-model change). DEFERRED (the bigger pieces, sequenced): (1) GROUPING by tower needs a tower-crop LINKAGE -- add tower_id: Option<String> to CropInstance + GuiCrop, thread it through pending_plant_tower (-> a (tower_id, plant_ids) tuple) + the channel + the FarmingSystem handler (tag each spawned crop), then render a collapsible group per tower; (5) the LEFT+RIGHT panel inventory restructure (a real page redesign); (6) NPK/water/temp columns need the PlantDef data (plants.csv) surfaced into the GUI (it is in the runtime PlantRegistry/DataStore, not GuiState). VERIFIED: cargo check clean (no warnings); release build + emdash_lint pending. NEXT: the tower-grouping (tower_id linkage + collapsible-per-tower), then the left/right panel + the plant-data columns.
recent_decisions.why: The operator's redesign was multi-part (nested-by-tower + compact rows + fixed columns + 200px bar + left/right panel + NPK columns) -- too much for one safe increment, and the v0.386 channel bug is a fresh reminder that big multi-file changes carry risk. So I shipped the single highest-value, lowest-risk slice first: the compact aligned table fixes the biggest stated pain ('full page width is rough', 'three rows each') with one render edit and no data-model change, using egui::Grid for the fixed-column alignment he wanted. The grouping-by-tower he led with is the immediate next step but needs a crop->tower linkage (a data-model change across several files) so it is its own increment; the left/right panel is a page restructure; the NPK columns need the plant-def data threaded into the GUI. Sequenced them so each is verifiable on its own, keeping the tight iteration the operator clearly likes.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.387.0 — FIX: plant-a-tower (v0.386) did nothing (operator: 'nothing happens when I click plant a tower or dev grow all'). ROOT CAUSE: I registered the new 'plant_tower_request' DataStore channel in the WRONG place. The gardening channels are registered in TWO spots: the runtime (lib.rs ~984-990, in the winit `resumed` startup) AND a TEST helper farming::gardening_tests::make_store (farming/mod.rs ~547, 'mirroring the runtime wiring'). My v0.386 edit anchored on `data.insert("plant_request", ...)` with replace_all:false, which matched the FIRST occurrence -- the TEST make_store -- so the channel was registered for the tests but NOT the runtime. At runtime data.get("plant_tower_request") returned None, so the bridge wrote nothing + the FarmingSystem handler drained nothing -> no crops spawned. (dev-grow-all 'did nothing' too simply because there were no crops to grow -- it is gated on existing crops, not broken.) FIX: added `data_store.insert("plant_tower_request", Mutex::new(Option::<Vec<String>>::None))` to the RUNTIME registration in lib.rs (next to plant_request). Verified the rest of the chain is ungated + runs in the menu: system_runner.tick (lib.rs 1646) + the bridge + the GuiState.crops sync are NOT gated on world_loaded (world_loaded only gates the lazy 3D-world LOAD at lib.rs 3676), and the FarmingSystem is registered at startup (lib.rs 1021) -- consistent with the operator's vitals decaying in the menu. So with the channel registered, the full loop now works from the Inventory Garden without entering 3D. VERIFIED: cargo check clean; release build + emdash_lint pending. LESSON (logged): when adding a DataStore channel, register it in the RUNTIME (lib.rs resumed, ~984) AND the test make_store (farming/mod.rs); replace_all:false on a string that exists in both hits the test first. NEXT: operator re-test plant->grow->harvest; then the compatibility check + tower-crop linkage.
recent_decisions.why: A real bug from my v0.386 work: the channel-registration anchor matched the test helper before the runtime, so the runtime never knew the channel. Diagnosed by tracing the symptom (both plant-tower AND dev-grow 'do nothing' -> the FarmingSystem isn't getting the command) rather than guessing, which led to the duplicate-registration-site discovery (grep showed the runtime channels live in lib.rs 982-1000, and the farming/mod.rs inserts I'd edited were inside make_store, a #[cfg(test)] helper). Confirmed nothing else is gated on world_loaded so the menu-driven garden works without 3D. Logged the lesson so the next channel addition does not repeat it.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.386.0 — AEROPONIC TOWERS increment 3: FARMING HOOKUP (plant a tower -> crops grow -> harvest). Operator: 'keep developing the rest of the features.' The towers were fully REPRESENTED (curation/Home/3D/inventory) but not FUNCTIONAL; this makes them work, reusing the existing gardening loop. An Explore agent mapped the farming system: the GUI->ECS bridge pattern (GuiState flag -> main-loop writes a DataStore Mutex channel -> FarmingSystem drains in tick) is identical for plant/water/harvest/dev-grow; CropInstance is a logical entity (crop_def_id = a plants.csv id, growth_stage, planted_at, water_level, health) with NO Transform; growth/water/harvest/dev-grow + the GuiState.crops mirror + the inventory Garden render are all REUSABLE as-is. BUILT (dev-friendly default I proposed to the operator: planting a tower spawns its curated varieties with NO seed consumption, to get the loop working; the seed-economy + staggered planting layer on later): (1) GuiState.pending_plant_tower: Option<Vec<String>> (the plant ids). (2) lib.rs bridge: pending_plant_tower -> the new DataStore 'plant_tower_request' Mutex<Option<Vec<String>>> channel (mirrors pending_plant_seed). (3) FarmingSystem: register the channel + a handler that drains it and spawns one CropInstance per plant id (first_stage from PlantRegistry, water 1.0, health 100) -- the SAME spawn the seed handler uses, just batched + no seed cost. (4) inventory Garden section: a 'Plant a tower:' row with a button per tower config; clicking sends the flat plant-id list (honoring planting.slots). The spawned crops auto-mirror to GuiState.crops and render in the Garden with the existing Water/Fertilize/Harvest/Dev-grow-all controls -- so the whole loop (plant tower -> 33 crops appear -> grow -> harvest into inventory + Farming XP) works with ZERO new growth/harvest code. VERIFIED: cargo check clean (no warnings); 4 farming tests pass incl. plant_grow_harvest_full_loop (no regression); release build + emdash_lint pending. NEXT: the compatibility check (plant pH/temp/humidity shared window); tying tower crops back to their tower (a tower_id on CropInstance so the inventory tower nodes show 'N/M ready'); then the seed-economy + the real-world parts list.
recent_decisions.why: The operator said keep developing, and the highest-value next step was making the towers FUNCTIONAL (they were only display so far). Investigating first paid off hugely: the entire gardening loop (growth, water, harvest, the GUI mirror, the Garden render) was reusable, so the hookup was just a new command channel + a batched-spawn handler + a button -- I added almost no new gameplay logic, I connected the towers to the proven loop. Used the dev-friendly no-seed-cost default I had floated to the operator (he repeatedly said 'just get it to work'), keeping the seed economy as a later layer. Put the 'Plant a tower' buttons in the inventory Garden section where crops already live, so the plant->grow->harvest loop is all in one place. Verified the existing farming tests still pass (the full plant-grow-harvest loop) so the new handler didn't regress the gardening system.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.385.0 — AEROPONIC TOWERS in the inventory + winding fix CONFIRMED. Operator (two screenshots of v0.384): (a) the garden view shows the cylinders now render SOLID from outside + the two towers are visibly different (wide+dense nutrition vs slim+coarse apothecary) -> the row-major sphere-winding fix WORKED, and the data-driven geometry is visible. (b) the Inventory page -> 'I'm not seeing the towers in the inventory page. We're making progress. Just gotta keep developing the rest of the features.' BUILT: the towers now appear in the inventory's 'You & your places' tree, matching the operator's container model (everything is a Place/container; a tower is a structure that holds plants). inventory.rs: a new towers_tree_node builds an 'Aeroponic Towers' TreeNode whose children are the two towers (each labeled with its name + plant count + height), and each tower's children are its planted varieties (humanized names + role). It is injected UNDER the Home node (find by label 'Home'; fallback to top-level) so it sits in the home's hierarchy. Reused the existing widgets::TreeNode + kind_color; added a local tower_humanize. VERIFIED: cargo check clean (no warnings); release build + emdash_lint pending. NEXT: the compatibility check (plant pH/temp/humidity shared window + outliers) + the farming hookup (plant a tower -> CropInstances grow -> harvest); the inventory tower nodes are non-selectable display for now (clicking a plant could later show its details / planting state).
recent_decisions.why: The operator confirmed the v0.384 winding fix visually (the principled sphere-winding match worked, no trial-and-error needed) and flagged a real gap: the towers existed on the Home page + in 3D but not in the inventory's places tree, where his container model says they belong (a tower is a container in your home holding plants). So I injected them into that tree under Home, reusing the existing TreeNode/place rendering rather than inventing a parallel widget. Kept them under Home (correct semantics) with a top-level fallback; display-only for now since selection/planting is the farming increment. Small, focused increment addressing exactly his observation while he said 'keep developing the rest of the features.'

recent_decisions.decision: v0.384.0 — AEROPONIC TOWERS increment 2b: fix inverted cylinder normals + DATA-DRIVEN tower geometry. Operator (with a screenshot of v0.383): 'face normals are inverted, I'm seeing the inside of the cylinder. At least I can see them! We'll need to design the towers + determine the amount DYNAMICALLY. The helix design is great, scales infinitely. We could allow a wide-diameter tower + adjust helix density vertically, like coarse vs fine threads on a bolt.' FIXES: (1) INVERTED NORMALS: the cylinder side rendered backfacing (you saw the inner far wall) while the SPHERE rendered fine. Root cause: my cylinder used a different (effectively reversed) index winding than the sphere. Fixed by rebuilding Mesh::cylinder with ROW-MAJOR rings (bottom ring then top ring, ring*(seg+1)+i) and the IDENTICAL winding as Mesh::sphere ([a,b,a+1, a+1,b,b+1]) -- now outward-facing. Dropped the top/bottom caps (they were also inverted + a real tower's top is open-ish anyway); it is a solid-walled open tube. (2) DATA-DRIVEN GEOMETRY (his 'dynamic + scalable' + bolt-thread idea): TowerConfig gains diameter_m + height_m + helix_turns (serde defaults 0.4 / 2.0 / 4.0); the RON sets them per tower to SHOW the parametrization -- nutrition = wide + fine (0.6m / 2.4m / 6 turns), apothecary = narrow + coarse (0.32m / 1.9m / 2.5 turns). load_world now reads each tower's geometry: a per-tower cylinder of that diameter/height + one plant-marker sphere per CURATED VARIETY (dynamic count = plantings.len(), capped at 40 for the object budget) along a helix of helix_turns wraps, markers sitting just off the column (radius + 0.12). Towers spaced by their width so wide ones do not overlap. Borrow-safe: geometry snapshotted into a local Vec before the renderer mutations. VERIFIED: cargo check clean; tower test passes (RON + geometry parse, max-variety holds); release build + emdash_lint pending. Visual correctness operator-verified next (the winding fix is reasoned from the working sphere; can't run GUI headless). NEXT: the compatibility check (plant pH/temp/humidity shared window + outlier flags) + the farming hookup. The fuller dynamic model (capacity computed FROM geometry: circumference x height / spacing; plants_per_turn) is a refinement once planting is real.
recent_decisions.why: The operator caught a real visual bug (inverted cylinder faces) and gave a strong design direction (dynamic, scalable geometry). The winding fix was diagnosable from the evidence: the sphere rendered correctly, so I made the cylinder use the sphere's exact winding (row-major rings) rather than guessing a flip -- a principled fix, not trial-and-error (which I can't do headless). His coarse-vs-fine-threads framing is a clean parametric model, so I made diameter + height + helix density data-driven per tower and set the two towers to visibly DIFFERENT values so he can confirm the parametrization works at a glance. The plant count follows the curated varieties (his 'one of each' max-variety), with the geometry shaping the arrangement. Kept the deeper capacity-from-geometry math for when planting is real (don't over-build before the farming loop needs it).

recent_decisions.decision: v0.383.0 — AEROPONIC TOWERS increment 2: MAX-VARIETY reframe + 3D placeholder. Operator: 'anything else to improve? proceed with the plan. Not concerned about specific plants, the community will tune the mix. We could just say max variety -- instead of 10 lettuce, 1 lettuce + other types, to show the wide range people can grow. Make sure they all grow together. Excited to see the placeholder model.' DID: (1) REFRAMED both towers to MAXIMUM VARIETY (one of each, not multiples): nutrition = 33 distinct food varieties, apothecary = 24 distinct herbs, slots:1 each (slots stays 50 = net-cup capacity; room for the community to add more). Updated the test to the max-variety invariant (all distinct, within capacity, >=20 varieties) -- passes. (2) Captured the COMPATIBILITY insight (his 'grow together' requirement) in the RON header + reply: in AEROPONICS plants share a nutrient reservoir + the room's air, NOT soil, so soil-based companion/adverse rules relax (no allelopathy / soil-pest sharing) -- the real constraint is a shared reservoir pH + a shared temp/humidity window, which actually WIDENS the compatible variety vs a soil bed. A compatibility check computing that window from plants.csv (ph/temp/humidity columns already exist) is the next feature + a teaching tool. (3) BUILT THE 3D PLACEHOLDER (operator excited): a render-path Explore mapped it (a direct state.placeholder_objects: Vec<(mesh_idx,mat_idx,Vec3)> list pushed into all_objects with per-object model matrices, like homestead_floors; ECS Renderable is NOT drawn). Added Mesh::cylinder + Mesh::sphere helpers (src/renderer/mesh.rs, following the cube/from_vertices pattern); a grey cylinder per tower config + a green sphere per planted variety in a 3-turn helix, placed on the GARDEN room floor side by side (markers capped at 12/tower for the object budget; full variety lives in the Home list). CRASH-SAFE: render_scene_onto soft-truncates at 256 objects (if i>=256 break) + continues on bad mesh idx, so no panic; placeholders are added before the holograms so they're within budget. (4) IMPROVEMENTS proposed to the operator: the aeroponic-compatibility check (above); tie the tower to the self-sufficiency loops (sum water_liters_per_day = water-loop demand + a harvest timeline from growth_days); community tower 'recipes' (a tower is data -> anyone publishes a salsa/tea/kids tower); staggered planting for continuous harvest. VERIFIED: cargo check clean (no warnings); tower max-variety test passes; release build + emdash_lint pending. Visual correctness (winding/placement) is operator-verified (can't run GUI headless); geometry reasoned carefully + the path matches the homestead. NEXT: the compatibility check (load plant ph/temp/humidity, compute the tower's shared window, show it + flag outliers) + the farming hookup (plant a tower -> CropInstances grow -> harvest).
recent_decisions.why: The operator's max-variety reframe is better (showcases the range you can grow; 'community tunes the mix' = his federation/sharing vision), so I adopted it immediately -- a data change + a test-invariant flip. The 'grow together' requirement turned into a genuine teaching insight (aeroponics relaxes soil companion rules; the real constraint is the shared reservoir pH/temp window), which I captured now + will build as a verification feature next. The placeholder was his explicit excitement, so I prioritized it: investigated the exact render path (don't guess at wgpu), wrote standard cylinder/sphere geometry, reused the homestead's direct-list render path (not the un-rendered ECS Renderable), and verified the 256-object cap is a SOFT truncate (no crash) before shipping a visual I can't run headless. Capped the markers to protect the per-frame budget while still looking planted (the real variety is exhaustively in the Home list). Offered improvements that deepen the real-world bridge (water/harvest math ties the tower to the self-sufficiency loops; community recipes make the data-driven design a sharing feature).

recent_decisions.decision: v0.382.0 — AEROPONIC TOWERS increment 1: two curated 50-plant tower configs + a Home-page browser. Operator (new task): 'aim for the ideal default minimalistic vegetable garden on a 50-plant aeroponic tower covering all nutritional needs as best as possible; then another 50-plant tower for herbs/spices incl. natural medicinal properties; eventually I'll make 3D models, use simple shapes as stand-ins.' An Explore agent mapped data/plants.csv (128 plants, 30-col schema) + found EVERY target species already exists (kale/spinach/lettuce/beet/cabbage/broccoli/cauliflower/bean/pea/soybean/tomato/pepper/cucumber/strawberry/carrot/radish/chive for nutrition; basil/mint/oregano/thyme/rosemary/sage/parsley/cilantro/dill/chive/lemongrass/chamomile/lavender/ginger/turmeric/echinacea/valerian/feverfew/calendula/st_johns_wort/comfrey for the apothecary) -- so ZERO new plant data, pure curation. Also: nutrition is NOT tracked per-plant at runtime (the FoodSystem is satiation/hydration + generic profiles), so 'covers nutrition' is a DESIGN-level curation documented in the data, not a runtime sim. BUILT data/towers/aeroponic_configs.ron: two towers, each summing to exactly 50 slots -- (1) 'Daily Greens and Beans' (nutrition): 17 species (greens/brassicas/legumes incl. soybean for protein/fruiting/roots/allium) with a covers list (vit A/C/K/folate/iron/calcium/K+Mg/fiber/plant-protein) and an HONEST gaps list (bulk calories, fats, B12, D, omega-3 -> field crops/ranch/sun/animals, not a tower); (2) 'Remedy and Flavor' (apothecary): 21 herb species with per-plant culinary + TRADITIONAL/folk medicinal notes and a clear disclaimer (not medical advice; cautions flagged per plant -- comfrey topical-only, valerian sedative, st_johns_wort drug-interactions, feverfew not-in-pregnancy). New TowerConfig/TowerPlanting structs + load_tower_configs (RON) in gui/mod.rs; GuiState.tower_configs loaded at startup; an 'Aeroponic towers' section on the Home page (collapsible per tower: purpose/covers/gaps/disclaimer + the 50-slot planting list with count/plant/role/note). Test asserts the RON parses + BOTH towers sum to 50. VERIFIED: cargo check clean; tower test passes; release build + emdash_lint pending. NO em-dashes in the data strings (they render in-app). NEXT: increment 2 = the 3D placeholder (a cylinder Mesh helper + a tower entity placed in the world + simple plant markers), then increment 3 = farming integration (plant a tower -> CropInstances grow -> harvest -> nutrition).
recent_decisions.why: Direct operator content/design task, and the investigation paid off twice: every plant already exists (so this is curation over the real plant DB, no duplication) and nutrition isn't a runtime sim (so the towers document their nutritional/medicinal design as data, which is exactly the real-world-bridge north star -- the food loop made concrete). I led with the HONEST constraint (towers can't do bulk calories/fats, so I put a gaps list right in the data) because a homestead-nutrition claim has to be truthful, not aspirational. I framed all medicinal notes as culinary + traditional/folk use with explicit cautions + a disclaimer, because health claims demand responsibility. Curated as DATA (infinite-of-X: more towers are just more RON) and shipped the browser first so the operator can review + refine the composition before I invest in the 3D placeholder + farming wiring (he cares most about the curation; the simple-shape 3D is explicitly 'eventually'). Verified the 50-slot math + RON parse with a test rather than eyeballing 100 plantings.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.381.0 — SAVE/LOAD LIFECYCLE (homes increment 3): offline progress now PERSISTS between sessions. Operator: 'keep developing offline.' A read-only Explore agent mapped the lifecycle; KEY FACTS: (a) the game persisted NOTHING between sessions before this (persistence::{save_world,load_world,auto_save} were wired ONLY in tests; entering the 3D world calls a different EngineState::load_world that regenerates the homestead fresh); (b) system_runner.tick at lib.rs:1554 is UNGATED -- systems run every frame in the menu-driven loops AND in 3D -- so the ECS player entity (spawned once at startup, lib.rs:985-996, with Inventory/PlayerSkills/Health/Vitals/etc.) is authoritative ALWAYS; (c) inventory + skills have clean get/set, while health/position need glam + vitals/crops/quests are NOT in WorldSave. BUILT a new src/save_load.rs: extract_world_save (player INVENTORY + SKILLS -> WorldSave), apply_save_to_world (WorldSave -> player, offline-kind-guarded), load/save_active_home (a single offline_home.json in saves_dir; progressive disclosure = one home), maybe_periodic_save (self-throttling static-timer, every 120s). WIRED 4 hooks in lib.rs: pub mod save_load; APPLY at STARTUP right after the player spawns (NOT on 3D-enter -- because the player is authoritative from startup, and applying-at-startup also makes the exit-save SAFE: the player always carries the loaded state, so closing without playing round-trips the save instead of overwriting it empty); SAVE on WindowEvent::CloseRequested (graceful close); PERIODIC save after the tick (robust to in-app-quit/crash where CloseRequested would not fire). SCOPE = inventory + skills ONLY (the actual progress, lowest coupling -- no Transform/glam); DEFERRED health/position/game_time (TimeSystem owns its clock)/vitals/crops/quests, so reload = 'wake rested at home with your stuff + skills intact'. 2 new tests (extract->wipe->apply round-trips inventory+skills; apply ignores non-offline kind) PASS + persistence round-trip. VERIFIED: cargo check clean (no warnings); 3 save_load/persistence tests pass; release build + emdash_lint pending. NEXT (offline): extend the save to health/position/game_time (game_time needs a TimeSystem set_time), then vitals + crops + quests (each needs a new WorldSave field + a no-double-spawn guard for crops); then the self-sufficiency SCORE data layer once the operator reacts to the variables doc.
recent_decisions.why: The operator said keep developing offline, and the real prize was that the game persisted nothing between sessions, so wiring save/load is genuine new value (not polish). Investigating first was essential and paid off: it revealed the tick is ungated (so the ECS player is authoritative from startup, which is why apply-at-startup is correct, not the agent's suggested 3D-enter hook) and it surfaced the data-loss trap (extract-on-close would overwrite a good save with empty if the player never played) -- solved elegantly by apply-at-startup, which makes the player always carry the loaded state so close-save round-trips. Scoped to inventory+skills only to keep this correctness-sensitive first slice clean and low-coupling (no glam, no schema changes), with the riskier fields (crops need a no-double-spawn guard, game_time needs TimeSystem cooperation) deferred to their own careful increments. Added a periodic save because the operator may quit via the in-app menu (not the window X), and losing a session's progress would be a bad first impression. Tested the core round-trip + the kind-guard rather than shipping unverified save logic.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.380.0 — SELF-SUFFICIENCY model doc + SAVE-WRAPPER model (homes increment 2). Operator asked '(1) what variables are there to consider for homestead self-sufficiency? (2) go ahead and start the save wrapper.' BOTH delivered. (1) NEW docs/design/self-sufficiency.md: a thorough breakdown of the variables, organized as coupled LOOPS (energy / water / food / waste-nutrients / air / thermal-envelope / materials-maintenance), each with supply/demand/storage/losses/time-window, PLUS the cross-cutting gates (location+climate = the single biggest driver, scale/occupancy, autonomy margin, time horizon, loop COUPLING as a network, labor, redundancy). Key honest insights captured: energy is Wh-over-time + storage NOT nameplate watts (the exact reason I refused to ship a naive ratio); self-sufficiency = the MINIMUM across loops (Liebig's limiting factor); the loops are a coupled graph; everything is gated by place + household size. Proposed a buildable SCORE model: per-loop min(1, supply_over_time/demand_over_time) + an autonomy-days number + overall = the limiting loop, gated by location+scale, same metric sim/real (sensors feed it in Real mode). Listed the data we'd add (an editable component-output table: solar W + sun-hours, tank L, etc.) and what's deferred (full network sim, weather modeling). (2) SAVE-WRAPPER MODEL: KEY FINDING from reading the code -- the game has NO working save/load lifecycle today: persistence::{save_world,load_world,auto_save} are defined but wired ONLY in tests; entering the 3D world calls a DIFFERENT EngineState::load_world that REGENERATES the homestead fresh (src/ship/fibonacci::generate_homestead) and never applies a saved state. So 'the save wrapper' splits: the MODEL is mine to build now, the LIFECYCLE (Play loads a home, progress persists) is a careful multi-system increment that the game lacks entirely and deserves its own focus. Built the MODEL: WorldSave gains `kind` (default 'offline') + `design` (default 'fibonacci') via #[serde(default=...)] so pre-v0.380 saves load unchanged; a WorldSave::new_offline(name, design) constructor; 3 tests (new_offline defaults, round-trip preserves kind/design, legacy-JSON-without-the-fields defaults) ALL PASS. The Home page now frames the design as 'your offline home' (a small identity card: kind offline / you own the save / saving+Play-entry is next). Progressive disclosure (#7): one home, no manager, until there's a reason for more. VERIFIED: cargo check clean; 4 persistence lib tests pass; release build + emdash_lint pending. NEXT (offline): increment 3 = the SAVE/LOAD LIFECYCLE -- extract live game state (inventory/skills/game_time/crops/constructions) <-> WorldSave, load the active offline home on world-enter, auto-save on exit/interval (the game currently persists NOTHING between sessions except config/identity, so this is real new value). Then the self-sufficiency SCORE data layer (improvement #4) once the operator reacts to the variables doc + the editable component-output numbers.
recent_decisions.why: The operator asked the self-sufficiency question genuinely (he's unsure how he thinks about it), so the most useful thing was a thorough, organized variable breakdown he can react to -- persisted to a design doc because it's durable knowledge that shapes the score feature + the whole real-buildable north star, not a throwaway reply. I led with the honest engineering truth (energy is Wh+storage not nameplate; Liebig's minimum) because correctness matters more than a flashy number, and proposed a concrete buildable model so we're not stuck in abstraction. For the save wrapper, reading the code first paid off: the game has no save/load at all, so I scoped honestly -- ship the clean low-risk MODEL now (kind+design + backward-compat + tests), and treat the lifecycle as its own careful increment rather than rushing a multi-system save/load into an already-large turn (correctness not sacrificed for speed). Used #[serde(default)] so this is a zero-migration change. Kept the Home page to a single-home framing (progressive disclosure, improvement #7) rather than building a multi-home manager nobody needs offline yet.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.379.0 — HOMES feature, increment 1: the offline HOME page (Fibonacci homestead Design browser). Operator greenlit building the homes + character model AND asked me to improve the ideas; he then said 'I like your suggestions, let's keep developing offline. We'll worry about multiplayer after offline singleplayer works.' So: OFFLINE-FIRST, multiplayer deferred. An Explore agent mapped the existing systems and found the KEY thing: the Fibonacci homestead ALREADY EXISTS as rich data (data/blueprints/fibonacci_homestead.ron, 399 lines, 13 rooms F1->F233, each with materials [(item_id,qty)] + power_watts + water_liters_per_day + tier + purpose + environment_notes + 4 tiers + build_order + scaling_notes). So I did NOT author a blueprint, I SURFACED the existing one. BUILT: (a) new structs HomesteadDesign/DesignRoom/Size3/RoomRequirements/DesignTier + load_homestead_design (ron::from_str, like src/ship/fibonacci.rs) in src/gui/mod.rs; GuiState.homestead_design loaded at startup. (b) new GuiPage::Homes + pages/homes.rs: a read-only browser with a SCALE selector (Solo F1-8 / Family F1-13 / Community F1-55 / Colony all, from scaling_notes), and for the chosen scale shows: total power + water DEMAND, the aggregated BILL OF MATERIALS (parts list, sorted by qty = improvement #3), a SELF-SUFFICIENCY summary (counts of solar/wind/generator+battery/water-systems/greenhouse+composter components that close the loops = improvement #4, partial), and the rooms grouped by tier (collapsible, each with footprint/power/water/notes/parts). (c) a 'Home' top-level nav tab (paint_house icon) by Profile (you -> your home); nav is now 14. IMPROVEMENTS the operator approved ('I like your suggestions'), recorded as the design direction: #1 base character = the EXISTING crypto identity/signed profile (don't invent a second you); #2 home 'kind' reframed as WHO OWNS THE TRUTH (you=offline / server / sensors=real); #3 blueprint carries its BOM from day one (DONE in this view); #4 closure/self-sufficiency SCORE (started: demand totals + self-sufficiency kit; the exact output-vs-demand score needs per-component generation-capacity data = next layer); #5 designs are forkable/signed/CC0 commons; #6 Real home = digital twin (sim+real fused, predictive); #7 progressive disclosure (one home/character until you make a second). VERIFIED: cargo check clean (no warnings); release build + emdash_lint pending. NEXT (offline): increment 2 = the Home SAVE-WRAPPER (WorldSave gains kind=Offline + design ref; a minimal home list; Play enters the selected home) + ground the base character in the existing identity (#1); then the closure SCORE data layer (#4) + real-BOM (#5 sharing, real part numbers). All multiplayer/server/Real-home/character-augmentation work stays deferred until offline singleplayer is solid.
recent_decisions.why: The operator made the call (build it, offline-first) and explicitly liked the improvements, so I folded the confident ones into a real first increment rather than just talking. The Explore survey changed the plan for the better: the Fibonacci homestead is already rich data, so the highest-value, lowest-risk first slice was to SURFACE it as a browsable Design with its BOM + demand + self-sufficiency summary, which is the real-world-bridge north star made visible and needs no save-format migration. I deliberately did NOT build the save-wrapper or character-augmentation yet: the wrapper is a riskier save-format change better done as its own increment, and the per-server/character-augmentation + shared/locked-stash are gated on multiplayer, which the operator just deferred. The closure SCORE is shown as demand + self-sufficiency-kit for now because a true output-vs-demand score needs per-component generation data that does not exist yet (a clean next data layer, and arguably the operator's to shape since it encodes his homestead's real generation capacity).

recent_decisions.decision: v0.378.0 — BIG NAV + PROFILE + LIBRARY batch (operator, from a live screenshot). Eleven asks, all shipped in one Rust increment: (1) NAV REORG (escape_menu.rs): Play moved ALL THE WAY LEFT (leftmost tab); Real RENAMED to 'Profile' (label only; still GuiPage::Real internally); Tasks promoted to a top-level tab to the RIGHT of Quests; Map promoted to a top-level tab (GuiPage::Maps -> Cosmos, paint_pin icon) closing the game cluster. New nav (13): Play, Humanity, Chat, Studio, Profile, Quests, Tasks, Inventory, Crafting, Map, Platform, Library, Settings. (2) real.rs: REMOVED the Possessions/Tasks/Map sections (now their own top-level tabs) + the Streaming section (moved to Studio); the Profile sidebar now holds only the Profile sections + Wallet + Market; section_nav title 'Real'->'Profile'; trimmed imports + match arms. (3) PROFILE SELECTOR added at the top of the Profile sidebar (operator: 'add a profile selector. I only want one profile') — a real ComboBox with one option 'Base (your real self)' + a one-line explainer; page-local thread_local; the slice is the seam per-server augmented versions slot into later. (4) STREAMING -> STUDIO: the public streaming fields (profile_streaming_url + profile_streaming_live) now render in Studio's right panel as a 'Your Public Channel' group; the old Profile streaming section is unreachable (harmless, like the other dead renderers). (5) CHAT/STUDIO PERSIST-LOAD: documented (no code needed) — stream state lives in GuiState.studio (not the draw fn) and chat runs on a background WS thread, so navigating away never resets/kills them; studio.rs header now states the architectural rule that when REAL streaming/capture lands its pump MUST run in the engine loop, not gated on the active page. (6) LIBRARY CARDS (library.rs): the External Resources cards are now SELF-CONTAINED — a 'Load website' button at the TOP of each card (operator: 'move the load website to the top of each card') + ALL the data crammed in below it (title/tag/description/url). Removed the click-to-detail gate (Sel::Detail deleted); the explicit Load button is the only thing that launches the browser, preserving the no-auto-launch intent. (7) CHARACTER MODEL adopted + documented (docs/design/homes-as-profiles.md new 'Characters' section): ONE base character = your real self (identity/biography typed ONCE, owned by you); servers store an AUGMENTED version (look/gear) while the base stays shared so you look consistent across servers; inventory can be SHARED across a character-set (Diablo II offline/open-bnet) or LOCKED locally+on-servers (closed/ladder); a server can force starter-gear-only new characters. The two axes compose: a CHARACTER (shared base + per-server augmentation) enters a HOME (offline/server/real). VERIFIED: cargo check clean (no warnings); release build + emdash_lint pending (in flight). NOTE: the character data-model (multi-character saves, shared/locked stash) is NOT built — captured as design with open forks, same posture as the home model.
recent_decisions.why: A direct operator batch from playing the live build — every item was an explicit instruction, so I executed them all in one coherent Rust increment (nav + real.rs + studio.rs + library.rs) rather than dribbling. The nav promotions (Inventory was already top-level; now Tasks/Map join, Play leads, Real->Profile) continue dissolving the old Real/Play fold into direct top-level access, which is the operator's clear direction. Kept the riskier/foundational pieces as documentation, not speculative code: the persist-load requirement is ALREADY satisfied by the immediate-mode + GuiState architecture (nothing is torn down on navigation; the stream is a mock flag that persists), so I documented the guarantee + the forward rule instead of building a fix for a non-bug; and the character model (base + per-server augmentation + shared/locked inventory) is a real save-architecture that, like the home model, has open forks that are the operator's call, so I captured it in the design doc with my leanings rather than guessing the schema. The profile selector is the one small UI seam I DID build (a working one-option ComboBox) so the concept is visible and extensible. 13 nav tabs is over my old <=10 note, but the operator is explicitly promoting these to top-level and they fit the horizontal bar at his resolution.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.377.0 — PLAY button (FPS-mode entry) + HOMES-AS-PROFILES direction adopted. (1) PLAY BUTTON shipped (operator: 'add a dedicated button for the FPS game part ... right now it's just I press Esc to bring up the FPS game mode, there's no indicator in the menu ... Click Play to start FPS game mode'). Mechanism: FPS mode IS GuiPage::None (the nav bar + pages are hidden for None/MainMenu per lib.rs ~3650; the post-egui reconcile ~3929 grabs the cursor when a frame ends on None). So 'Play' is a nav button whose page = GuiPage::None — clicking it hides the chrome and grabs the mouse for look, exactly like pressing Esc from a page. Added paint_play (right-pointing filled triangle) in icons.rs + routed P::None -> paint_play; placed Play to LEAD the game-colored cluster (Play · Quests · Inventory · Crafting); and tweaked the generic nav click handler so entering None records last_page = the page you came from (Esc out of the world returns there, not to a stale last_page). Nav is now 11: Humanity, Chat, Studio, Real, Play, Quests, Inventory, Crafting, Platform, Library, Settings (slightly over the <=10 guide, acceptable since Play is an action that hides the nav). (2) HOMES-AS-PROFILES MODEL ADOPTED (operator's bigger proposal, captured in NEW docs/design/homes-as-profiles.md): each HOME is a SAVE PROFILE rendered by ONE shared homestead interface, differing only by a 'kind' toggle — Offline (local sim; the FIRST offline home = the 100%-self-sustaining Fibonacci design = the gamified blueprint others copy), Server (multiplayer on a relay), or Real (bound to real monitoring/automation hardware; the north-star control layer, built LAST). Homes are built from DESIGNS (blueprints; first = Fibonacci spiral-deck homestead). This DISSOLVES the Real/Play firewall: real-vs-game becomes a property of the home you're in, not a top-level page split — the same UI, different connection. Play (this increment) enters the world for the SELECTED home. The doc maps it to existing code (saves/persistence, the Place spine, ship/RON layouts + the Fibonacci generator, relay multiplayer, device-mesh) and sequences it: Play DONE -> home model+select -> offline Fibonacci design -> server homes -> parts-list-from-design -> real homes LAST. VERIFIED: cargo check clean; release build + emdash_lint pending (in flight). NOTE: did NOT build the save-model changes — that is a real architecture needing operator alignment on the open forks in the doc (can a home change kind? design-vs-home split? where home-select lives? what a Real home shows); recorded them rather than guessing.
recent_decisions.why: The Play button is a small, concrete, decided win that fixes a real discoverability gap (no on-screen way into FPS mode) and reuses the existing None=FPS mechanism, so it was pure UI wiring — shipped immediately. The homes-as-profiles model is the operator's synthesis and it is genuinely better than the Real/Play page split we'd been circling: it makes the real-world-bridge north star concrete (design once -> game level + parts list + real dashboard, one interface) and unifies offline/server/real under one homestead UI. Captured it durably in its own design doc the moment it was established (cross-session-persistence rule) with the model, the code mapping, and the open forks — but did NOT speculatively build the save-system rework, because the forks are the operator's architecture call and guessing a big persistence change wrong would waste real work. Built the decided part, documented the rest, kept moving.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.376.0 — NAV REORG step 1: INVENTORY + CRAFTING promoted to top-level tabs (operator: 'the inventory page definitely need to become a top level page' + 'let's have the crafting page its own page for now at least' + 'add crafting to the main menu'). escape_menu.rs: the one-item 'Play' fold (which held only Crafting after Studio was promoted in v0.374) is REPLACED by two direct top-level tabs — Inventory and Crafting (both nav_sim color; icons already existed: paint_box for Inventory, paint_hammer for Crafting). Nav is now Humanity, Chat, Studio, Real, Quests, Inventory, Crafting, Platform, Library, Settings (10 tabs, at the <=10 rule). DELIBERATELY MINIMAL/LOW-RISK: GuiPage::Play + play::draw + its dispatch arm are LEFT as unreachable dead code (cleanup pass later, same list as resources::draw/onboarding::draw); the Real page KEEPS its 'Possessions' shortcut to Inventory for now (removing it touches the Real page the operator is still redesigning). The internal inventory layout (the 2-vs-3-panel tree->contents->detail rebuild) was NOT touched — that is the genuine design fork the operator is actively deciding. My recommendation given to him: 3-pane file-manager style (place/container tree -> contents list -> item detail) via a SHARED widget that Crafting reuses (Have vs Make = same layout, different source); garden plugs in as a container node; market/profile stay separate pages. VERIFIED: cargo check clean, release build, emdash_lint 2/2. NEXT: the inventory-internals rebuild (gated on the operator's panel call) + the full Real/Play removal + Home/Profile grouping (still being designed with him).
recent_decisions.why: These two promotions are the DECIDED, unambiguous, reversible part of the nav reorg, so I shipped them immediately rather than waiting on the still-open design questions (per the no-checkpoints directive: build the decided part, keep moving). Kept it nav-only — no page rewrites, no Real surgery, dead Play left in place — so the increment is low-risk and isolated; the contested inventory-internals redesign stays a live conversation (he explicitly asked my thoughts and is unsure on 2 vs 3 panels) so I did not pre-build a big speculative rebuild he might redirect. Icons were already wired (paint_box/paint_hammer), so the promotion was a pure nav swap.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.375.0 — LIBRARY v4: external resources reimagined for scale + an in-app detail gate (operator). The websites are no longer a nested category tree; they are now ONE 'External Resources' view (selected from the left rail) showing every website as a full-width card in a single scrolling column (1 column, infinite rows), with a SEARCH box + clickable TAG-FILTER chips (tags are the catalog categories; built to scale to thousands). The DOCS (HumanityOS) stay as the collapsible nested tree. KEY CHANGE the operator wanted: clicking a website no longer launches the external browser immediately; it opens an in-app DETAIL page (the site profile: title, tag, description, url) with a 'Load website' button, so the person chooses to leave. (An in-app browser is the noted future enhancement; for now Load website opens the system browser via ctx.open_url.) library.rs rewritten with a 3-state right pane (Doc | Resources | Detail) in a thread_local. No data/model change: websites are flattened from the existing Tools-and-Websites Link entries, tagged by their category. VERIFIED: cargo check clean, release build, emdash_lint. NEXT: the nav reorg, the operator decided INVENTORY must be a top-level page and CRAFTING its own page 'for now', and is pondering the inventory page design (he asked my thoughts: what combines elegantly into inventory? 2 or 3 panels? he wants the nested-tree-left + details-right pattern). The full Real/Play removal + Home/Profile grouping is still being designed with him.
recent_decisions.why: Two concrete Library asks: (1) the websites could grow to thousands, so a nested-category tree does not scale, a flat searchable + tag-filterable card list does; (2) clicking a link should not immediately launch the browser, it should open an in-app detail page first (a gate + context) with an explicit 'Load website'. Built both, and reused the existing library data (flattened the website links, category = tag) so there was no data migration. Kept the docs as the tree (few docs; the tree is fine there); only the websites needed the reimagining. Deferred the inventory/nav reorg because the operator is still actively designing it (panels, what combines) and asked for my thoughts first.

recent_decisions.decision: DIRECTION SHIFT + REAL-WORLD-BRIDGE NORTH STAR (operator, design discussion). (1) REMOVE the Real/Play distinction: make the survival/production game systems (garden, inventory, crafting, market, etc.) first-class top-level features that just WORK, not wrapped in a real-vs-sim split. Add Crafting to the main menu (top-level). (2) DEFER real-life integration to LAST, but keep it as a design-shaping north star throughout. THE NORTH STAR: every in-game system (garden, water, energy, crafting) should be designed to MAP to a real-world system a person can actually build; that mapping generates a PARTS LIST of everything anyone needs to 3D-print / buy / trade to build these systems at their own home; and LAST, the app gains a way to AUTOMATE + MONITOR those real systems (the app becomes the control/monitoring layer for your real homestead, e.g. real aeroponics at the operator's house). Design the game systems NOW as real buildable systems, not just game abstractions, so this is achievable later. (3) This SUPERSEDES the earlier 'Real vs Sim = separate pages, never a toggle' firewall framing FOR NOW: the operator is collapsing it to build the systems first; any real/sim separation + the real-life layer return LAST. (4) REORG PENDING: the operator is unsatisfied with the cramped Real page (Profile sections + Inventory/Wallet/Tasks/Map/Market all crammed in one) and explicitly asked for my thoughts on how to reorganize. I am proposing a homestead-centered IA, Profile (you) | Home (your homestead: possessions/garden/market/wallet/tasks/map) | Crafting (make/3D-print), removing Real + Play, and asking him to pick the granularity before I build.
recent_decisions.why: The operator is reprioritizing: get the survival/production systems working as first-class top-level features now, drop the real/sim wrapper that added friction, and treat the real-life-automation layer as the LAST step, but one that shapes every system's design (each must map to a real buildable thing + a parts list). Recorded the real-world bridge as a durable north star because he explicitly said to 'keep it in mind while we develop'; it is the project's deepest purpose (teach + equip real self-sufficiency, then automate it) and must survive a compaction. Asked before reorganizing because he said he is unsure how and asked for thoughts: a genuine information-architecture fork in his design domain, where guessing a big nav rebuild wrong would waste real work.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.374.0 — five structural changes (operator batch). (1) RESOURCES RETIRED into the Library: removed the Resources section from the Humanity sidebar (the Library's Tools and Websites section already shows the same catalog.json links), and the 2 quest steps that said 'Check the Resources page' now say 'the Library'. (2) LIBRARY top-level sections are now COLLAPSIBLE (each section, HumanityOS and Tools and Websites, is a CollapsingHeader, not just a label). (3) LIBRARY SEARCH for scale: a filter box at the top filters every entry by title, url, and description (the operator expects the websites list could reach 1000+, so search plus collapse is the navigability answer); doc bodies are not searched, titles only. (4) QUESTS promoted to its OWN TOP-LEVEL TAB (operator: 'add a top level quests page for now') via a thin pages/quests.rs that renders the shared onboarding::draw_quests; new GuiPage::Quests + a nav tab (compass icon) + lib.rs dispatch; removed the Real > Quests section added in v0.373. (5) STUDIO promoted to its own TOP-LEVEL TAB right of Chat (operator: 'move the studio page to the top level main menu to the right of the chat button'); GuiPage::Studio already existed and dispatched, so this added the nav tab (after Chat, palette icon) and removed the Studio section from Play (Play now folds in Crafting only). Nav is now Humanity, Chat, Studio, Real, Quests, Play, Platform, Library, Settings (9 tabs, under the <=10 rule). VERIFIED: cargo check clean (no exhaustive-match break for the new GuiPage::Quests), release build, emdash_lint. DEAD CODE (left in place, flagged for one cleanup pass): resources::draw joins profile::draw_quests and onboarding::draw on the retire-and-delete list.
recent_decisions.why: All five were direct operator asks. Retiring Resources removes the duplication the Library introduced, one home for docs and links. Collapsible sections plus a search filter are the 'reimagine the websites for scale' answer: search is what makes a 1000-entry directory usable, and collapsing lets you hide what you are not browsing. Quests and Studio both go top-level because the operator wants them one click away (Studio for livestreaming, Quests as the self-sufficiency path); reused the existing renderers (onboarding::draw_quests, studio::draw, the GuiPage::Studio dispatch) so the promotions were nav-only, no rewrites. Left the now-dead page renderers in place (pub, so no warnings) for a single cleanup pass rather than scattered deletion surgery mid-batch.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.373.0 — QUESTS UNIFIED into a single learn-by-doing surface under Real (operator: 'tie quests + learn-by-doing together... we don't want game: build tower / real: build tower, that is duplicated... include an onboarding quest chain, onboarding category at the top'; chose 'own section under Real' via AskUserQuestion). BEFORE: two quest surfaces, the onboarding page's learn-by-doing chains (data/onboarding/quests.json, manual checklists) AND the Profile in-game Quests panel (XP, auto-tracked). NOW: ONE 'Quests' section in the Real tab (LIFE group) that renders the learn-by-doing chains via onboarding::draw_quests (made pub and reused), with First Steps (the onboarding chain) first because it is first in quests.json. The Profile 'Quests' sub-section was removed from the Real nav (the section id 'quests' now renders learn-by-doing, not the game panel), and the standalone onboarding page is RETIRED: its nav item is gone from the Humanity sidebar and the Mission Dashboard 'Get oriented' CTA now jumps to Real > Quests (active_page = Real, active_real_section = 'quests'). No game/real duplication: the sim is practice woven into the chain STEPS (e.g. 'Try the farming simulation' then 'Plan your first tower'), not a separate quest row. VERIFIED: cargo check clean. DEAD CODE (left in place, pub so no warnings, flagged for a cleanup pass): profile::draw_quests (the old game-quest panel) and onboarding::draw plus its hero/concepts/core-pages/cta (the rest of the retired page) are now unreachable.
recent_decisions.why: The operator's refined design: quests and learn-by-doing are the same thing (the sim is safe practice, the real task is the goal), so showing 'game: X' and 'real: X' as two rows is ugly duplication. The learn-by-doing chains already model this (sim + real steps in one chain), so the cleanest unification was to make those chains THE quests, reuse the existing renderer, and remove the two redundant surfaces (the Profile game panel and the standalone onboarding page, whose hero/concepts now overlap the Mission Dashboard). Put it under Real (his pick) in the LIFE group; First Steps is the onboarding chain, already at the top. Left the now-dead renderers in place (pub, no warnings) rather than do deletion surgery mid-merge, and flagged a cleanup pass.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.372.0 — LIBRARY v2: nested sections + external websites/tools (operator: 'expand the library to also include the websites/third party software. The current list could be nested into a parent category like HumanityOS'). The Library is now a 3-level tree (section -> category -> entry). load_library builds TWO sections: 'HumanityOS' (the Accord + companion docs, from data/library/) and 'Tools and Websites' (the curated external links, read from the SAME data/resources/catalog.json the Resources page uses, so the link data has ONE source, no duplication). New data model in src/gui/mod.rs: LibrarySection { categories } / LibraryCategory { entries } / LibraryEntry { title, kind: Doc(markdown) | Link { url, desc } }. library.rs renders section headers + collapsing categories + entries: Doc entries are selectable and render in the right pane via the shared widgets::markdown reader; Link entries are hyperlinks that open the browser (description on hover). Auto-selects the first doc so the pane is never blank. VERIFIED: cargo check clean (enum + matches! + hyperlink_to + borrows + the Vec<LibrarySection> type change all good), release build, emdash_lint. OPEN: the Library's 'Tools and Websites' now overlaps the Resources page (both read catalog.json) - candidate to consolidate (retire the Resources section, or keep both views) - operator's call. NEXT: the QUESTS rework (unify learn-by-doing, no game/real duplication, onboarding chain pinned to the top).
recent_decisions.why: The operator wanted the Library to be the one knowledge hub: the HumanityOS docs nested under their own parent, plus the external tools/websites we recommend. Built it 3-level (section/category/entry) and, crucially, sourced the web links from the existing resources catalog rather than copying them, so there is a single source of truth shown in both the Resources page and the Library. Links open externally (hyperlink), docs render inline, kept fully data-driven (add a doc via data/library/, a link via the catalog). Flagged the now-redundant Resources page as a consolidation question for the operator rather than unilaterally retiring it.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.371.0 — DEDUPE (the tracked follow-up task from the v0.370 Library). The minimal markdown reader (render_markdown + strip_md), which had been copied into both pages/humanity.rs (the Accord viewer modal) and pages/library.rs (the Library doc pane), is now a single shared module src/gui/widgets/markdown.rs (pub render_markdown + strip_md). Both call sites use it (humanity via widgets::markdown::, library via a `use crate::gui::widgets::markdown;`), the two local copies are deleted, and widgets/mod.rs gains `pub mod markdown;`. Byte-identical rendering (the exact same code moved, same U+00B7 bullet glyph). VERIFIED: cargo check --features native clean (no orphaned imports), emdash_lint passes. NOTE on versioning: bumped MINOR per the SOP (the rule is 'Rust code changed -> 0.X.0'; the spawn_task prompt said 'patch' but that was an imprecise suggestion, and the whole session has used minor for every .rs change).
recent_decisions.why: A pure, behavior-preserving refactor that closes the duplication I deliberately deferred (as a spawned task) while shipping the Library, so there is one markdown reader with two consumers instead of two copies that could drift apart. Did it as its own small increment exactly as flagged, keeping the Library ship focused and this cleanup isolated and easy to verify.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.370.0 — LIBRARY shipped as a new top-level tab (operator-chosen direction). A data-driven in-app reader for the project's reference docs. scripts/build-library.js copies a curated set (the Humanity Accord + 16 companion docs from docs/accord/) into data/library/ and generates index.json with 5 nested categories (The Accord / Your Rights and Consent / How We Decide / Safety and Care / Reference). load_library reads the manifest + markdown files at startup into GuiState.library. pages/library.rs renders a nested CollapsingHeader category tree on the left and the selected doc on the right via a minimal markdown reader. Wired end to end: GuiPage::Library variant, escape_menu nav tab (reuses the nav_reality color + the existing paint_scroll icon), lib.rs startup loader + dispatch, pages/mod. Data-driven per infinite-of-X: add a doc by dropping a file in data/library/ and a manifest line, no Rust. VERIFIED: cargo check clean (no exhaustive-match breaks, CollapsingHeader + structs fine), release build, emdash_lint. KNOWN DEBT (flagged as a separate task): render_markdown/strip_md are duplicated in library.rs and humanity.rs; dedupe into a shared widgets::markdown module. NOTE: the Accord + companion docs keep their own em-dashes because they are the operator's formal constitution text, not our marketing copy; the em-dash ban targets AI-tell UI copy, not quoted human-authored documents. NEXT: the QUESTS rework (one page, two kinds, retire the onboarding page).
recent_decisions.why: Operator chose a top-level Library (prominence over my section recommendation). Built it data-driven per infinite-of-X, a build script curates docs/accord/ into data/library/ plus a manifest the app loads, reusing the existing read_data_json + load_* + paint_scroll conventions so it slots cleanly into the app. The nested CollapsingHeader category tree mirrors the inventory feel the operator referenced ('kinda like the inventory'). Shipped with the render_markdown duplication tracked as a follow-up task rather than doing risky surgery on the just-shipped humanity.rs viewer mid-build, keeping this large multi-file increment focused and low-risk.
