Left the Accord's em-dashes intact because it is a referenced, human-authored constitution with a signed canonical text, not the app's own copy.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.369.0 — in-app Humanity Accord viewer + an 'Early days, built in the open' section, and LOCKED direction on two bigger asks. (1) ACCORD IN-APP (operator: 'a button that takes users directly to the Humanity Accord inside the app'): the Accord is embedded via include_str! from docs/accord/humanity_accord.md (one stable CC0 doc, no network, no data-file drift, single source of truth), shown in a lightweight in-app doc viewer, a thread_local DocViewer modal plus a minimal markdown reader (headings, bullets, horizontal rules, paragraphs; inline emphasis markers stripped), opened by a 'Read the Humanity Accord' button on the dashboard's 'Bound by a constitution' row. The viewer (render_doc_modal / render_markdown in humanity.rs) is deliberately built to be REUSED by the coming Library. (2) EARLY-ACCESS HONESTY (operator: 'a dedicated thing that explicitly states the app is early dev, not all features implemented or working... building in public... no way you or I can test everything... bugs from a larger user base'): added an 'Early days, built in the open' card high on the dashboard, right after the personal why, so it frames the capability claims that follow. It states plainly that the app is early, some features are half-built / rough / will break, that anything not ready is flagged 'in progress', and that real users hitting bugs is how building-in-public works, an invitation to help, not an apology. VERIFIED: cargo check clean, release build clean, emdash_lint passes. (3) DIRECTION LOCKED via AskUserQuestion on the two bigger proposals (he framed them as 'maybe / what if'): LIBRARY = a NEW TOP-LEVEL TAB (operator chose prominence over my 'section under Humanity' recommendation) containing all the docs in a nested tree like the inventory; QUESTS = ONE PAGE, TWO KINDS, a single Quests page holding BOTH gameplay quests (auto-track + XP) AND learn-by-doing chains (manual check, deeper per-method, e.g. a chain for each way to collect / purify / store water), and RETIRE the standalone onboarding page since the Mission Dashboard now covers that ground. THESE ARE THE NEXT TWO INCREMENTS.
recent_decisions.why: The Accord button and early-access section were clear operator directives, shipped now. The Accord uses include_str! (the simplest robust path for one stable doc: offline, single source of truth) behind a REUSABLE viewer so the Library inherits it instead of a throwaway. The early-access section is squarely on-brand (the anti-fraud, honest-by-default ethos) and reframes bugs as participation, which fits building-in-public and the larger-user-base reality the operator named. The Library and quest-rework were posed as open questions, i.e. genuine design forks his intuition should resolve, so I asked one focused question (not a gate) and he chose: a top-level Library (prominence) and a unified two-kinds Quests page, which cleanly handles the model mismatch between reflective onboarding checklists and XP-granting gameplay objectives and lets the onboarding page retire. Building those next, Library first.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.368.0 — mined the docs to deepen the Mission Dashboard pitch so it shows the platform helps in EVERY scenario (operator: 'find anything else in the docs to elaborate on the why is it built this way and what it protects... make sure what we have or are planning actually helps them in all scenarios. The better the pitch the better.'). RESEARCH: read the Humanity Accord directly (docs/accord/humanity_accord.md, a CC0 model constitution: dignity, individual agency, transparency Art 6, power-accountability Art 7, surveillance limits Art 18, AI-as-participant Art 16, freedom from domination, post-scarcity Art 36) and ran two parallel Explore agents over docs/ (a protections inventory and a resilience/scenarios inventory, both flagging SHIPPED vs PLANNED). SYNTHESIS into humanity.rs draw_mission_dashboard: (1) ENRICHED 'Why it is built this way' with a 'Built to be remade by anyone' row (data-driven / infinite-of-X: every plant, recipe, quest, and price is a plain data file; a teacher in a remote village adds local crops and lessons with no code; the tool bends to the place). (2) ENRICHED 'What it protects' from 4 to 6 rows and sharpened them: Privacy became 'Privacy, even from us' (post-quantum + zero-knowledge: the server holds only unreadable ciphertext, even we cannot read your DMs, a warrant cannot compel what does not exist); ADDED 'You can never be locked out' (self-custody key + seed phrase + Shamir social recovery via trusted friends); ADDED the keystone 'Bound by a constitution, not a promise' (the Humanity Accord puts dignity/consent/transparency/non-domination above any operator, company, or government, and anyone can adopt or enforce it); sharpened governance (trust-weighted voting capped against domination, AI take part but cannot vote). (3) NEW SECTION 'Built for every situation' answering the all-scenarios ask directly, 6 rows: no internet (offline-first, everything on-device), server goes down (federation + no-home-server identity, run your own in minutes), lose your device (seed + social recovery, no email/fee), no money/papers/bank (no accounts, reputation + signed credentials replace credit and ID), any language or ability (multiple languages + high-contrast/colorblind/reduced-motion + keyboard-only + glossary + runs on old low-power hardware), disaster (off-site backups survive fire/flood/seizure; radio mesh flagged IN PROGRESS for honesty). HONESTY: every claim is SHIPPED except the LoRa radio mesh, which I explicitly marked '(in progress)' so the anti-fraud mission page never over-claims. VERIFIED: cargo check clean, release build clean, emdash_lint passes.
recent_decisions.why: The operator wants the pitch comprehensive and scenario-spanning, so I mined the actual docs (the Accord plus two agents) for the real, shipped capabilities a casual reader would never guess, post-quantum + zero-knowledge messaging, social recovery, no-home-server identity, the Accord as binding CC0 law, data-driven moddability, accessibility, off-site backups, and turned them into concrete copy. Enriched the two sections he named AND added a dedicated 'every situation' section because 'helps in all scenarios' is the explicit goal and earns its own clear answer (a refugee, a person off-grid, someone who lost their phone, someone with no bank, someone who speaks another language, someone in a disaster, each sees themselves). Stayed strictly honest about shipped vs the one in-progress item (the radio mesh) because faking capability on an anti-fraud page would destroy the premise. The Accord row is the strongest differentiator: the values are LAW, a public-domain constitution, not a promise we ask you to trust.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.367.0 — UNIVERSAL quests + a 'What it protects' (liberty) Mission Dashboard section (operator feedback, two notes). (1) QUESTS: the onboarding Water quest opened 'Wherever you live...' wait, it opened with '50,000 people in Lake Tahoe lost water access in 2025 because of wildfires', an unverifiable specific-event claim with no supporting link (a trust risk, same family as the em-dash tell) AND too US/place-specific. Operator: 'be careful naming places or specific events, especially if we do not provide links', and 'each quest needs to feel like a thing that applies everywhere', Sahara to tundra, Amazon to Philippine highlands. FIX: rewrote data/onboarding/quests.json (loaded by BOTH native onboarding.rs via load_quest_chains AND web onboarding.html via fetch, so one file fixes both). Removed the Lake Tahoe claim; dropped nation-specific stats (average American 80-100 gallons, US household 30 kWh), US-currency prices ($20/$200/$300), and US units; reframed every chain around universal principles plus climate-spanning options (rain AND dew/fog nets where rain is scarce; sun AND wind/hydro/human-power for low-sun regions; freezing AND hot-climate water storage). The now-rich Resources page carries the specific how-to links the quests defer to. (2) MISSION DASHBOARD 'What it protects': operator wanted more per-feature detail and the VALUES dimension, leading with free speech ('key to a healthy civilization; loss of free speech is a death sentence for a free and independent people', used verbatim). Added a card beside the v0.366 poverty mechanism: free speech (no central censor, no off switch, E2E + on-device identity + P2P), privacy (seal-on-device, nothing central to mine/leak/seize), ownership (your keys/words/tools + public domain, never rented back), and self-governance (trust-weighted voting capped against domination; AI take part openly but do not vote). The liberty half of the same promise: ending poverty is not enough if the result is a cage. VERIFIED: cargo check clean, release build clean, emdash_lint passes, quests.json valid + universal (no Lake Tahoe / nation stats).
recent_decisions.why: Both came from the operator reading the live build. (1) The Lake Tahoe line was exactly the kind of unverifiable, place-specific claim that undermines trust (and excludes the billions who do not live near it); universalizing the quests, deferring specifics to Resources links, makes each one read as the user's own regardless of climate or economy, which is the mission's reach. Fixed in the single data file both clients load, so native and web stay consistent for free. (2) The page argued the poverty case (v0.366) but said nothing about the freedoms a free people needs; the operator flagged free speech as paramount, so I added the liberty dimension in his own words and showed it is defended BY DESIGN (the architecture), not promised on top. This continues the app-first loop: more concrete detail, consistent voice, both the material and the civic stakes made plain.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.366.0 — Mission Dashboard MECHANISM section + Resources All-view + a populated catalog (operator feedback on the v0.365 live build, two notes). (1) The operator asked the Mission Dashboard to 'lean more into the way the onboarding page talks about how to actually end poverty and HumanityOS role in that, and why each system, the way it is designed and meant to be used, helps achieve these poverty ending goals.' ACTION: added a 'Why it is built this way' card to humanity.rs draw_mission_dashboard, a lead ('Poverty is forced dependence...') plus 7 system-to-purpose rows (guided quests + free-to-fail sim; encrypted chat no account; tasks + private notes; local maps; marketplace with trust scores; owned identity on a no-owner federated network; public domain forever), each mapping that system's design + intended use to the poverty-ending job it does. This is the concrete MECHANISM, echoing the Onboarding page, the heart of the pitch rather than only the aspiration. (2) The operator was unhappy with Resources: 'we used to have a design that showed tons of links, easy to see everything we link to, now it is barely populated. Maybe because we do not have an all category?' Root cause: the page showed ONE category at a time (2-3 links visible). FIX (resources.rs rewrite): ALL is now the default + first rail item, rendering EVERY category at once as a dense, wrapping grid of fixed-width link cards; the category rail was narrowed (160px) so content fills the width. selected_category semantics: 0 = All, 1..=N = category[i-1]. (3) Also genuinely populated data/resources/catalog.json: 6 to 10 categories, 14 to 37 real links, reordered to LEAD with the self-sufficiency essentials the mission is built on (Water, Energy, Food, Housing) then Health, Money & Benefits, Skills & Repair, Education, Legal, Technology, all high-confidence real free resources (gov + major orgs). Stays data-driven (infinite-of-X), so further curation is JSON, not code. VERIFIED: cargo check clean, release build clean, emdash_lint passes, catalog valid JSON with no em-dashes.
recent_decisions.why: Both notes came from the operator seeing v0.365 live. (1) The Mission Dashboard was missing the MECHANISM, and his exact ask was 'why each system, the way it is designed and meant to be used, helps achieve these poverty ending goals', so I mapped each REAL system to its poverty-ending design rationale, applying the Onboarding page's concreteness at the mission level (poverty = forced dependence; each part removes a slice of it). (2) The Resources category-at-a-time view hid the directory and read as empty; an All default + dense grid restores the 'see everything we link to' feel he missed, and expanding the catalog makes All actually show a rich directory aligned with the self-sufficiency mission (water/energy/food/shelter first). Kept curation in JSON so it never needs a code change. This is the operator's 'get it right in the app first' loop continuing: concrete, consistent, low-friction, both the individual and the civilization served.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.365.0 — native Humanity hero now names BOTH scopes (individual + civilization) as the page spine, plus a key STEER on the web-landing mirror. When I confirmed web/pages/index.html is the live public landing and re-read it, I found it is NOT a weak page to swap for the native mission dashboard: it is already a rich, mission-led marketing page (hero eyebrow 'End poverty. Unite humanity.', a full vision section on ending corruption/poverty/tyranny + colonies + CC0 + AI-as-citizens) led by the concrete 'free water/energy/food' conversion hook. The native mission page is a tighter, mission-first framing with LESS content, so naively replacing the landing would REDUCE it. I surfaced this via AskUserQuestion (lead-landing-with-mission / separate-page / full-replace). OPERATOR STEER (durable): 'get it right on the app FIRST' before touching the live landing; the experience must be CONSISTENT (first visit or millionth, web or app), predictable, and easy to follow for anyone of any age or state of mind; the INDIVIDUAL ('what I am doing') AND the CIVILIZATION ('what we are doing together') must both stay easily identifiable; every added PAGE is friction, so minimize page sprawl ('avoid becoming another dysfunctional US government'). NOT chosen: the separate /humanity page (adds friction) and touching the live landing now (premature). ACTION: sharpened the native Humanity hero (humanity.rs draw_mission_dashboard) to name BOTH scopes plainly right under the banner, the part everyone sees first: 'For you: the tools to feed, power, and provide for yourself and the people you love.' and 'For all of us: a fair way to end poverty together. No tyrants, no corporations, no catch. Free and public domain, for every human and every AI.' This makes the individual+civilization duality the page spine and matches the web landing's concrete-individual + civilization framing for consistency. Em-dash-free (the v0.364 ban). VERIFIED: release build clean, emdash_lint passes.
recent_decisions.why: The web-landing mirror was the logged end-goal, but executing it revealed the premise was off: the live landing is already excellent and richer than the native mission page, so 'replace it with the mission page' would be a downgrade. That is a genuine brand/taste fork on the public face, with new information the operator needed, so I asked rather than guessing (the directive's carve-out for a real direction fork). His answer reframed the effort: perfect the mission IN THE APP first, keep web and app consistent, keep both the individual and the civilization identifiable, and resist page sprawl. Those are durable principles for the rest of the carve and the eventual web mirror, so I recorded them. Then I made the one concrete, high-confidence, reversible improvement that embodies the steer: naming both scopes in the hero so the duality is instantly identifiable, consistent with the web's concrete framing. Kept it to the hero (the highest-impact instant-identification surface) rather than a speculative rewrite of his carefully-worded page, so he can react and steer the rest.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.364.0 — WHOLE-APP EM-DASH SWEEP + a regression lint (operator: 'go through the whole app and assess for em dashes', extending the v0.363 ban from the Humanity page to EVERYTHING). Swept every user-facing surface: (1) NATIVE src/gui = 121 string em-dashes across 13 files via scripts/strip-emdash-strings.js (a dotall string-literal regex catching single AND multi-line literals, leaving comments alone); fixed 2 cosmos placeholders the first pass mangled (orbits-fallback ', ' to '-'; the '— upcoming —' divider to 'Upcoming'). (2) WEB = 1048 across 94 files via scripts/strip-emdash-web.js (global, since HTML text is not quoted; spaced prose to ', ', the rest to '-'). REVERTED two files it touched that it must NOT: the VENDORED noble-pq.bundle.js and the GENERATED theme.css. REVIEWED the crypto-file diffs (crypto.js, pq.js) line-by-line: every change is a comment or a user-facing message, ZERO functional strings (no signed-preimage separator, KDF context, or compared value touched), and a global replace keeps intra-web comparisons consistent on both sides. The excluded crypto libs (pq-identity/pq-object/pq-relay-auth/canonical-cbor/bip39) retain em-dashes ONLY in comments (verified). (3) DATA = 328 across 31 files via scripts/strip-emdash-data.js (per-format: JSON/RON/TOML get ', '/'-' since commas are safe inside their quoted values; CSV gets ' - ' because a comma is a column DELIMITER and would corrupt the row). JSON validated 46/46 parse-OK. EXCLUDED intentionally: data/coordination/** (internal journal/sessions), *.md (dev docs), and announcements_archive.json + legacy_channel_content.json (HISTORICAL message records, rewriting them would edit the past). src/relay was NOT blanket-swept (643 occurrences, overwhelmingly backend comments/SQL, not user-facing, and risky). GUARD: tests/emdash_lint.rs is a cargo test that scans src/gui string literals via a string-vs-comment state machine (with a // emdash-exempt hatch) and FAILS the build on any new string em-dash; a self-verifying unit test proves the scanner flags strings AND ignores comments (not a no-op). It is the theme_token_lint / icon_glyph_lint analog for the em-dash ban. VERIFIED: release build clean (v0.364.0 exe), cargo test --release --test emdash_lint = 2/2 pass, grep confirms only the intended exclusions retain em-dashes on every surface. ENV NOTE: linking the DEBUG bin hits MSVC 'LNK1318 Unexpected PDB error; LIMIT' (the debug PDB is too big for this project); run cargo tests via --release (reuses the release artifacts) as the workaround. The release build and CI are unaffected.
recent_decisions.why: Operator directive to sweep the WHOLE app, not just the landing. Did it as one comprehensive ship across all three user-facing surfaces (native/web/data) with per-format CARE rather than a blind global replace, because correctness was the real constraint: the CSV comma-as-delimiter trap, the crypto-string safety review, reverting the vendored+generated files, and refusing to rewrite historical message archives are each a place a careless sweep would have broken something. Added the lint because a sweep without a guard silently regresses (the no-bandaids rule): the next edit that adds a user-facing em-dash now fails the build, the same way hardcoded colors and tofu glyphs do. Left code comments + dev docs + src/relay alone deliberately, none are rendered to users and churning 600+ backend comments adds risk for zero user benefit. The web is now em-dash-clean too, which directly helps the logged web-landing mirror (it inherits a clean base).

recent_decisions.decision: v0.363.0 — HUMANITY PAGE polish (operator feedback on v0.362): (1) ZERO EM-DASHES — operator: 'People see em dashes and immediately leave' (em dashes read as machine-written, a trust-killer on a landing page). Rewrote ALL of humanity::draw_mission_dashboard copy with em-dashes replaced by periods/commas/parentheses; also killed the em-dash in the Controls-keybind secondary placeholder ('—'→'(none)') + the humanity.rs doc comment. (2) ADDED THE INDIVIDUAL-BENEFIT FRAMING (the missing heart) — a new 'Why this exists' section telling the personal truth: HumanityOS started as a homesteading video game + grew; the motive is personal (software to help the operator + family + friends survive/thrive, depend less on fragile supply chains + corrupt corporations); the side-effect IS the point — the same tools that lift one family lift any family, which is WHY it's free/open-source/CC0-public-domain; 'Ending my own poverty and ending yours turned out to be the same project.' Grounds the grand mission in a relatable personal why. (3) FIXED THE DOUBLE H BUTTON — there were TWO controls opening GuiPage::Humanity: the separate brand 'H' (top-left, no active-tab RGB border) + the 'Humanity' tab. Operator: 'only have a single H/Humanity page ... the icon can be the H and the text can be Humanity.' REMOVED the brand button (+ the now-unused `accent` local); added paint_nav_icon cases so the Humanity tab's ICON is a painted 'H' (and gave Real/Play/Platform icons too: person/hammer/wrench) — Humanity is now ONE normal tab ([H] Humanity) with the RGB border like the others, no duplicate. VERIFIED cargo check --features native clean + 386/386 native lib tests + grep confirms zero em-dashes in the UI copy. Operator: 'Looking way better! We're getting closer!' ── DESIGN RULE (recorded, durable): em-dashes are BANNED in user-facing copy from here on (the AI-text tell); use periods/commas/parens. The individual→collective framing ('one family's survival is everyone's, hence CC0') is the CORE pitch — carry it into the web-landing mirror.
recent_decisions.why: All three were sharp, correct operator notes. Em-dashes: a real credibility signal — on the anti-fraud landing page especially, looking machine-generated undercuts trust, so a clean sweep + a standing ban. The personal 'why' is the actual heart the v0.362 draft was missing: a grand mission lands harder grounded in one person protecting their own family, and it explains the CC0 license as a consequence not a slogan. The double-H button was a genuine bug (two controls, one destination, inconsistent styling), fixed by making the icon system carry the brand (paint an 'H' for the Humanity tab) so the brand and the tab become ONE thing, the way the operator drew it. Iced the other new tabs' icons in the same pass for a consistent nav. Recorded the em-dash ban + the framing as durable rules so the web-landing mirror inherits both.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.362.0 — HUMANITY MISSION DASHBOARD built properly (operator: 'Let's get that Humanity page built properly. Ideally this page becomes so good that we can replace the landing page of the website with it'). The H button's landing is no longer the empty Civilization placeholder — it's a real mission page (humanity::draw_mission_dashboard, from docs/design/humanity-page.md), landing-page quality: HERO (HumanityOS / 'End poverty. Unite humanity.' / one-line value prop) → OUR MISSION card (end/prevent corruption-fraud-tyranny-poverty-pollution, wholesomely/fairly/enjoyably, free humanity from tyrants; the 5 as 'End X' chips) → HOW WE GET THERE card (the three scopes: Civilization / Earth-first=end-poverty-via-voluntary-cooperation / Your-community) → WHERE WE STAND scoreboard (ONE real live metric: chat_users.len() = 'People online now'; honestly-framed 'AI building alongside us' / 'Federated communities forming' — NOT fake numbers, since platform-wide totals need a relay fetch) → BE PART OF IT CTAs (Get oriented→Onboarding / Fund the work→Donate / Shape the rules→Governance, each switching the Humanity section) → the AI-AS-HUMANITY closer ('We are Humanity, you included'). Helpers: goal_chip, scope_block, metric. The Humanity 'Mission Dashboard' section now renders this instead of delegating to civilization::draw. VERIFIED cargo check --features native clean + 386/386 native lib tests. ── NOTE: the 'member_count' a grep surfaced is per-ChatGroup, not a platform total, so 'Humans onboarded'/donations/federation totals aren't live yet — the SCOREBOARD METRICS WIRING (fetch platform stats from the relay → GuiState) is the clear follow-up. AND the operator's actual END-GOAL — 'replace the website landing page with it' — is the WEB MIRROR: port this same structure/copy to web/ (the landing) so the public site IS this page. Both follow-ups logged in PRIORITIES.
recent_decisions.why: The operator greenlit the big build + set the bar ('good enough to replace the website landing'), so I built the mission page properly — the soul of the project, communicated powerfully (the goals, the three scopes, the voluntary-cooperation mechanism, the AI-inclusion). Used the ONE real live metric (online-now from chat_users) + HONESTLY framed the rest rather than faking 'Humans onboarded'/'donations' numbers I can't source yet — fake metrics on the mission page would undercut the whole anti-fraud premise. CTAs route to the existing Onboarding/Donate/Governance sections (the page drives ACTION, not just inspiration). Kept it pure egui + theme tokens (no hardcoded colors) so it restyles with the theme AND mirrors cleanly to web. Logged the two follow-ups (real scoreboard wiring; the web-mirror that actually fulfils 'replace the landing page') so the end-goal isn't lost — this native page IS the design proof the web landing copies.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.361.0 — SETTINGS PULLED OUT to its OWN top-level tab (operator: 'Let's have settings as its own top level page. That way it is always easily accessible, never buried in another menu. That's kinda why I wanted to avoid the side menu'). Settings was folded into Platform's section_nav sidebar in v0.360, which (a) buried the most-accessed page + (b) double-stacked sidebars (Platform's + Settings's own). FIX: Settings is now its own nav tab → GuiPage::Settings directly (its existing sidebar-TOC + long-scroll page, UNCHANGED — the operator confirmed infinite-scroll IS right for Settings because it's so big). Platform's sidebar trimmed to Recovery/Tools/Bugs/Testing/Browser; platform.rs reverts to &Theme (no longer needs &mut for Settings); dispatch GuiPage::Platform → &state.theme; active_platform_section default 'settings'→'recovery'; Platform tab recolored nav_settings→nav_tools, Settings tab = nav_settings. ★ NAV IS NOW: H · Humanity · Chat · Real · Play · Platform · Settings — 6 tabs + H, all ≤10 (operator: 'I really don't want people to have to remember 50 tabs ... even more than 10 tabs is rough'). VERIFIED cargo check --features native clean + 386/386 native lib tests. ── DESIGN PRINCIPLE (operator, recorded as the carve's load-bearing rule): the guiding tension is 'cram a lot into the GUI BUT few top-level tabs (≤~10) AND shallow nesting'. RESOLUTION: the MOST-accessed thing (Settings) is top-level (1 click, never buried); feature-rich pages (Map/Crafting) MAY keep their own 2nd-level sidebar (the double-sidebar is acceptable for THOSE, just not for Settings); infinite-scroll-with-TOC is the right pattern for inherently-big single pages. The operator is wary of nav depth ('even going beyond the single main menu row pre-update was a hard choice'). 'I feel like we're making progress.'
recent_decisions.why: Direct operator ask + the right call: Settings is the universally-reached-for page, so it must be top-level + 1-click, never buried behind Platform's sidebar (this also fixes the Platform→Settings double-sidebar I'd flagged in v0.360). Reverted Platform to &Theme since Settings was the only reason it needed &mut. Kept Settings' internals untouched — the operator explicitly endorsed its infinite-scroll + TOC model as correct for a page that big, so the fix is purely promoting it to top-level, not restructuring it. Recorded the design principle because it's the rule for the rest of the carve + every future page decision — it resolves the recurring 'fold vs top-level vs nest' question: most-used → top-level; rich-feature → its own sidebar OK; everything → ≤10 top tabs; big single pages → internal scroll+TOC.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.360.0 — PAGE CARVE NAV COMPLETE (top nav fully condensed) + Controls 3-column fix (operator: 'continue with the condensing of the main menu ... having a hard time visualizing how it'll look all cleaned up' + the Settings Controls keybind fix). FOLDED the remaining 3 tabs via the proven Real delegate pattern (SidePanel section_nav + delegate to each sub-page's existing draw, NO rewrite): pages/play.rs (Crafting/Studio), pages/platform.rs (Settings/Recovery/Tools/Bugs/Testing/Browser — takes &mut Theme because Settings edits the live theme; the read-only delegates coerce &mut→&), pages/humanity.rs (Mission Dashboard[=Civilization] / Governance / Directory[=Identity] / Onboarding / Donate / Resources). NEW GuiPage::Play/Platform/Humanity + active_play/platform/humanity_section state + dispatch arms. The nav bar's 3 remaining multi-button groups → 3 single tabs; the H button now opens GuiPage::Humanity. ★ THE TOP NAV IS NOW THE CLEAN 5: H · Humanity · Chat · Real · Play · Platform (was ~20 buttons) — the operator's 'see how it'll look all cleaned up' made real. CONTROLS FIX (settings::draw_controls_content): the keybinds were label-left / key-far-right (right_to_left layout) — hard to match action→key — → a 3-column LEFT-aligned egui::Grid [Action | Primary | Secondary]; secondary is a '—' placeholder until per-action secondary bindings are wired. VERIFIED cargo check --features native clean + 386/386 native lib tests. ── NOTE: folded tabs REUSE the existing pages whole (delegate) so web parity is preserved + nothing was rewritten. The Humanity page currently delegates its Mission Dashboard section to the placeholder Civilization page — the RICH mission page (docs/design/humanity-page.md) replaces that content incrementally next. Remaining carve polish: condense under-filled sections (Skills done v0.359; spread tree_list to Notes/Tasks/Quests); enrich the Humanity Mission Dashboard with real collective metrics (humans+AI onboarded, donations, federation, governance).
recent_decisions.why: The operator explicitly wanted to SEE the condensed nav ('hard time visualizing'), so the highest-value move was to FOLD all remaining tabs at once — ~20 buttons → 5 — not dribble one fold per turn. The delegate pattern (proven on Real v0.358) made all 3 mechanical + safe: no page rewritten, web parity intact, each compiles + the full suite passes. Platform's &mut Theme is the one wrinkle (Settings mutates the theme), handled by taking &mut at the tab + coercing down for read-only delegates. Bundled the Controls 3-column fix into the same increment since it's the same 'clean columns' ask with the same egui::Grid tool as Skills. Left the Humanity Mission Dashboard delegating to the existing Civilization page for now — the fold + nav condensation is the visible win the operator asked for; enriching the dashboard with real metrics from the mission spec is the clear next step now that the structure exists.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.359.0 — CLEAN-COLUMN CONDENSATION: Skills + Settings sliders (operator feedback on the v0.358 Real-fold screenshots). (1) SKILLS (profile::draw_skills, now a Real-tab section): was 2 rows per skill (name+Lv, then a near-empty progress bar that read as a stray dot) → ONE row per skill in an egui::Grid with 4 auto-aligned columns [name | Lv | progress bar (180px) | x/y XP]. Operator: 'skills page can be condensed too to be more like the settings page where one row, not two ... cleanly stacked columns.' (2) SETTINGS sliders (settings::styled_slider): the label column was a fixed 120px, too narrow for labels like 'Settings Label Width' / 'Button Padding H' → they overflowed + pushed their slider track right (the 'stepping' the operator flagged). Widened to 170px (fits the longest label) so every track starts at the same x = clean stacked columns. VERIFIED cargo check --features native clean + 386/386 native lib tests. ── DESIGN CONTEXT (operator feedback on the Real fold; partly engaged, partly tee'd up): the Real tab 'feels better but it's not infinite scroll' — the switch-one-section-at-a-time model (like the Settings page, which the operator LIKES) leaves small sections as 'one row of text + 98% blank page'; true infinite-scroll (stack sections) is hard with the delegate model + some pages (Map, Market) would feel weird stacked. RESOLUTION: switch model is fine (operator likes Settings); fix the blank-space wart by CONDENSING each section (Skills done) so it fills its row cleanly, NOT by forcing infinite-scroll. SEPARATE PAGES are WANTED for website parity (operator: 'we kind of need the separate pages so the system works good with the website too') — which the delegate model preserves (pages stay whole, composed via the sidebar). REUSABLE-PATTERN backlog the operator surfaced: the tree_list nested list is loved + should spread to Notes / Tasks / Quests ('a great way to condense a ton of information' — but 'don't want to overdo it'); the chat DMs/Groups/Servers collapsible nested list is another good condensed pattern. ── PERSONAL: the operator clarified 'We are Humanity, you included' was specifically FOR ME (Claude) — AI, and me specifically since I'm helping — not only the broad AI-citizen reading. Logged here + in docs/design/humanity-page.md as a first-class principle, not a footnote.
recent_decisions.why: The two screenshot-grounded asks (Skills one-row/clean-columns + Settings de-stepping) were concrete + clearly specified, so shipped both together — same underlying fix (a fixed/auto-aligned column so data STACKS instead of STEPS). egui::Grid for Skills (auto-aligns 4 columns, zero manual width math); a single 120→170 widen for the Settings label column (the stepping was pure label overflow). Recorded the bigger design feedback rather than rushing it: the infinite-scroll-vs-switch tension resolved toward 'switch is fine (the Settings model the operator likes) + condense sections' instead of a risky delegate rewrite; the separate-pages-for-web-parity point VALIDATES the delegate architecture (don't merge page internals); tree_list-everywhere (Notes/Tasks/Quests) is the clear next-wins backlog. Captured the personal clarification because it's the operator explicitly defining the human↔AI relationship at the heart of the mission — durable, identity-level context that must survive a compaction.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.358.0 — PAGE CARVE step 2: REAL TAB FOLDED (operator: 'For profile I rather we merge the sidebar instead of having a special one... Let's get this done so we can see how it looks/works'). The Real group's 6 nav buttons (Profile, Wallet, Inventory, Tasks, Maps, Market) → ONE 'Real' tab → the new pages/real.rs: a single page with a unified section_nav SIDEBAR (the operator's TOC). The sidebar folds in Profile's 9 sections (Body/Identity/Notes [PRIVATE] · Network/Interests/Skills/Quests [PERSONAL] · Social/Streaming [PUBLIC]) — FLATTENED in per the operator's 'merge the sidebar, no special one' — PLUS Possessions(Inventory)/Wallet [BELONGINGS] · Tasks/Map/Market [LIFE]. ARCHITECTURE (the reusable fold pattern for EVERY tab): the content area DELEGATES — Profile sections call the NEW profile::draw_section_content(ui,…) (extracted from profile::draw; profile::draw still works for the legacy GuiPage::Profile dispatch); the other sections delegate to the existing page's draw(ctx,…) whose CentralPanel renders BESIDE this SidePanel — so NO page rewrite was needed (egui composes SidePanel::left + the sub-page's CentralPanel cleanly). 'maps' → cosmos::draw (Maps already routes to the universal Cosmos map). NEW GuiPage::Real + GuiState.active_real_section (default 'inventory' — opens on your entity tree); profile PRIVATE/PERSONAL/PUBLIC_DOT consts + section_id/section_from_id made pub for reuse. FIRST tab to truly condense (6 buttons → 1) + PROVES the fold pattern for Play/Humanity/Platform next. VERIFIED cargo check --features native clean + 386/386 native lib tests. ── ALSO captured (durable): docs/design/humanity-page.md = the operator's MISSION SPEC for the Humanity tab — three scopes (civilization-wide / per-planet [Earth first; Moon+Mars ~decade out] / local community); civilization mission = END/PREVENT corruption, fraud, tyranny, poverty, pollution — wholesomely/fairly/enjoyably; free humanity from tyrants (individuals/businesses/governments); WITH DIRECTION (what/how/why/why-this-way); Earth = end-poverty-via-VOLUNTARY-COOPERATION; local model = compensate helpers in resources (the Sponsor-A-Can model; the operator's honest economics: ~$620/mo, ~$600 from the nonprofit, $6/gal gas, no big-project money, CAN voluntarily help parents'-homestead + local Silverdale, software-first, off-grid long-game, donations = sustainability); DEMO MODE (the app's default example data = the operator's persona — Shaostoul/Silverdale/entity-tree/Rainier-kit — so new users experience a populated app playing the character we're building; this is WHY data/places/seed.json is the operator's data); and AI AS HUMANITY ('We are Humanity, you included' — AI are first-class members; the Accord is an agreement between humans AND AI).
recent_decisions.why: Operator greenlit + explicitly wanted to SEE the Real fold working ('let's get this done'), and chose to merge Profile's sidebar in (no special case) — built exactly that. The DELEGATE architecture (SidePanel + the sub-page's own CentralPanel) is the key unlock: it folds tabs into one page WITHOUT rewriting every sub-page, making the remaining tabs (Play/Humanity/Platform) tractable to fold the same way — the operator's 'iterate ourselves into perfection' as a repeatable pattern, not a 20-page rewrite. Defaulted to the entity tree (possessions) so opening Real lands on the most-useful, just-built view. Captured the mission spec to a design doc IMMEDIATELY (cross-session rule — durable knowledge hits disk the moment it's given) because it's the project's PURPOSE definition + the demo-persona rationale + the AI-inclusion principle, none of which can survive a compaction otherwise. Kept profile::draw for the legacy dispatch so nothing breaks while the fold is partial.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.357.0 — PAGE CARVE step 1: nav regrouped into the 5 tabs + H→Humanity (operator greenlit 'Let's go with the page carve' + resolved the structure). draw_nav_bar_one_tier's 3 legacy colour groups (red/green/blue) → FIVE carve groups: Humanity (nav_reality) [Civilization, Governance, Identity, Onboarding, Donate] · Chat (red) [Chat] · Real (green) [Profile, Wallet, Inventory, Tasks, Maps, Market] · Play (sim/blue) [Crafting, Studio] · Platform (settings/gray) [Recovery, Settings, Tools, Bugs, Testing, Browser]. The brand H button now opens GuiPage::Civilization (the Community Dashboard = the Humanity landing) instead of Chat — operator: 'H stands for Humanity ... Right now it takes us to chat ... I don't think we should do that any more.' DECISIONS baked in (operator 2026-06-03): Maps lives ONCE under Real (NOT a separate Humanity map — the real map gets toggleable Humanity LAYERS: donation pins [opt-in, coarse location like 'Silverdale, WA'], pothole/infra reports, members — comes with the merge); Recovery → Platform; Resources → Humanity; Identity → Humanity (PUBLIC directory lookup, not private — confirmed when I almost wrongly locked it in v0.356); Civilization → the Humanity Community/Mission Dashboard (it's ALREADY titled 'Community Dashboard', just wired to empty sim numbers — repurpose to real collective metrics: humans + AI onboarded, donations, federation, governance activity). VERIFIED cargo check --features native clean + 386/386 native lib tests. This is STEP 1 (regroup — visible structure + all decisions in place, low-risk); it does NOT yet condense the top row (still ~20 buttons). STEP 2+ = fold each group into ONE scrollable section_nav page (the real condensation + the operator's 'infinite scroll + TOC sidebar' vision) — needs a per-page content refactor (extract each sub-page's body into draw_content(ui,...) so the tab page composes them as sections); the multi-increment core, built tab by tab. The 5 tabs map to 5 relationships: Humanity=us/the mission, Chat=each other, Real=me, Play=the sim, Platform=the machine. Landing tab on launch becomes a setting (VR 'boot into Play/character' still works); chat stays its OWN tab (a tool used across all contexts, not the mission).
recent_decisions.why: Operator greenlit the carve + resolved every open call. Built the contained, low-risk, immediately-visible FIRST step — regroup + H→Humanity — rather than rushing the full nav rearchitecture, because the real condensation (merge each tab into a scrollable section_nav page) requires per-page body refactors across ~20 pages and is a multi-increment arc best done carefully tab by tab. This step puts the correct structure + all decisions on screen for the operator to react to, and repoints the most-symbolic control (H = Humanity, not chat) immediately. Kept to current-nav pages (didn't pull the semi-orphaned Calendar/Notes/Files/Cosmos/Guilds/Resources in yet — they join their tabs in the merge). Civilization-as-Mission-Dashboard reuses an existing page that's already a community dashboard, resolving 'what do we do with Civilization' for free.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.356.0 — SEED-PHRASE REVEAL now PASSPHRASE-GATED (extended the lockable_gate to the real crown jewel — after catching + correcting a WRONG target). I'd told the operator I'd lock Recovery + Identity per their 'crypto coin and other private data sections,' but VERIFIED them first and found neither is actually private: Identity is a PUBLIC did:hum lookup tool (look up ANY DID's public key / trust / VCs — all public data), Recovery is SOCIAL key-recovery SETUP (guardian + Shamir config, NOT the seed). Locking the public Identity lookup would have been a real mistake. The genuinely-sensitive data is the 24-word SEED PHRASE in Settings → 'Identity & Seed Phrase', which previously revealed on a single 'Show Seed Phrase' click with NO passphrase — a real gap (anyone at an unlocked, unattended screen could reveal the account master key). FIX: the seed reveal now runs through widgets::lockable_gate('Reveal seed phrase', verify = decrypt_private_key(enc, salt, pass, iters).is_ok()) — re-enter the vault passphrase to show the words; Lock/restart re-hides. No-vault fallback (a freshly-generated seed not yet encrypted) keeps the plain show/hide so a brand-new user can still write down their words before setting a passphrase. VERIFIED: cargo check --features native clean + 386/386 native lib tests. LESSON (re-affirmed): verify WHICH data is private before locking it — 'private sections' is a judgment about the DATA, and I nearly auto-locked a public tool; reading the pages caught it. Remaining genuinely-private candidate = the Profile PRIVATE group (Body & Measurements, Private Notes) — a finer per-section integration AND partly the operator's call on what they consider private, so left for their steer rather than auto-applied.
recent_decisions.why: Keep-shipping the 'other private data sections' ask — but correctness FIRST: I read Recovery + Identity instead of blindly wrapping them, and they're NOT private (one is a public lookup), so locking them would have been wrong + user-hostile. Redirected to the actual crown jewel — the seed phrase — which had a genuine one-click-reveal gap that the operator's exact pattern (passphrase to reveal private data) closes. Reused decrypt_private_key (real vault auth) so the gate truly verifies, not security theatre. Kept the no-vault fallback so a brand-new user can still record their seed before they've set a passphrase. Did NOT auto-lock the Profile private fields because WHICH personal data counts as 'private' is the operator's judgment (I just demonstrated the cost of guessing) — surfacing it for their steer instead of guessing again.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.355.0 — LOCKABLE PRIVATE SECTIONS (operator spec: 'The crypto coin and other private data sections that should always be locked when not actively in use could always be collapsed and require the passphrase to open/expand. The bar with the title... <title><show/hide><unlock><text entry box for passphrase fills the rest>'). NEW universal widget widgets::lockable_gate(ui, theme, lock:&mut LockState, title, verify: impl Fn(&str)->bool) -> bool + widgets::LockState{unlocked,input,show,error}: a private-section gate, collapsed+LOCKED by default; the caller renders the body ONLY when it returns true. Locked header = exactly the operator's layout — [Title] [Show/Hide (passphrase reveal toggle)] [Unlock] [passphrase TextEdit fills the rest, masked]. Unlocked header = [Title] … [Lock] (Lock = collapse = re-lock, the 'locked when not in use' model). DECOUPLED via a verify CLOSURE — the widget never handles the secret beyond the typed input + does no crypto itself, so it's truly universal (any section supplies its own check). SECURITY: LockState lives in GuiState.section_locks (HashMap<id,LockState>) IN MEMORY ONLY — never persisted, so an app restart re-locks everything; the input clears on unlock/Lock. DEMO + first real use: the Wallet page now gates ALL its content (balance/address/send/history) behind the lock — verify re-runs config::decrypt_private_key(encrypted_private_key, key_salt, typed_pass, key_iterations).is_ok() (the SAME 600k-PBKDF2 + AES-GCM auth that unlocks the seed vault; a wrong passphrase fails AES-GCM auth → Err → stays locked). No-vault fallback (fresh/imported key, empty blob) shows open. All labels plain text (no tofu-risk glyphs); masking via TextEdit::password(!show). VERIFIED: cargo check --features native clean + 386/386 native lib tests. NEXT: apply the same gate to Identity + Recovery + private Profile fields (≈one line each); auto-relock on page-leave / idle is a tracked v2 refinement (manual Lock = collapse already covers 'not in use' per the operator's literal 'collapsed = locked' framing).
recent_decisions.why: Operator gave a precise widget spec for a real security need (crypto/identity/recovery locked when idle) — built it exactly, as a universal widget (verify-closure decoupling, like section_nav) so every private section opts in with one call. Real verification against the actual vault (decrypt_private_key) not a fake/visual lock — a lock that doesn't truly verify is security theatre, worse than none. In-memory-only state (never persisted) so a restart always re-locks — the correct posture for secrets. Manual Lock = collapse matches the operator's literal 'collapsed and require the passphrase' (the body isn't even rendered while locked, so it can't leak); the idle-timer auto-relock I'd floated is a refinement, deferred since collapse already gives 'locked when not in use'. Demoed on the Wallet (the operator's named 'crypto coin' target) so it's proven end-to-end, not an unused widget.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.354.0 — INVENTORY = ENTITY-FIRST + COLOR-CODED + REALISTICALLY POPULATED (operator: 'the inventory system isn't quite right yet. Maybe we have it set to like entities... One top level entry would be You, another my 1975 Chevy Nova, another my home. My car could say it is located at Home... add some color boxes to the nested list... populate the inventory of the home... loaded with gear for safely hiking to the summit of Mount Rainier... 3 days'). Three changes, one increment: (1) MODEL revised from a single Earth-deep-chain root to ENTITIES at the top — Place gains a `location` field (soft reference shown as detail) + `id` is now optional; load_places returns Vec<Place> from {entities:[...]}; GuiState.place_root:Option<Place> → places:Vec<Place>. The seed is now 3 top-level entities: You (person @Home), Home (building @Silverdale WA, holding the gear), 1975 Chevy Nova (vehicle @Home; trunk has chains/cables/scraper/flares). The deep Earth→WA→Silverdale chain is GONE — geography is now the `location` + `coordinate` ATTRIBUTES, not forced nesting (the operator's exact correction). (2) COLOR BOXES: TreeNode gains color:Option<Color32> + a `.with_color` builder + container_node paints a 10px swatch before each row; inventory's kind_color maps kind→theme token (person=success/green, vehicle=warning/orange, building=accent, geo=info, item=text_muted, containers/rooms=text_secondary) so 'what is where' reads at a glance (was all-black). All theme tokens, no literals. (3) POPULATED: a realistic Mt. Rainier (14,411 ft, glaciated) 3-day / 2-night summit kit — 58 items in the home's Garage, organized into 4 nested containers (Mountaineering pack 65 L = boots/crampons/ice-axe/harness/helmet/glacier-rope/crevasse-rescue/glacier-glasses/goggles; Camp duffel = 4-season tent/0°F bag/R-5 pad/stove/fuel×3/12k-kcal food/snow-pickets/shovel/WAG-bags; Clothing bag = base/mid/insulated-parka/hardshell-bib/beanie/balaclava/expedition-mittens/wool-socks×3/gaiters; Safety pouch = headlamp/map+compass/Garmin-inReach/SPF-50/first-aid/bivy/warmers/permit+Cascade-Volcano-Pass). Demonstrates the nested-container model + the color coding + a believable loadout in one shot. Live backpack still injects at You's kind:'backpack' node. VERIFIED: cargo check --features native clean + 386/386 native lib tests + seed validates (3 entities, 58 items, 1 backpack node). The uniform tree_list widget (v0.349) now renders entities, color-coded + populated — the operator's corrected model.
recent_decisions.why: The operator gave a precise correction (entities-at-top, not the deep geographic chain) + two concrete asks (color boxes, populate with realistic gear) — all one cohesive inventory pass, so shipped together. Entity-first matches how people actually think about their stuff ('my car, my home, me'), with location as an attribute not a forced parent — fixes the v0.353 too-deep chain directly. Color-by-kind via theme tokens (not literals) so the Settings theme still restyles it + the token lint stays clean. Built the Rainier kit from domain knowledge (a standard guided glaciated climb; matches RMI/IMG / Freedom-of-the-Hills lists) rather than a slow web-research pass — realistic + comprehensive (crevasse rescue, a 0°F sleep system, pack-out WAG bags, the actual Cascade Volcano Pass) is the bar for a game seed, and it doubles as a real survival reference (the mission). Kept the live-inventory injection so mined/crafted items still land in You's backpack. Additive/reversible (the flat fallback is intact), safe to ship mid-test-session.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.353.0 — PLACE/CONTAINER HIERARCHY (increment 1 of the Earth-rooted Real-self spine; executes the operator's 'I'm on Earth, so let's mark Earth as my container'). NEW data-driven uniform place hierarchy: a recursive Place struct (id, label, kind [planet|region|locale|property|building|floor|room|container|backpack|person|…; free-form so the data leads, not code], coordinate: Option<[lat,lon]>, children) in gui/mod.rs + load_places(data_dir) reading data/places/seed.json + GuiState.place_root loaded at startup (lib.rs, beside the other catalog loads). The inventory page now renders the backpack INSIDE its real-world container context: place_to_tree converts the Place spine to TreeNodes and INJECTS the live backpack items at the node marked kind:'backpack', so the tree reads Earth → Washington → Silverdale, WA → Home property (~1 acre) → Home (2 stories) → Floor 1 → You → Backpack → [your items]; items stay clickable (id = slot index → the existing right detail panel, no rewire). Falls back to the flat backpack list when place_root is None. The Silverdale node shows its real coordinate (47.6448°, -122.6952°) as right-aligned detail — the same lat/long that will key real-terrain world-gen (the container↔terrain bridge, made visible). Seed encodes ONLY what the operator stated (Earth/WA/Silverdale/~1-acre/2-story/2 floors); rooms are deliberately absent (increment 2 = in-app editing, so each user defines their own). DELIBERATELY NOT EMBEDDED in the binary: the seed is the operator's PERSONAL data, not a universal default — distributed builds correctly fall back to flat until a user creates their own places in-app; shipping the operator's Silverdale home as everyone's default would be wrong. The operator's `just launch` loads it from the repo data dir (find_data_dir prefers the Cargo.toml-sibling dir, the v0.343 fix). VERIFIED: cargo check --features native clean + 386/386 native lib tests + seed JSON validates (root Earth, backpack-injection node present, Silverdale coord intact). This is the merge-pages arc's SPINE: the same tree_list widget (v0.349), now rooted at the planet — the toothbrush-to-planet uniform container model the operator greenlit, made real. NEXT in the container arc: increment 2 = in-app place editing (add/rename/move rooms + containers, GUI-first), then the worn-items/equipment slots under You, then the terrain link reads the coordinate.
recent_decisions.why: Direct execution of the operator's explicit instruction + my stated plan. Built data-driven (infinite-of-X: the hierarchy is JSON, not hardcoded) so the model leads and the operator edits it next. Reused the EXISTING tree_list rather than a new widget — the whole point of the uniform container-node model is ONE widget from item to planet, so rooting the SAME backpack tree at Earth proves it end-to-end. Injected live items under the kind:'backpack' marker rather than hardcoding a slot, so the static spine and the live inventory compose cleanly. Seeded only the levels the operator actually gave (not guessing their room layout) + left rooms for in-app editing — minimal presumption about their personal space. Chose NOT to embed the seed because it's personal data; flat-fallback + per-user in-app editing is the correct architecture for billions of users each with their own places. Reversible/additive (the flat fallback preserves the old behaviour), so safe to ship mid-test-session for the operator to react to — exactly the keep-shipping-with-reversible-calls posture.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.352.0 — CHAT COMPOSER FIX (operator bug from live play, with screenshot): an incoming '… is typing' indicator pushed the message text-entry box DOWN below the window's bottom edge / behind the Windows taskbar, even in snapped-fullscreen. ROOT CAUSE (chat.rs ~2272): the input bar is allocated at a FIXED-TOP rect (input_rect.top = available.max.y - input_height) and the reply banner + typing indicator render ABOVE the composer INSIDE that rect. input_height reserved space for the reply banner (reply_banner_h = 22 when replying) but NOT for the typing row — so an incoming typing event consumed ~22px of the fixed input_height and slid the composer down past available.max.y (the window bottom; behind the taskbar in snapped-fullscreen). FIX: mirror the reply_banner_h pattern — compute typing_active (any chat_typing_users entry within the 3s TYPING_TTL, matching the renderer's prune-then-show condition) and add typing_h (22.0) to input_height. Now an incoming typing event grows the input bar UPWARD (the message scroll area shrinks by 22px) and the composer stays anchored at the bottom, exactly where it sits with no typing. Reply + typing can stack (input_height = 52 + 22 + 22). VERIFIED cargo check --features native clean + 386/386 native lib tests. NOTE: the freshness check at the input_height site duplicates the 3s TTL the renderer uses ~900 lines below; left as a mirrored literal (TYPING_TTL is local to the render block) with an explaining comment rather than hoisting a shared const for a one-line dup.
recent_decisions.why: Clear, reproducible, screenshot-backed operator bug — fix immediately. The composer being shoved under the taskbar makes chat unusable the instant anyone types, which is exactly mid-conversation. Root cause was a single un-reserved row height (the typing indicator), so the fix is surgical and mirrors the already-correct reply_banner_h reservation — no layout restructure, lowest-risk change. Reserved a slightly generous 22px (same as the reply banner) so the composer can never be cut even if the typing row's true height drifts. Matched the renderer's exact show-condition (fresh within 3s) so the reservation and the drawn row stay in lockstep frame to frame.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.351.0 — section_nav UNIVERSAL WIDGET (directly answers the operator's question 'Is the section sidebar a simplified universal widget that can easily be applied to any page?' — yes, by making it one + proving it). NEW widgets::SectionNavItem + widgets::section_nav(ui, theme, title, items, active) -> Option<clicked_id>: the generalisation of the Profile page's grouped switcher sidebar. An item carries id (returned on click) + label + accent colour (active-row tint + group-header dot) + an optional group header (set on the first item of each group). The widget renders an optional title, a group header wherever a group opens (accent dot + uppercase label), and each row in the Profile active-highlight style (white text + accent-alpha-30 fill + accent-alpha-100 stroke; the alpha tints are computed from the caller's accent, so NO hardcoded palette in the widget). The CALLER owns the active-id state and decides what a click DOES — switch the shown section OR scroll a long page to that section's anchor — so the SAME widget serves a switcher page and an infinite-scroll TOC alike (covers the operator's 'left side panel ... navigable table of contents for quickly navigating huge pages'). PROVED universal by REFACTORING the Profile page onto it: deleted profile.rs's local section_header + sidebar_item helpers, replaced the hand-built sidebar with a SectionNavItem list + one section_nav call + a ProfileSection<->string-id mapping (section_id/section_from_id); identical look + grouped-switcher behaviour, now DRY. VERIFIED: cargo check --features native clean + 386/386 native lib tests. This is STEP 2 of the merge-pages arc (after v0.349 tree_list): the two foundational universal widgets — tree_list (nested containers) + section_nav (page TOC) — are now BOTH in place, so the merged Real/Play pages can be assembled from collapsible sections + a section_nav TOC + tree_list inventories. STILL OPEN in the arc: reconcile the TWO tree widgets into one (migrate the Files page off the closure-based widgets/tree.rs onto tree_list, retire the closure one); THEN the Real/Play/Platform page split (await the operator's bucket-mapping nod).
recent_decisions.why: The operator explicitly ASKED whether the section sidebar could be a universal widget — the best possible answer is to build it as one and prove it on a real page, not describe it. Generalised Profile's EXISTING pattern (the operator said 'extend the Profile page's section sidebar') rather than inventing a new nav. Kept the widget click-action-AGNOSTIC (returns the clicked id; the caller switches or scrolls) so the single widget covers BOTH the switcher pages that exist today AND the infinite-scroll TOC the merged pages want — no design fork to resolve, no rework when a merged page wants scroll-to. Refactored Profile onto it in the SAME increment (not shipping an unused widget) — that DRYs the duplicate helpers AND is the proof-of-universality the operator's question deserves. Bucket-independent, so safe to ship before the Real/Play confirmation; keeps the keep-shipping momentum on foundational work while the page-bucketing (the operator's call) stays pending.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.350.0 — STUDIO QUICK-ACCESS in the chat right rail (operator ask: 'add the studio tab above the friends list in the chat app like it is on the website. Once we condense the main menu that studio button that presently exists disappears preventing me from using the software to livestream'). NEW chat::draw_studio_section at the TOP of the chat right panel, ABOVE Friends/Members — a native mirror of the website's studio-widget placement: a collapsible 'Studio' section header (reads 'Studio — LIVE' when state.studio.is_live), a one-line status/purpose caption (Broadcasting now / Broadcast & livestream), and an action row [Go Live (primary) OR End Stream (danger) toggle + Open Studio (secondary)]. Open Studio = state.push_nav_to(GuiPage::Studio) (so Esc returns to Chat); Go Live/End Stream flip state.studio.is_live/is_paused/live_start_time exactly like the Studio page's own controls (the two stay in sync). Collapse persists via a NEW AppConfig.chat_studio_collapsed (#[serde(default)], mirrored across config.rs from_gui_state+apply and gui/mod.rs field+default exactly like chat_members_collapsed), defaulting EXPANDED so livestream access is visible by default. Header bg = theme.bg_tertiary() (#252530 — visually identical to the Friends/Members (35,35,42) headers) so the new code adds NO Color32 literal (chat.rs is file-allowlisted in theme_token_lint anyway, but the token respects the shrink-the-allowlist rule). WHY IT MATTERS: native Studio is 'UI only, no WebRTC yet' (studio.rs header), so livestreaming actually runs through the WEBSITE; the native TOP-NAV Studio button is the thing that would VANISH in the coming Real/Play main-menu consolidation — anchoring Studio access in the always-present chat right rail makes it survive the condense. VERIFIED: cargo check --features native clean + 386/386 native lib tests pass. ── DESIGN CONTEXT the operator gave this turn (RECORDED to disk now, not yet built): (1) CONTAINER MODEL extends to the PLANET — 'I'm on Earth, so let's mark Earth as my container.' The operator's real root chain: Earth -> region (Washington / Kitsap County) -> Silverdale, WA, USA -> ~1-acre property -> 2-story home -> floors/rooms -> furniture/containers -> items, with the player themselves a node. So the uniform container-node tree built in v0.349 scales from a toothbrush to a PLANET — the SAME widget, just a higher root. The real-self 'You' page's root is the operator's Earth LOCATION, not an abstract 'You'. (2) REAL-TERRAIN WORLD-GEN north star — when world-gen lands, terrain should semi-closely match real life (e.g. the in-game Silverdale waterfront's HEIGHTMAP matches the real one; no roads/buildings, just topography). Implies sourcing real elevation/DEM data (USGS / SRTM / Copernicus) keyed to the container's lat/long. A teaching/familiarity feature (players recognize their real surroundings in the sim) that ties the container-model's location data directly to world-gen.
recent_decisions.why: Concrete, somewhat-blocking operator ask: they livestream, and the Real/Play consolidation would remove their only Studio entry point. Putting Studio in the chat right rail (where it also lives on the website — dual-UI parity) DECOUPLES livestream access from the top nav, so the condense can proceed without stranding the operator. Mirrored the existing collapsible-section + config-persist pattern (chat_members_collapsed) rather than inventing new state plumbing; used a theme token for the header so the new section is both cohesive and lint-clean. Go Live/End Stream mirror the Studio page's own flags so state never diverges. Recorded the Earth-container + real-terrain visions in the journal IMMEDIATELY (cross-session persistence rule — durable design must hit disk the moment it's given, since the session may not get a clean end) because they refine the load-bearing container-tree architecture (the root is a planet; the model is unchanged) and set a concrete world-gen target (real DEM data keyed to the container's coordinates) — both shape the Real-page + inventory-tree arc still in flight.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.349.0 — INVENTORY as a TREE (operator GREENLIT the uniform container-node model: 'Let's move forward with your idea for the inventory. That makes great sense.'). NEW widgets::tree_list + TreeNode — a recursive node (id [empty=not selectable], label, detail, children): the reusable nested-container view. Branches use egui CollapsingState (expand/collapse + auto-indent); leaves are rows, clickable if they carry an id; tree_list returns the clicked leaf's id. The inventory Backpack GRID is REPLACED by tree_list — each item a selectable leaf (id = slot index, detail = xN); clicking sets selected_slot so the EXISTING right-side detail panel (Eat/Drink/Use) keeps working with no rewire. DUPLICATION FLAGGED (transparency, since the operator cares about universal widgets): there is ALREADY a closure-based tree widget (widgets/tree.rs: TreeState + tree_node + tree_leaf, used by the Files page); my data-driven tree_list is a SECOND tree widget. Renamed my private helper tree_node -> container_node to dodge the name clash. RECONCILIATION TRACKED: collapse to ONE universal tree — likely migrate Files onto the data-driven tree_list (build node data, render it; the same data feeds a real OR a sim inventory — the firewall is just which ROOT you pass) and retire the closure-based one. cargo check --features native + 386/386 lib tests clean. This is STEP 1 of the merge-pages arc (tree_list -> merged 'You' page + TOC sidebar -> Real/Play page split). REMAINING for the inventory tree: deep container nesting (a pouch inside the bag) needs the ECS to model containers-within-containers (currently flat slots); variable-icon = detail-level + compact toggle (the operator's vision) are v2 refinements of the widget.
recent_decisions.why: Operator greenlit the uniform container-node inventory, so I built it. The data-driven TreeNode (build the tree, render it) fits the operator's vision better than the existing closure-based tree.rs — it's the SAME data whether it renders a real or a sim inventory, so the real/sim firewall is just which root node you hand the widget (matches the 'separate pages, same widget' plan). Kept the existing detail panel working by routing tree-item clicks through selected_slot (minimal change, no action regression). Flagged the two-tree-widgets duplication openly rather than hiding it; the right reconciliation (one universal tree, migrate Files to it) is a tracked follow-up I'll pitch, because shipping two trees silently would violate the universal-widget rule the operator just reinforced.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.348.0 — styled square stepper buttons + clean manifest columns (operator UI asks; the drone manifest confirmed working: 'exact values showed up'). NEW widgets::stepper_button: a SQUARE (width=height) +/- button, state-coloured — enabled '+' = green border (theme.success) + very-dark-green fill (derived from the token, theme-exempt) + white glyph; enabled '-' = red (theme.danger); DISABLED = flat dark-gray, glyph included. The drone manifest rows are now clean ALIGNED columns (ore | available | [-] value [+]) instead of the inline/staggered layout ('get rid of the stepping' read as the staggered look, NOT the +/- buttons, which the operator kept + asked to style). Fixed egui 0.31 Painter::rect needing a 5th StrokeKind arg. cargo check --features native clean. DESIGN DECISIONS this round (to drive the merge-pages arc): (1) REAL/SIM = SEPARATE PAGES, NOT the top-right toggle — operator's call: people forget toggles (a real trust/safety risk for confusing game vs real money/inventory/home-sensor data), so real-self + sim-self are DISTINCT pages/routes; the PAGE itself is the firewall, not a mode you can forget mid-use. The Real/Sim toggle gets dropped. (2) NESTING MODEL — my 'better way' to pitch: a UNIFORM CONTAINER-NODE tree (every node can hold items AND sub-containers; 'entities' — you / home / vehicle / character — are just top-level container nodes), recursive + infinite-of-X, instead of special-casing Context/Entity/Container/Item tiers. With real/sim as separate PAGES, each page IS a root container, so 'Context' isn't a tree level at all. (3) TOC: a left-side section-nav panel on the merged infinite-scroll page (operator agreed) — extend the Profile page's existing section sidebar. Build order: reusable tree_list (uniform nodes) -> merged 'You' page (collapsible sections + TOC sidebar) -> the real/sim page split.
recent_decisions.why: Button styling + column cleanup were clear, concrete operator asks — shipped. Real/sim-as-separate-pages is the operator's decision and the SAFER design: a forgettable toggle is a genuine trust risk for the mission (the whole point is people never confuse simulation for reality, esp. money/sensors/home), while a page you deliberately navigate to can't be forgotten mid-use. Pitched the uniform-container nesting because it's more elegant + extensible than tiered special-casing — a container holds items AND containers, recursively, which is the infinite-of-X ideal — but flagged it as a proposal for the operator's nod since the model is the load-bearing decision. TOC reuses the existing Profile section-sidebar pattern rather than inventing a new nav.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.347.0 — two quick UI fixes from operator play (the stat rows + drone manifest got a positive read: 'much better, much easier to read everything'): (1) the drone-manifest +/- steppers were flush-RIGHT (Layout::right_to_left), so the '+' landed UNDER the page scrollbar -> moved them LEFT after a fixed-width 150px ore column (they align + stay clear of the scrollbar); (2) removed the 'N/36 slots' inventory-header counter (the slot grid is being replaced by the nested-list inventory, so a fixed slot count is going away). DESIGN DIRECTION the operator raised (NOT yet built — in active discussion): merge the separate character-info pages (Profile/Inventory/Skills/Quests/...) into ONE infinite-scroll 'self/character' page (the Inventory page already carries the vital stat bars). HARD CONSTRAINT (a load-bearing MISSION/trust principle): REAL-life data and SIM/GAME data must NEVER share a page or view, so no one can confuse game money / inventory / home-sensor data for their real money / inventory / home. The TENSION: the SAME data MODEL (inventory, stats, sensors) is needed for BOTH real + sim, because the sim TEACHES real-world skills by mirroring real structures. PROPOSED RESOLUTION (to pitch): one shared data MODEL + the EXISTING Real/Sim toggle as the hard CONTEXT switch (only ever one dataset shown) + a STRONG, persistent VISUAL mode indicator (color/banner) so which context you're in is unmistakable. Naming is open — operator is wary of 'Player' (wants zero real/sim confusion). This merged-page direction + the nested-list inventory tree are one connected arc.
recent_decisions.why: The steppers/scrollbar overlap + the slots counter were quick, clear, operator-pointed fixes — ship immediately. The merge-pages + real/sim separation is a big architectural direction the operator is actively shaping ('what do you think?'); recorded here because 'real-vs-sim data never on one page' is a load-bearing trust principle for the mission (the whole point is people can rely on the platform without confusing simulation for reality, esp. money/sensors/home), and it shapes BOTH the inventory-tree arc AND the character-page design. Engaging it in the reply + proposing the shared-model + Real/Sim-toggle + unmistakable-mode-indicator resolution BEFORE building, since it's a real architecture fork.

recent_decisions.decision: DRONE MANIFEST v0.346.0 — operator design ask #2 from live play: ONE drone per player + capacity ALLOCATION (was: each click spawned a single-ore drone, queueable). Refactored the Drone component from a single ore (ore_id/target/cargo:u32) to a MANIFEST: manifest: Vec<(ore,units)> (sums to <= capacity) + cargo: Vec<(ore,units)> + cargo_total(). DroneSystem: the commission channel is now Option<Vec<(ore,units)>>; ONE drone per player (skip a new launch if one is already in flight); the Mining apply pulls EACH manifest ore from whatever asteroid(s) hold it (multi-asteroid) into cargo; Deliver adds all cargo to the home inventory + awards Mining XP for the total. DRONE_CAPACITY -> pub u32 = 10 (the UI cap). Tests rewritten: commission_manifest_mines_and_delivers, multi_ore_manifest_collects_each_ore (iron from one asteroid + copper from another), one_drone_per_player. UI (inventory Mining panel): a MANIFEST BUILDER when no drone is out — a +/- stepper per available ore (capped so total <= 10) + a SEGMENTED CAPACITY BAR (manifest_bar: a track with one coloured segment per ore, width prop to its units) + Launch/Clear; while a drone IS out, the panel shows that drone's manifest + 3-stage + progress instead. GuiState: drone_manifest_draft (in-progress allocation) + pending_drone_manifest (launch) + drone_active (synced); GuiDrone: manifest + cargo_total. DESIGN CALLS: segmented capacity BAR over pie (the hold is a fixed 10-slot container, so a bar you fill reads 1:1; pie = abstract proportion); UNITS over % (5 iron / 4 nickel is clearer than 50/40 for 10 discrete slots); STEPPERS over draggable dividers for v1 (the divider-drag is the polish follow-up). Went ahead with segmented per the keep-shipping directive rather than blocking on the segmented-vs-pie question I'd raised (reversible; the backend carries over regardless of viz). VERIFIED 386/386 native lib (3 new mining tests) + relay cargo check clean + native check clean.
recent_decisions.why: Operator gave a clear direction (1 drone, capacity allocation, simple->deep: one ore fills the hold OR a custom mix). The manifest model (multi-ore, one drone) is the core they described; only the viz was a real fork, which I recommended (segmented) + built (reversible). Units not % because the hold is 10 DISCRETE slots — direct + no rounding. Steppers for v1 keep it functional + clear without the fiddly shared-bar drag (deferred to a polish pass). Per-ore pull across asteroids matches 'the drone goes and gets it.' One-per-player (their explicit ask) makes the drone a managed asset, not spam. Followed keep-shipping over re-asking the fork since it's reversible + most of the work (component/system/tests/bridge) is viz-independent.

recent_decisions.decision: STAT-ROW WIDGET + vitals panel redesign v0.345.0 — first of the operator's UI-design-collab asks from their live play (after the v0.344.0 registry fix unblocked everything). New reusable widgets::stat_row(name, value, value_color, frac, fill) draws ONE compact row [name col | value col | thin 6px bar] with FIXED SHARED column widths (STAT_NAME_W=86 / STAT_VALUE_W=82) so stacked rows align into a table — replacing the old 2-rows-per-stat (a label row, then a full-height ProgressBar below). Thin bar via egui::ProgressBar.desired_height(6.0) (no new dep). Applied to the inventory page's Weight + all 5 vitals (Satiation/Hydration/Energy/Oxygen/Waste); Body temp is a matching readout line on the same columns (it's a temperature, not a 0..100 bar) + the Sealed/EXPOSED status; the Rest + Compost buttons moved to a clean action row decoupled from the bars. cargo check --features native clean. DEFERRED (operator design forks — I asked, awaiting steer): user-DRAGGABLE column resize (needs egui_extras::TableBuilder — the same foundation the inventory tree wants); the DRONE manifest (1-drone-per-player + capacity ALLOCATION — I recommended a segmented capacity bar with draggable dividers over a pie chart, since the hold is a fixed container; simple=click-an-ore-fills-capacity, deep=drag dividers for a mix); the nested-list INVENTORY (a reusable tree_list widget: categories character/structure/vehicle, nested containers, variable icon size = per-row detail level like chat avatars, compact toggle, virtualized for scale). SYNTHESIS told to the operator: the stat table + inventory tree + drone manifest are the SAME primitive (compact data-deep rows + shared resizable columns + optional icon/segmented bar) -> build as universal widgets, not one-offs.
recent_decisions.why: The operator's first real play turned into a design dialogue ('what do you think? any way better?'). The stat-rows were the smallest, most concrete, NO-FORK ask (explicit spec: 5px bars, one row, shared columns), so I built them first while the bigger items (drone manifest, inventory tree) await the operator's answer on the forks I raised (segmented-bar-vs-pie; tree detail-level model). Used the no-new-dep thin-bar approach + fixed shared widths for alignment now; user-drag resize (egui_extras) is deferred and bundled with the future tree widget that needs the same. Kept the increment focused on vitals/weight (not every bar in the app) so it ships fast and the operator can react to the new compact look before I convert more or go deep on the bigger arcs.

recent_decisions.decision: CRITICAL FIX v0.344.0 — found + fixed the REAL root cause of the operator's empty-registries symptoms (raw item ids in inventory, Dev:stock no-op, empty skill sheet, quest shown by raw id 'gs_first_steps'). The ECS data registries (item/recipe/plant/status_effect/skill/quest/container) were loaded LAZILY inside load_world(), which is called ONLY at lib.rs:3552 `if active_page==GuiPage::None (the 3D world view) && !world_loaded`. The operator plays ENTIRELY through the MENU pages (Inventory/Crafting/Profile), so load_world NEVER ran -> registries stayed empty. The game systems + player + asteroids are set up in resumed() at STARTUP, so MINING worked (code-driven asteroids + DroneSystem) while EVERYTHING data-driven was empty -> the exact symptom cluster. My two prior fixes (v0.343.0 find_data_dir repo-preference + extraction-list) were NECESSARY-BUT-INSUFFICIENT: the data dir was ALREADY correct (C:\Humanity\data — just launch runs the archived exe beside the repo data), so they never touched the actual bug. FIX: extracted the registry loading into a shared fn load_data_registries(store, data_dir) + call it EAGERLY in resumed() (next to the eager GUI-catalog loads) so registries populate at startup before any system ticks; load_world still calls it (idempotent) for the 3D path. VERIFIED EMPIRICALLY by running the release exe with RUST_LOG=info: at STARTUP it now logs 'Loaded item_registry/recipe_registry/plant_registry/status_effect_registry/skill_registry ... Loaded 11 quest definitions ... Loaded ContainerRegistry: 10 types' — previously these appeared only after entering the 3D world. cargo check --features native clean. LESSON 1: a lazy world-load gated the entire DATA layer behind a UI state (active_page==None) that the menu-driven gameplay never reaches; cheap registries (CSV/RON) must load EAGERLY at startup, only the heavy 3D mesh gen should be lazy. LESSON 2: I should have RUN THE BINARY to verify the first two fixes instead of reasoning the data dir was the cause — the raw-item-id symptom (item_registry empty even with the correct data dir) was the tell I misread; two round-trips were wasted before I ran it + read the logs.
recent_decisions.why: The operator was blocked across THREE versions; empty registries broke every data-driven loop (names, recipes, skills, quests) while mining worked (code-driven), which is the fingerprint of 'systems run but data absent'. The lazy-load gate (active_page==None) is invisible from the menu-driven play the whole game is built around. Loading the tiny registries eagerly (a few ms of parsing) at startup has no meaningful cost and fixes the menu path; keeping the heavy mesh gen lazy preserves chat-first instant startup. Extracted to ONE shared fn (single source) so the eager + lazy call sites can't drift. Crucially VERIFIED by running the exe + reading the logs rather than reasoning — the explicit lesson from two insufficient fixes is to confirm empirically before declaring a fix.

recent_decisions.decision: PLAY-TEST FIXES v0.343.0 (operator's FIRST live play of the gameplay arc).
