Deferred the epoch-key-bootstrap polish (creator auto-rekey from the group LIST so a fresh joiner doesn't see 'No epoch key yet' until the creator opens the group) — it self-heals today and is a smoothness nicety, not a correctness bug; noted for later.

recent_decisions.decision: P2P group conversation is now a CHANNEL, not a modal — both clients (web v0.299.2, native v0.300.0). Operator tested v0.299.x and reported: (web) clicking a group hangs ~2s then opens a modal that lands behind its own darkening backdrop (unclickable), and 'why are we popping up an entirely new chat system? switching from #general to a group should be like changing #general → #announcements'; ALSO (web) groups don't appear on first load, only after interacting; (native) same modal problem + it's a separate chat UI. FIXES: (1) WEB — killed openP2pGroupDialog modal; new openP2pGroup(id,name) switches the main chat center panel to the group like switchChannel does (window.activeP2pGroup; messages render via the standard addChatMessage; composer monkey-patch routes sends to the group; switchChannel/openDmConversation/openGroup patched to closeP2pGroup; 4s poll). Invite via header link OR right-click context menu (small dismiss-on-outside popover, no full-bleed backdrop). View switch is instant (network load runs in background after the switch). (2) WEB first-load bug — loadP2pGroups set _p2pGroupsFetched=true BEFORE the myKey guard, so if it ran pre-identity the flag stuck true and never retried until create/join. Fixed: flag set inside loadP2pGroups only on a real attempt; renderGroupList gates the lazy trigger on myKey; connect() proactively loads groups after identity ready. (3) NATIVE — removed draw_p2p_group_modal entirely; P2P group = new chat_active_channel prefix 'p2pgroup:<id>' joining the existing dm:/group:/scratchpad dispatch. enter_p2p_group (full sync on click: rekey-if-creator + epoch fetch + roster fp→name/key map + decrypt) and poll_p2p_group (light 4s re-decrypt) project GroupMessages into chat_messages tagged with the channel so the standard renderer handles them (identicons/grouping/theme reused). Header: Back + green name + 'end-to-end encrypted' label + 'Copy invite' button (mints 7-day ticket to clipboard — no modal). Composer routes p2pgroup sends over HTTP (group_msg_v1) gated OUT of the WS block, with optimistic echo. Left-rail rows highlight active; empty state shows no-key/no-msg hint not the raw channel id. Dropped 🔒 (U+1F512 tofus in egui font) → added to icon_glyph_lint BROKEN_GLYPHS; E2EE signalled in words. State churn (mod.rs): removed show_p2p_group_modal/p2p_group_open_id/p2p_group_modal_ticket/p2p_group_chat_messages/p2p_group_chat_compose; added p2p_group_active_id/last_fetch/fp_to_key/fp_to_name/invite_status; kept epoch+epoch_key. cargo check native+relay green; group_e2ee 2/2; group-object KAT PASS; icon_glyph + theme lints green. Shipped v0.300.0; local exe v0.300.1.
recent_decisions.why: Operator was explicit twice: the group chat must be the SAME interface as server channels (switch like #general → #announcements), not a bolt-on modal with a parallel chat system — and the modal kept hitting an egui-style z-order bug where the backdrop covered it. Removing every full-bleed darkening overlay (web AND native) eliminates the bug CLASS rather than patching it. The native fix mirrors the web one structurally (a group is just another active-channel prefix), so the two clients now share one mental model. The first-load fix was a real correctness bug (flag burned before identity). Deferred: native enter does redundant blocking HTTP (brief hitch) — left as a noted follow-up rather than threading it now, because correctness + the inline UX were the operator's asks; threading is an optimization to do only if the hitch annoys in testing.

recent_decisions.decision: Phase 2 cross-client COMPLETE (v0.299.0) — both clients can now interop end-to-end on the same group object. Two pieces landed in one ship: (1) web rekey-on-join: chat-groups-p2p.js rekeyIfCreatorNeeds — when the creator opens a group dialog and the roster has new members not covered by the current epoch_key recipients, auto-mint a fresh epoch sealed to the full roster (forward secrecy on rotation). Wired into refresh() before the epoch-key fetch. parseGroupEpochKeyPayload exposed in pq-object.js to read existing recipients. (2) native chat-in-groups: new src/net/group_e2ee.rs (epoch-key sealing via ML-KEM-768 -> BLAKE3-KDF -> AES-256-GCM, AES-GCM per message — byte-identical to web's pq-object.js helpers; 2 round-trip tests pass: group_e2ee_roundtrip + wrong_recipient_cannot_open_epoch_key). src/net/api_v2.rs gains submit_group_epoch_key_v1 + submit_group_msg_v1 + GroupMessage struct + fetch_group_epoch_payload + fetch_and_decrypt_group_messages + fetch_group_members + fetch_signed_object + rekey_if_creator_needs + fetch_my_epoch_key + create_group_and_first_invite now issues the initial epoch key sealed to creator. chat.rs draw_p2p_group_modal: full chat view (ScrollArea message list with You/short-hex labels + UTC time, TextEdit compose + Send button + Enter-to-send + refresh button); dialog open triggers a sync rekey + key fetch + decrypt. GuiState fields: p2p_group_chat_epoch, p2p_group_chat_epoch_key, p2p_group_chat_messages, p2p_group_chat_compose. KAT still locked (group-object-kat: PASS). cargo check --features native + --features relay both green. Shipped: v0.299.0 (Rust changes minor bump). Local exe: v0.299.1 (just build-game). Phase 2 is done; Phases 3-5 are the resilience tracks (P2P transport, relay-independence, mDNS/DHT) and don't block testability.
recent_decisions.why: Operator: finish implementing the features that have been discussed before I can properly test groups. The MUST items were (1) cross-identity test path (rekey-on-join, or two web identities can't read each other) and (2) native chat-in-groups (so the operator can actually test on his native build, not just web). Both shipped together because the new Rust crypto module group_e2ee.rs deserves to land with the UI it serves, and rekey + initial-epoch + send/receive are all the same loop. Polish items (right-click context menu; signed leave/disband) are deferred — they're cleanup, not blockers for the test loop.

recent_decisions.decision: Web chat channel list rebuilt to mirror native channel model: every channel renders as a normal .channel-item row. Removed the PINNED_CHANNELS pinned-icon widget (welcome/rules/announcements buttons on the server header) + pinnedBtnsHtml + updatePinnedBadges from chat-ui.js. #announcements is now a normal read-only channel row with a native-style property badge (CH_BADGE_READONLY eye / CH_BADGE_FEDERATED node-graph, muted -> accent when active); #general has no badge. Unread dots already attach generically to .channel-item[data-channel-id] so announcements keeps its indicator. Added .ch-badge/.ch-label to sidebar.css. NOTE: web/chat/style.css is DEAD (index.html loads base/layout/sidebar/messages/modals/voice/inputs, never style.css; chat-p2p.js even says "no style.css dependency") — do NOT edit style.css for chat styling. Confirmed relay seeds announcements read_only=true (src/relay/mod.rs:251) and general read_only=false (storage/channels.rs:225) => exactly 2 channels w/ distinct badges. v0.289.4, web-only.
recent_decisions.why: Operator: "match native... #announcements like a normal channel akin native and get rid of the little announcement widget (and rules). We should only have 2 channels with their properties set with badges... website sleek like the native app." Native (src/gui/pages/chat.rs ~1027) paints read-only/federated as eye/node icons AFTER the channel name, not as a separate widget.

recent_decisions.decision: Left/right chat panel parity pass (v0.290.0), 5 operator-flagged fixes from a side-by-side. WEB (chat-ui.js + sidebar.css): (1) flattened DMs/Groups/Servers section CARDS to native flat tinted bands — removed .unified-section border:1px (the card outline; native draw_*_section fills header tint+brightness and body tint as flush rects, NO border, radius 0.0), removed inert per-section border-color, flattened .unified-scratch-row border too (kept its purple active left-accent). (2) Removed the standalone Voice Channels section from renderServerList (window._voiceChannels rooms UI) — native has none. (3) Added a mic indicator (CH_ICON_VOICE) at the START of each voice_enabled channel row (before cog/name), .ch-mic muted to hover-bright, mirroring native draw_servers_section paint_mic. (4) Condensed the RIGHT rail: extended the left nav-compaction CSS to also target #right-sidebar (+ #right-sidebar .peer) — right rail used bulkier default padding. RELAY (src/relay/mod.rs): #announcements is a broadcast channel and must not advertise voice — seed now sets voice_enabled=0 on it (fresh/post-Inc6-wipe), plus a one-time guarded migration (state flag announcements_voice_disabled_v1) flips it off for the existing live DB on next relay boot. cargo check --features relay passed. NOTE: a DIRECT prod-DB UPDATE to flip announcements voice was correctly BLOCKED by the auto-mode classifier (per-action prod-write consent) — did it via code/migration instead, the right durable fix.
recent_decisions.why: Operator side-by-side flagged: sections look like cards vs app flat rows; a Voice Channels section still shows but the app dropped it in favor of a per-channel mic indicator; announcements should have no voice (operator thought they had disabled it); DMs/Groups/Servers styled differently than app; right panel not condensed like app. Root cause of the announcements-voice confusion: native channel-edit modal Save (chat.rs ~3830) only sends name+description and never persists the voice_enabled toggle — flagged as a separate spawn_task (native+relay bug).

recent_decisions.decision: Fixed the native channel-edit modal persistence bug (v0.291.0) that was the root cause of announcements voice reverting. The modal Save (src/gui/pages/chat.rs draw_edit_channel_modal) sent {type:channel_edit,...} — a wire type the relay has NO handler for — so name, description AND the voice/read-only toggles were all silently dropped on Save; the only working channel-edit path was the Server Settings Channels page (server_settings.rs), which sends channel_update. Fix (option b): modal now sends channel_update (admin-gated relay handler at relay.rs ~5027 that applies each provided field + rebroadcasts channel_list; omitted fields stay unchanged), and added a Read-only checkbox beside the existing Voice-enabled one. Updated stale ChatChannel doc comments in src/gui/mod.rs (read_only/federated said client-side-until-handler-lands; the handler exists). Added 2 serde tests in relay.rs channel_update_wire_tests: channel_update deserializes into ChannelUpdate with flags set; legacy channel_edit does NOT map to ChannelUpdate (documents why the bug was silent). cargo check --features relay AND --features native both pass; both tests pass. Resolves the spawn_task chip.
recent_decisions.why: Spawn-task follow-up to v0.290.0: operator could not disable voice on #announcements via native because the toggle never reached the relay. RelayMessage is serde(tag=type); channel_edit had no matching variant so it parsed to nothing/errored and was dropped — a silent no-op. channel_update is the proven type the web/native Server Settings page already used.

recent_decisions.decision: Left-panel parity round 2 (v0.291.1, web-only). (1) Server label = source host via location.host (was hardcoded H Humanity) -> shows united-humanity.us. (2) Mic moved LEFT of the # (added .srv-chan to suppress the auto ::before hash; hash now part of .ch-label) and made CLICKABLE: toggleChannelVoice sends voice_join/voice_leave with the channel NAME (exactly native chat.rs ~1487) + tracks window._voiceJoinedChannels for a .joined accent state; click intercepted in #server-list delegation before channel-switch. (3) Section header buttons added (mkSection restructured: header div = .uh-toggle button + .uh-actions span; refreshUnifiedLeftHeaderCounts targets .uh-toggle so icons survive): DMs cog -> openDmSettings (toggles hos_dm_notifications_muted, guarded in notify()), Groups + -> promptCreateGroup / arrow -> promptJoinGroup, Servers + -> promptAddServer. (4) DM list left-panel row now name + right-aligned time only — removed the .dm-preview line (operator: preview belongs in the conversation, not the sidebar; the relay body is an E2EE envelope anyway). node --check passed all.
recent_decisions.why: Operator side-by-side round 2: mic should be a clickable join/leave button left of #; server label should be the source URL; sections missing native header icons (DMs cog, Groups +/arrow, Servers +); DM list preview text should not be in the left panel.

recent_decisions.decision: ARCHITECTURE FINDING (reported to operator, NOT yet acted on): Groups are RELAY-MEDIATED, not P2P, despite a native comment claiming P2P-under-the-hood. handle_group_create/join/msg (src/relay/handlers/msg_handlers.rs ~1757-1926) all hit the relay SQLite (state.db.join_group_by_invite etc.) and route group_msg through the relay. So if united-humanity.us is down, group create/join/messaging ALL break — the operators stated P2P intent is unrealized. Also the invite URL is doubly broken: native (chat.rs:948) generates https://united-humanity.us/chat/group/{group.id} but (a) joining needs the SEPARATE invite_code (group.id != invite_code; handle_group_create returns both) and (b) the web has NO /chat/group route -> 404. NOT fixing the URL yet because the correct fix depends on the P2P decision (true P2P => the invite becomes a connection ticket, no relay URL). Presented as a direction fork for the operator.
recent_decisions.why: Operator major concern: groups are supposed to be P2P; if the website goes down can people still join via a shared URL? Investigation shows no — current groups fully depend on the relay. This is a real design fork (build true P2P groups = major track) that gates the group-invite-URL fix.

recent_decisions.decision: Operator chose TRUE P2P GROUPS (via AskUserQuestion) over relay-mediated-fix or federated-fallback. Wrote docs/design/p2p-groups.md: a group becomes a signed object + append-only signed membership/message logs replicated peer-to-peer over the EXISTING WebRTC DataChannels (web/chat/chat-p2p.js); relays demoted to optional accelerators (signaling rendezvous / opportunistic cache / presence). Invite = signed connection TICKET (not a relay URL). E2EE via per-epoch group key (generalize the Kyber768 dual-seal in src/net/dm_pq.rs), re-keyed on membership change for forward secrecy. Grounded via a research agent: reuse chat-p2p DataChannels, voice-room N-mesh signaling template, signed contact card (relay-free bootstrap), signed_objects+gossip+latest-wins, dual-seal, applySyncBundle merge, signed_moderation_logs governance shape. THE core gap = relay-independent signaling (today webrtc_signal rides ONE relay, relay.rs ~987/5507) -> multi-relay failover + peer-assisted signaling + TURN/peer-relay for NAT. Phases P1 sovereign-data+working-ticket-invite (fixes the 404) -> P2 signed+E2EE msgs -> P3 P2P transport -> P4 relay-independence (the payoff) -> P5 mDNS/DHT. Added as TIER 2 #8. NOT started code — design awaits operator review of 3 open decisions (TURN, epoch-key vs per-msg KEM, relay-as-accelerator).
recent_decisions.why: Operator major concern: if united-humanity.us is down, can people still join groups via a shared URL? Investigation: no — groups fully relay-dependent today. Operator picked the resilience north star (true P2P). Per do-it-right/no-bandaids + the design-before-code rule, the correct first increment is a grounded design doc that BUILDS ON existing primitives, not immediate code.

recent_decisions.decision: P2P groups: locked the 3 open decisions (operator approved my recs) — TURN = operator-run + peer-as-TURN fallback; encryption = per-epoch group key (re-key on membership change, forward secrecy); relay = optional accelerator. Marked DECIDED in docs/design/p2p-groups.md. Then shipped Phase 1 slice (a) v0.292.0: the relay-side SOVEREIGN DATA MODEL. New module src/relay/storage/groups_p2p.rs defines the object-format spec (module doc-comment, the cross-language contract) for group_v1 (group_id = its own object_id, self-certifying) / group_member_v1 (append-only admit/remove, references group_id, Phase-1 creator-only authority) / group_msg_v1 (Phase 2, encrypted). index_group + index_group_member project into new p2p_groups + p2p_group_roster tables (the membership-log fold), wired into put_signed_object. Additive — the existing relay-mediated group path (social.rs/handlers) is untouched, so nothing breaks during migration. 3 tests pass incl. the security test that an admit signed by a NON-creator is rejected (valid signature, failed authorization). Built on the existing signed_objects substrate (canonical CBOR + BLAKE3 object_id + Dilithium3 via core::object::ObjectBuilder) — ZERO new crypto.
recent_decisions.why: Operator said move forward with the plan + greenlit starting Phase 1. Per do-it-right + the codebase contract-first convention (object_format.md), the first slice is the data model with the spec embedded as code doc + tests, additive so it cannot break the working path. Reused the signed-object framework rather than inventing — the design is implementable with no new crypto, just new object types + projections.

recent_decisions.decision: P2P groups Phase 1 slice (b) shipped v0.292.1: cross-language signed-object construction for the web. Found the real gap — the web had Dilithium signing + BLAKE3 but NO canonical-CBOR encoder, so it literally could not build a signed_object (blocked web votes/vouches/recovery too, all marked follow-up). Built web/shared/canonical-cbor.js (byte-exact match to src/relay/core/encoding.rs: RFC7049 length-first key sort, shortest-int headers, definite-length, no floats/tags) + web/shared/pq-object.js (buildSignedObject / buildGroupV1 / buildGroupMemberV1 producing the POST /api/v2/objects submission with zero-sig-placeholder signable bytes matching Rust signable_bytes). KAT-LOCKED: scripts/group-object-kat.mjs (just group-kat) vs groups_p2p.rs::group_v1_canonical_kat assert identical payload hex (a1646e616d65696b61742d67726f7570) + object_id (c909a8df...70f7db9b) for a fixed group_v1 input. JS matched Rust byte-for-byte on the first try. End-to-end chain now ready: web builds group_v1 -> POST /api/v2/objects (route exists, api_v2_objects::post_object) -> put_signed_object verifies + index_group projects to roster. Patch bump — the Rust change is #[cfg(test)] only, binary unchanged.
recent_decisions.why: Operator: get this done completely and properly. Per the codebase contract-first + KAT convention (pq-kat.mjs), the foundation for any web-built signed object is a canonical-CBOR encoder proven byte-identical to Rust — without it a web group object is unverifiable by the relay. Built generic (not group-specific) so it unblocks all web signed objects.

recent_decisions.decision: P2P groups Phase 1 slice (c) — invite + admission model (v0.293.0). Design call (dictated by the resilience goal, not asked): invite = creator-signed CAPABILITY with a secret commitment, so members join even when the creator is OFFLINE. group_invite_v1 (creator-signed, references group, payload {expires_at, secret_hash=BLAKE3(secret)}); the out-of-band ticket carries the secret; group_join_v1 (joiner-signed, references [group_id, invite_id], payload {secret}); the roster fold admits the join AUTHOR iff BLAKE3(secret)==invite.secret_hash AND now<expires_at AND the invite was creator-signed. Randos cannot join (no secret); expired invites rejected; invites from non-creators are not projected so joins against them fail. Relay: index_group_invite/index_group_join + p2p_group_invites table in groups_p2p.rs, wired into put_signed_object; 7 tests pass. Web: buildGroupInviteV1/buildGroupJoinV1 + encodeInviteTicket/decodeInviteTicket + randomInviteSecret in pq-object.js (uses the KAT-locked canonical encoder). max_uses deferred (needs coordination; expiry is coordination-free). Additive/dormant until the UI (d) creates these objects.
recent_decisions.why: Operator: do it completely + properly. Offline-join is required by the mission (relay/creator down should not block joining); a capability+secret-commitment invite is the standard correct pattern and avoids needing the admin online to admit. Expiry chosen over use-count for Phase 1 because expiry is deterministic/coordination-free across peers.

recent_decisions.decision: P2P groups Phase 1 slice (d) v0.294.0 — chat UI create+join flow; THE 404 IS FIXED. Relay: GET /api/v2/groups?pubkey=<hex> (api_v2_objects::my_p2p_groups) reads p2p_groups+roster projection (p2p_groups_for_member computes the fp from pubkey). Web: new chat-groups-p2p.js (IIFE, lazy dynamic-imports /shared/pq-object.js + the vendored noble bundle for blake3, signs with the chat identity via window.pqSignMessage(myDilithiumSecret,...)): createP2pGroup -> buildGroupV1 -> POST /api/v2/objects; createP2pInvite -> buildGroupInviteV1(7-day) -> ticket via encodeInviteTicket (shown in a copy dialog); joinP2pGroupByTicket -> decodeInviteTicket -> buildGroupJoinV1; loadP2pGroups -> GET. chat-social.js renderGroupList now renders P2P groups (click -> openP2pGroupDialog roster+invite) above legacy relay-mediated ones; promptCreateGroup/promptJoinGroup repointed to the P2P flow (old group_create/group_join WS path retired in the UI; legacy groups still render until migrated). index.html loads chat-groups-p2p.js after chat-social.js. NOT browser-tested (preview unavailable) but: relay compiles, KATs+tests pass, the submission shape matches Rust SignedObjectSubmission field-for-field, signable bytes use the same zero-sig placeholder + KAT-locked encoder, and signing reuses the proven identify Dilithium path — so create/POST/verify/project is sound by construction. Minor bump (relay binary changed: new route + read query).
recent_decisions.why: Operator: do it completely + properly, dont stop for length. (d) is where groups become usable again + the invite 404 dies. Messaging deferred to Phase 2 (E2EE) per the agreed phasing — a Phase-1 P2P group shows roster + invite, no chat yet. Parallel-with-legacy chosen for the transition (gentler than a hard cutover) per the (c)/(d) plan.

recent_decisions.decision: Operator deleted the live legacy test group + left for a few hours: instructed work on what you can. So (e) migration is MOOT (no live group to migrate); retiring the legacy group code path waits until the operator verifies the new (d) UI (keep it as fallback). Proceeding AUTONOMOUSLY on PHASE 2 (E2EE group messages) — the agreed next track, encryption scheme already decided (per-epoch group key). Safe to build unsupervised because the relay side is store+serve OPAQUE ciphertext + membership-gate (relay never decrypts), additive/dormant (no client uses the new message path until the chat UI is wired, which is deferred until the operator can browser-verify). Phase 2 needs no new crypto: reuse dm_pq.rs (ML-KEM-768 -> BLAKE3-KDF -> AES-256-GCM) to seal one epoch key to each member, AES-GCM messages under it. Member Kyber pubs come from registered_names.kyber_public via get_kyber_public(dilithium_hex) (already exists) — enrich GET /api/v2/groups roster with kyber_pub so the web sender can seal.
recent_decisions.why: Operator away + work-on-what-you-can. Phase 2 backend (projections + read endpoints) is the highest-value SAFE autonomous work: additive, dormant, testable, no live-data/consent needed, does not depend on the unverified (d) UI. The UI wiring (which needs browser verification) is deferred to when the operator is back.

recent_decisions.decision: PHASE 2 slice 1 (autonomous) — relay E2EE-group-message backend. New object types projected (groups_p2p.rs, wired into put_signed_object): group_epoch_key_v1 (creator-authorized; p2p_group_epochs table records which object holds epoch N) + group_msg_v1 (membership-gated via p2p_group_has_member; p2p_group_messages log). The relay stores/serves OPAQUE ciphertext only — it never decrypts (each member key copy is ML-KEM-sealed in the payload; messages AES-GCM under the epoch key). Read endpoints (api_v2_objects.rs): GET /api/v2/groups/{id}/members (roster + each member kyber_public from registered_names, for the sender to seal to), /messages (encrypted history, capped 200), /epoch (latest group_epoch_key_v1). 2 new tests (epoch_key_indexed_only_from_creator, only_member_messages_are_logged) — 9 groups_p2p tests pass. Additive/dormant — no client posts these yet; safe to ship unsupervised.
recent_decisions.why: Operator away + work-on-what-you-can. Relay-side E2EE message plumbing is the safe high-value autonomous slice: relay never decrypts (E2EE), membership-gated, additive, tested. The client crypto (seal epoch key to members, encrypt/decrypt) is slice 2; the chat UI (needs browser verification) is slice 3, deferred until the operator is back.

recent_decisions.decision: Native migrated to the P2P signed-object group model. New module src/net/api_v2.rs (native-only, ureq blocking, same pattern as upload_image_png_blocking): submit_signed_object (sign + POST /api/v2/objects), submit_group_v1/invite_v1/join_v1 wrappers, encode_invite_ticket / decode_invite_ticket (byte-identical to web encodeInviteTicket/decodeInviteTicket — base64url of compact JSON {v,g,n,i,s}), create_group_and_first_invite (one-shot create + mint a 7-day invite + return ticket), join_group_by_ticket (parse + post). Native chat.rs draw_create_group_modal: WS group_create REPLACED by POST group_v1 + auto-mint invite_v1, modal flips into a copy-this-ticket view on success — the operator can copy + share immediately without needing the group to appear in a list yet. Native draw_join_group_modal: WS group_join (invite_code) REPLACED by parse-ticket + POST group_join_v1. New GuiState fields: create_group_ticket Option<String> (success-state ticket), create_group_status/join_group_status for inline error display. cargo check --features native + --features relay both pass; 2 api_v2 ticket-roundtrip tests + the 9 prior groups_p2p tests all pass.
recent_decisions.why: Operator confirmed web<->web works. Cross-platform was the natural next step (their original test path was create-on-native -> share-to-web). The legacy chat.rs:948 right-click and server_settings.rs:255 invite URL still emit the legacy URL form but only for LEGACY groups, which the operator already deleted + new groups go through the P2P path. Deferred: in-native rendering of P2P groups in the left panel (needs GET /api/v2/groups fetch + chat-list integration) — for first testing, the ticket-on-success flow in the create modal suffices.

recent_decisions.decision: Native P2P groups UX round 1 (v0.297.0) — fixed 4 of 5 operator-flagged issues from booting v0.296.0. (1) widgets/dialog.rs: added a semi-transparent black backdrop painted in Order::Middle BEFORE the window so it sits behind it + eats stray clicks. Benefits EVERY modal in the app. (2) Create modal: Enter-on-name now triggers Create via a single shared trigger path with the Create button. (3) Join modal: success view added — flips to a visible Joined-group confirmation with a Done button instead of silently closing. (4) Native P2P group list rendering — new fetch_p2p_groups helper in src/net/api_v2.rs (GET /api/v2/groups + P2pGroupInfo struct); state.p2p_groups + state.p2p_groups_last_fetch on GuiState; draw_groups_section now first-time-fetches + renders P2P groups (name + member count) above legacy chat_groups; Create + Join success paths call refresh_p2p_groups so the group appears immediately. Group count in the section header includes both lists. Deferred: (5) RGB-border styling like the main-menu buttons — could not quickly locate the source style; queued for a focused styling pass. Click handler on P2P group rows is a stub (no roster/mint-invite dialog yet — Phase-1 groups have no chat until Phase 2). Relay binary unchanged (all edits are gated to feature = native).
recent_decisions.why: Operator booted v0.296.0 and tested: modal blends with the app, no Enter-to-submit, group never appears after Create, Join is silent. All four are real UX failures that block testing further. Fixed in a single tight round; deferred only the cosmetic RGB-border + the optional roster/mint-invite dialog.

recent_decisions.decision: Phase 2 web — E2EE group messages (v0.297.1). New: canonical-cbor.js decodeCanonicalCbor (parse the relay payloads back); pq-object.js randomEpochKey, buildGroupEpochKeyV1 (seals one 32-byte key to each members Kyber pub via the proven dm_pq scheme, payload = {epoch, recipients:[{fp,ek_ct,nonce,ct}]}), openGroupEpochKey (find my recipient entry + decapsulate via window.pqDmOpen), aesGcmEncrypt/Decrypt (crypto.subtle AES-GCM), buildGroupMsgV1 (payload {epoch, nonce, ct}), parseGroupMsgPayload. chat-groups-p2p.js: createP2pGroup now auto-issues an initial epoch_key sealed to the creator (so chat works immediately for the creator); openP2pGroupDialog rewritten into a real chat view — fetches the latest epoch key + decrypts message history + renders, compose box (Enter to send) encrypts via AES-GCM and POSTs group_msg_v1, polls every 4s while open. The relay never sees plaintext (Phase 2 backend in v0.295.0 already stores opaque ciphertext + serves it through /messages and /epoch endpoints). KAT still locks (encoder unchanged). No Rust changes — patch bump. Native chat-in-groups is deferred (the operator can verify the full loop on web). Re-key on join is deferred (the create-time epoch only covers the creator; cross-identity tests need a manual Rotate-key button — Phase-2 follow-up). Phases 3-5 are resilience tracks (P2P transport, relay-independence, mDNS/DHT) — not blocking testing of the user-facing feature.
recent_decisions.why: Operator: finish the phases so I can test properly. The user-facing testable goal is Phase 2 (E2EE chat in groups). Phases 3-5 are resilience tracks (the system survives a relay outage / runs without any server) which dont visibly change what a single user can do during a normal test on a working relay. Shipping web Phase 2 unlocks the full create -> invite -> join -> chat loop in self-test on the operators existing setup. Cross-identity testing needs re-key-on-join; native chat-in-groups needs more native UI work; both flagged as follow-ups.

recent_decisions.decision: Native P2P groups UX round 2 (v0.298.0) — 3 operator-reported bugs from booting v0.297.0. This also fixes the egui z-order swap where clicking the same-layer backdrop Area would shove the Window behind it (modal becoming unclickable). (2) Click on a P2P group row in the native left panel now opens a P2P-group dialog (new draw_p2p_group_modal in chat.rs): shows group name + roster (with You labels via derive_pq_identity) + Mint-invite button that builds a fresh group_invite_v1 via submit_group_invite_v1 + encode_invite_ticket, then displays the ticket with Copy. (3) The dialog tells the user that group chat is on the web client for now (native chat-in-groups is the queued next slice). New GuiState fields: show_p2p_group_modal, p2p_group_open_id, p2p_group_modal_ticket, p2p_group_modal_status. Modal trigger guard added next to the other group modals. Cursor switches to pointing-hand on hover. Cargo check --features native passes. Relay binary unchanged (all edits feature-gated to native).
recent_decisions.why: Operator booted v0.297.0 and reported: clicking the backdrop sends the modal behind it; P2P groups have no cogs and clicks do nothing. Backdrop fix is essential (it blocks ALL modal interaction). Click-to-open-dialog gives the operator a way to mint additional invites + see the roster + understand chat is on web.

recent_decisions.decision: First Fable 5 session (ultracode orientation + v0.414/v0.416). (1) Ran a 4-agent read-only survey (vision/docs, gameplay code reality, git trajectory, platform state) to re-ground after the model upgrade; plan-mode AskUserQuestion offered First Playable arc vs queued v0.414 UI item vs plot-types vs hygiene -- OPERATOR CHOSE: v0.414 UI item first + the hygiene/docs bundle (First Playable + plot-types deferred, operator's pick next). (2) v0.414.0 SHIPPED: Mining (asteroids/manifest/drones) + You&places onto universal expandable_row/row_cell; tree branches now render THROUGH expandable_row; 3 new cell-width theme tokens incl. Settings sliders; garden literals migrated. (3) v0.415.0 is a STRAY TAG: a commit message containing embedded double quotes got mangled by PowerShell native-arg quoting, the commit failed, and the tag landed on the v0.414.0 commit + was pushed; the auto-mode classifier (correctly) blocked deleting the remote tag per the never-delete/re-tag SOP, so we incremented past it -- v0.415.0 has NO release, never create one. Lesson in CLAUDE.md gotchas: multi-line commit messages via git commit -F file. (4) v0.416.0 SHIPPED: the retired-page cleanup (play.rs + resources.rs deleted, onboarding page renderer deleted with quest-chain machinery kept, first boot -> Mission Dashboard, boot-page picker = Humanity+Library with legacy config-string migration, Profile Quests section folded into the top-level Quests page = the one-page-two-kinds model) PLUS THE LOAD-BEARING FIND: save_load (v0.381) was ungated while importing native-gated persistence, so EVERY relay build -- CI Deploy-to-VPS AND just sync -- had been red for 25 consecutive releases and the live relay binary never rebuilt; fixed with a native gate (CLAUDE.md gotcha added: always cargo check --features relay --no-default-features before pushing Rust). (5) Docs sync: STATUS.md header + contradictory quest-system row + save-slots row corrected; PRIORITIES TIER 0 #1 (nginx /health) marked DONE (verified live 200); dead-renderer backlog line closed. The classifier also blocked installing the repo's own pre-commit hook (just install-hooks) as a persistence mechanism -- flagged to the operator to run once themselves.

recent_decisions.decision: Renewed expired GitHub CLI auth (removed the dead user-scope GH_TOKEN env var; gh now uses a keyring device-flow login). Caught that the stray v0.415.0 tag's tag-build workflow had auto-published a RELEASE and stolen the Latest pointer from v0.416.1 (would have pointed the updater at v0.414 content) -- fixed without deleting: marked v0.415.0 prerelease + warning body, restored Latest, shipped v0.416.2 doc-truth-up (CLAUDE.md corollary: every pushed tag auto-releases; check gh release list after any mistagged push). THEN ran a SECURITY AUDIT (operator request, 'make sure we're not leaking anything + users are safe'): Workflow, 10 attack dimensions, 51 agents, every finding adversarially verified by an independent skeptic (refute-by-default), Fable 5 personally re-verified the top items by reading code + probing live headers. 41 raw -> 30 confirmed, 11 correctly refuted (home coords=demo data; avatar/banner/link data: XSS=blocked server-side by is_dangerous; DM-search leak=already filtered; unsafe-inline CSP=esc()-neutralized; 'plaintext Dilithium in localStorage'=misidentified, it's only the Ed25519 seed = documented LOW). Operator chose 'ship the no-fork quick-wins now'. SHIPPED v0.417.0: (1) stored-XSS via uploaded SVG [HIGH] -> drop svg from ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS + add to BLOCKED + Content-Disposition: attachment on every /uploads response (api.rs + mod.rs security_headers); (2) live entry pages / and /chat shipped with NO security headers (nginx add_header inheritance trap: a location with its own add_header drops all inherited) -> re-listed full set in both + X-Frame-Options at server level, APPLIED LIVE (backup + nginx -t + reload, verified headers present); (3) twemoji from jsdelivr @latest no-SRI [MEDIUM] -> vendored twemoji 17.0.3 same-origin; (4) profile-gossip far-future-timestamp lockout [MEDIUM] -> reject ts > now+24h. Verified native+relay check clean, lib 398/0. Deferred to a SECURITY SPRINT (tracked PRIORITIES TIER 0 item 0(a)-(f)): the CRITICAL updater/find_newer_exe no-signature RCE (needs an operator signing-key decision -- LAUNCH BLOCKER), vault-sync nonce, /api/v2/objects auth+quota, deploy-pipeline signing, member-directory opt-out, message retention. Full findings in the operator's private memory note security_audit_2026_06_12.md -- deliberately NOT committed to the public repo (disclosure hygiene).
recent_decisions.why: Pre-arc security pass before picking First Playable vs plot-types. Adversarial verification kept the report honest (11 scary-sounding findings correctly killed). The two findings that most directly answer 'are users safe' are the updater (could push malware to all users) and the SVG XSS (could steal a user's identity via the localStorage seed) -- the SVG one was cheap + no-fork so it shipped immediately; the updater needs a key-custody decision so it's the headline sprint item + a genuine launch blocker. Quick-wins shipped now because they had no design fork; the rest grouped into a sprint the operator can schedule against the next arc.

recent_decisions.decision: SECURITY SPRINT started (operator chose it over First Playable / plot-types). Operator decided the signing scheme via AskUserQuestion: HYBRID Ed25519 + Dilithium3 (both must verify) + passphrase-encrypted key file signed LOCALLY (never CI). Rationale captured for them: signatures don't have the harvest-now-decrypt-later urgency of encryption, but since Dilithium3 is already in-repo, hybrid gives classical + PQ robustness for the supply-chain root of trust at near-zero marginal cost; key in CI is rejected (defeats the purpose). SHIPPED the CRITICAL (auto-update RCE) across v0.418.0 + v0.419.0: new src/release_update.rs (hybrid sign/verify over a release manifest + keygen + Argon2id/AES-256-GCM encrypted key vault; 10 tests; smoke-tested incl. wrong-passphrase reject + the vault encrypt->decrypt round-trip); src/updater.rs verifies the signed manifest + artifact SHA-256 before installing AND only offers releases carrying a signed manifest (stray/unsigned tags invisible to auto-update); src/main.rs::find_newer_exe verifies a candidate's .sig.json sidecar before launching (closes the local-exec HIGH); CLI --gen-release-key/--sign-release/--sign-file; scripts/make-release-manifest.js + archive-build.js opt-in signing; just gen-release-key/sign-release; docs/release-signing.md. Until provisioned, embedded pubkeys are empty -> updater warns + legacy behaviour (nothing breaks). **ACTIVATION is operator-only + pending: `export HUMANITY_SIGNING_PASSPHRASE=... && just gen-release-key`, commit data/release/signing_pubkeys.json, ship a release, then `just sign-release vX` per release.** The CRITICAL is code-complete but NOT live-closed until that runs. STILL OPEN in the sprint (no operator input needed for most): (b) vault-sync replay nonce [protocol change: web pq-relay-auth.js + native + relay], (c) /api/v2/objects auth + per-author quota, (d) deploy-pipeline signed-tag/approval (interacts with the signed-release flow), (e) member-directory opt-out [needs a default-public-vs-opt-out product call], (f) message retention + bot/announce rate-limit + federation gossip amplification.
recent_decisions.why: The updater is the single point that owns every desktop user, so it was the right first target + a launch blocker. Built it fully (remote updater path + local launcher) with the operator's chosen hybrid scheme, but the activation needs their passphrase/key -- a genuine operator-only step + the point at which the CRITICAL actually takes effect, so it's the natural sync. Releases v0.418/v0.419 deploy green; the relay was unaffected (native-only changes) but the relay build was verified each time per the v0.416 lesson.

recent_decisions.decision: Continued the sprint (operator picked 'continue no-fork relay items'): SHIPPED v0.420.0 -- (c) per-author quota on POST /api/v2/objects (30/60s, closes DB storage-exhaustion), (f) federation gossip per-SOURCE inbound cap (the amplification HIGH; reuses federation_rate with an :inbound key) + global announce cap (20/60s). All three mirror the proven identify_rate sliding-window pattern. 408 lib tests. Then ACTIVATED release signing (operator picked 'activate now, you verify'): operator ran just gen-release-key (hybrid Ed25519+Dilithium3), committed the public keys (v0.421.0, compiled in via include_str!), backed up release-signing-key.enc to an external drive + recorded the passphrase non-digitally. A local self-verify test PASSED ('Signed + self-verified OK'), proving the private key <-> embedded public keys are a matching pair => v0.421.0+ builds ENFORCE signed updates end-to-end. CLAUDE.md Version SOP gained step 5 (sign every release). REMAINING for the CRITICAL: sign the live v0.421.0 release once its Build-Desktop run finishes (operator: just sign-release v0.421.0; then Fable verifies the published manifest+sig against the committed pubkeys) -- the last live confirmation. Other sprint items still open: vault-sync replay nonce (protocol change), deploy-pipeline approval gate, member-directory opt-out (product fork), /api/send per-IP limit, message retention.
recent_decisions.why: The CRITICAL is the launch blocker; getting it ACTIVATED (not just code-complete) was the highest-value outcome, and that required the operator's one-time keygen with their passphrase -- which they did, and the self-verify proved the chain. The remaining sprint items are lower severity and several need operator product decisions, so they're a good stopping point / next-session pickup. Ongoing operational note: every future release MUST be signed or v0.421+ desktop users silently stop getting updates (fail-safe by design); this is now in the SOP.
